          Ix'Nonneg = dist‚Ąö-nonneg x'n 0‚Ąö

          -- ¬ß Phase 6: Product decomposition via midpoint x'¬∑y
          dxx'‚Č§őīx : dxx' ‚Č§‚Ąö őīx
          dxx'‚Č§őīx = <‚Ąö‚Üí‚Č§‚Ąö {dxx'} {őīx} dxx'<őīx

          dyy'‚Č§őīy : dyy' ‚Č§‚Ąö őīy
          dyy'‚Č§őīy = <‚Ąö‚Üí‚Č§‚Ąö {dyy'} {őīy} dyy'<őīy

          Iy‚Č§KY : Iy ‚Č§‚Ąö KY
          Iy‚Č§KY = ‚Č§‚Ąö-trans {Iy} {By} {KY} Iy‚Č§By By‚Č§KY

          Ix'‚Č§KX' : Ix' ‚Č§‚Ąö KX'
          Ix'‚Č§KX' = ‚Č§‚Ąö-trans {Ix'} {Bx'} {KX'} Ix'‚Č§Bx' Bx'‚Č§KX'

          d1 : ‚Ąö
          d1 = dist‚Ąö (xn *‚Ąö yn) (x'n *‚Ąö yn)

          d2 : ‚Ąö
          d2 = dist‚Ąö (x'n *‚Ąö yn) (x'n *‚Ąö y'n)

          d1Nonneg : 0‚Ąö ‚Č§‚Ąö d1
          d1Nonneg = dist‚Ąö-nonneg (xn *‚Ąö yn) (x'n *‚Ąö yn)

          d2Nonneg : 0‚Ąö ‚Č§‚Ąö d2
          d2Nonneg = dist‚Ąö-nonneg (x'n *‚Ąö yn) (x'n *‚Ąö y'n)

          d1‚Č§scaled : d1 ‚Č§‚Ąö (dxx' *‚Ąö Iy)
          d1‚Č§scaled = ‚ČÉ‚Ąö‚Üí‚Č§‚Ąöň° {d1} {(dxx' *‚Ąö Iy)} (dist‚Ąö-*‚Ąö-right yn xn x'n)

          d2‚Č§scaled : d2 ‚Č§‚Ąö (dyy' *‚Ąö Ix')
          d2‚Č§scaled = ‚ČÉ‚Ąö‚Üí‚Č§‚Ąöň° {d2} {(dyy' *‚Ąö Ix')} (dist‚Ąö-*‚Ąö-left x'n yn y'n)

          step1 : (dxx' *‚Ąö Iy) ‚Č§‚Ąö (őīx *‚Ąö Iy)
          step1 = ‚Č§‚Ąö-mul-nonneg-right dxx' őīx Iy dxx'‚Č§őīx IyNonneg

          step2 : (őīx *‚Ąö Iy) ‚Č§‚Ąö (őīx *‚Ąö KY)
          step2 = ‚Č§‚Ąö-mul-nonneg-left Iy KY őīx Iy‚Č§KY őīxNonneg

          scaled1‚Č§ : (dxx' *‚Ąö Iy) ‚Č§‚Ąö (őīx *‚Ąö KY)
          scaled1‚Č§ = ‚Č§‚Ąö-trans {(dxx' *‚Ąö Iy)} {(őīx *‚Ąö Iy)} {(őīx *‚Ąö KY)} step1 step2

          scaled1<őĶq : (dxx' *‚Ąö Iy) <‚Ąö őĶq
          scaled1<őĶq = ‚Č§<‚Ąö‚Üí<‚Ąö {(dxx' *‚Ąö Iy)} {(őīx *‚Ąö KY)} {őĶq} scaled1‚Č§ őīxKY<őĶq

          d1<őĶq : d1 <‚Ąö őĶq
          d1<őĶq = ‚Č§<‚Ąö‚Üí<‚Ąö {d1} {(őīx *‚Ąö KY)} {őĶq} (‚Č§‚Ąö-trans {d1} {(dxx' *‚Ąö Iy)} {(őīx *‚Ąö KY)} d1‚Č§scaled (‚Č§‚Ąö-trans {(dxx' *‚Ąö Iy)} {(őīx *‚Ąö Iy)} {(őīx *‚Ąö KY)} step1 step2)) őīxKY<őĶq

          step1' : (dyy' *‚Ąö Ix') ‚Č§‚Ąö (őīy *‚Ąö Ix')
          step1' = ‚Č§‚Ąö-mul-nonneg-right dyy' őīy Ix' dyy'‚Č§őīy Ix'Nonneg

          step2' : (őīy *‚Ąö Ix') ‚Č§‚Ąö (őīy *‚Ąö KX')
          step2' = ‚Č§‚Ąö-mul-nonneg-left Ix' KX' őīy Ix'‚Č§KX' őīyNonneg

          scaled2‚Č§ : (dyy' *‚Ąö Ix') ‚Č§‚Ąö (őīy *‚Ąö KX')
          scaled2‚Č§ = ‚Č§‚Ąö-trans {(dyy' *‚Ąö Ix')} {(őīy *‚Ąö Ix')} {(őīy *‚Ąö KX')} step1' step2'

          scaled2<őĶq : (dyy' *‚Ąö Ix') <‚Ąö őĶq
          scaled2<őĶq = ‚Č§<‚Ąö‚Üí<‚Ąö {(dyy' *‚Ąö Ix')} {(őīy *‚Ąö KX')} {őĶq} scaled2‚Č§ őīyKX'<őĶq

          d2<őĶq : d2 <‚Ąö őĶq
          d2<őĶq = ‚Č§<‚Ąö‚Üí<‚Ąö {d2} {(dyy' *‚Ąö Ix')} {őĶq} d2‚Č§scaled scaled2<őĶq

          d1‚Č§őĶq : d1 ‚Č§‚Ąö őĶq
          d1‚Č§őĶq = <‚Ąö‚Üí‚Č§‚Ąö {d1} {őĶq} d1<őĶq

          d2‚Č§őĶq : d2 ‚Č§‚Ąö őĶq
          d2‚Č§őĶq = <‚Ąö‚Üí‚Č§‚Ąö {d2} {őĶq} d2<őĶq

          sum‚Č§ : (d1 +‚Ąö d2) ‚Č§‚Ąö (őĶq +‚Ąö őĶq)
          sum‚Č§ = ‚Č§‚Ąö-sum‚Č§double-nonneg d1 d2 őĶq d1Nonneg d2Nonneg őĶqNonneg d1‚Č§őĶq d2‚Č§őĶq

          sum<őĶ : (d1 +‚Ąö d2) <‚Ąö őĶ
          sum<őĶ =
            ‚Č§<‚Ąö‚Üí<‚Ąö {(d1 +‚Ąö d2)} {(őĶq +‚Ąö őĶq)} {őĶ}
              sum‚Č§ őĶq+őĶq<őĶ

          tri : dist‚Ąö (xn *‚Ąö yn) (x'n *‚Ąö y'n) ‚Č§‚Ąö (d1 +‚Ąö d2)
          tri = dist‚Ąö-triangle (xn *‚Ąö yn) (x'n *‚Ąö yn) (x'n *‚Ąö y'n)
        in
        ‚Č§<‚Ąö‚Üí<‚Ąö {dist‚Ąö (xn *‚Ąö yn) (x'n *‚Ąö y'n)} {(d1 +‚Ąö d2)} {őĶ}
          tri sum<őĶ)
  }
\end{code}

\paragraph{Step 2: Additive Congruence.}
Addition is simpler: no eventual bounds are needed, only
$\varepsilon/4$-convergence in both sequences. The triangle
inequality then closes the argument directly.

\begin{code}
-- ¬ß Addition respects ‚ČÉ‚ĄĚ
+‚ĄĚ-resp-‚ČÉ‚ĄĚ :
  {x x' y y' : ‚ĄĚ} ‚Üí
  x ‚ČÉ‚ĄĚ x' ‚Üí
  y ‚ČÉ‚ĄĚ y' ‚Üí
  (x +‚ĄĚ y) ‚ČÉ‚ĄĚ (x' +‚ĄĚ y')
+‚ĄĚ-resp-‚ČÉ‚ĄĚ {x} {x'} {y} {y'} x‚ČÉx' y‚ČÉy' = record
  { conv0 = őĽ őĶ őĶpos ‚Üí
      let
        -- ¬ß Phase 1: Budget allocation
        őĶq : ‚Ąö
        őĶq = őĶQuarter őĶ

        őĶqPos : 0‚Ąö <‚Ąö őĶq
        őĶqPos = őĶQuarter-pos őĶ

        őĶqNonneg : 0‚Ąö ‚Č§‚Ąö őĶq
        őĶqNonneg = <‚Ąö‚Üí‚Č§‚Ąö {0‚Ąö} {őĶq} őĶqPos

        őĶq+őĶq<őĶ : (őĶq +‚Ąö őĶq) <‚Ąö őĶ
        őĶq+őĶq<őĶ = őĶQuarter-double<őĶ őĶ őĶpos

        NxPack : ő£ ‚Ąē (őĽ N ‚Üí (n : ‚Ąē) ‚Üí N ‚Č§ n ‚Üí dist‚Ąö (seq x n) (seq x' n) <‚Ąö őĶq)
        NxPack = _‚ČÉ‚ĄĚ_.conv0 x‚ČÉx' őĶq őĶqPos

        NyPack : ő£ ‚Ąē (őĽ N ‚Üí (n : ‚Ąē) ‚Üí N ‚Č§ n ‚Üí dist‚Ąö (seq y n) (seq y' n) <‚Ąö őĶq)
        NyPack = _‚ČÉ‚ĄĚ_.conv0 y‚ČÉy' őĶq őĶqPos

        Nx : ‚Ąē
        Nx = fst NxPack

        Ny : ‚Ąē
        Ny = fst NyPack

        NxConv : (n : ‚Ąē) ‚Üí Nx ‚Č§ n ‚Üí dist‚Ąö (seq x n) (seq x' n) <‚Ąö őĶq
        NxConv = snd NxPack

        NyConv : (n : ‚Ąē) ‚Üí Ny ‚Č§ n ‚Üí dist‚Ąö (seq y n) (seq y' n) <‚Ąö őĶq
        NyConv = snd NyPack

        -- ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ Phase 2: Global modulus N ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ
        N : ‚Ąē
        N = Nx +‚Ąē Ny

        Nx‚Č§N : Nx ‚Č§ N
        Nx‚Č§N =
          let
            mono : (Nx +‚Ąē zero) ‚Č§ (Nx +‚Ąē Ny)
            mono = ‚Č§-+‚Ąē-monoň° {a = zero} {b = Ny} z‚Č§n Nx
          in
          subst (őĽ t ‚Üí t ‚Č§ (Nx +‚Ąē Ny)) (+‚Ąē-zero-right Nx) mono

        Ny‚Č§N : Ny ‚Č§ N
        Ny‚Č§N =
          let
            mono : (Ny +‚Ąē zero) ‚Č§ (Ny +‚Ąē Nx)
            mono = ‚Č§-+‚Ąē-monoň° {a = zero} {b = Nx} z‚Č§n Ny

            base : Ny ‚Č§ (Ny +‚Ąē Nx)
            base = subst (őĽ t ‚Üí t ‚Č§ (Ny +‚Ąē Nx)) (+‚Ąē-zero-right Ny) mono
          in
          subst (őĽ t ‚Üí Ny ‚Č§ t) (+‚Ąē-comm Ny Nx) base
      in
      N , (őĽ n N‚Č§n ‚Üí
        let
          -- ¬ß Phase 3: Triangle inequality on sums
          Nx‚Č§n : Nx ‚Č§ n
          Nx‚Č§n = ‚Č§-trans Nx‚Č§N N‚Č§n

          Ny‚Č§n : Ny ‚Č§ n
          Ny‚Č§n = ‚Č§-trans Ny‚Č§N N‚Č§n

          xn : ‚Ąö
          xn = seq x n

          x'n : ‚Ąö
          x'n = seq x' n

          yn : ‚Ąö
          yn = seq y n

          y'n : ‚Ąö
          y'n = seq y' n

          dx : ‚Ąö
          dx = dist‚Ąö xn x'n

          dy : ‚Ąö
          dy = dist‚Ąö yn y'n

          dx<őĶq : dx <‚Ąö őĶq
          dx<őĶq = NxConv n Nx‚Č§n

          dy<őĶq : dy <‚Ąö őĶq
          dy<őĶq = NyConv n Ny‚Č§n

          d1 : ‚Ąö
          d1 = dist‚Ąö (xn +‚Ąö yn) (x'n +‚Ąö yn)

          d2 : ‚Ąö
          d2 = dist‚Ąö (x'n +‚Ąö yn) (x'n +‚Ąö y'n)

          d1Nonneg : 0‚Ąö ‚Č§‚Ąö d1
          d1Nonneg = dist‚Ąö-nonneg (xn +‚Ąö yn) (x'n +‚Ąö yn)

          d2Nonneg : 0‚Ąö ‚Č§‚Ąö d2
          d2Nonneg = dist‚Ąö-nonneg (x'n +‚Ąö yn) (x'n +‚Ąö y'n)

          d1‚Č§dx : d1 ‚Č§‚Ąö dx
          d1‚Č§dx = ‚ČÉ‚Ąö‚Üí‚Č§‚Ąöň° {d1} {dx} (dist‚Ąö-+‚Ąö-right xn x'n yn)

          d2‚Č§dy : d2 ‚Č§‚Ąö dy
          d2‚Č§dy = ‚ČÉ‚Ąö‚Üí‚Č§‚Ąöň° {d2} {dy} (dist‚Ąö-+‚Ąö-left x'n yn y'n)

          d1<őĶq : d1 <‚Ąö őĶq
          d1<őĶq = ‚Č§<‚Ąö‚Üí<‚Ąö {d1} {dx} {őĶq} d1‚Č§dx dx<őĶq

          d2<őĶq : d2 <‚Ąö őĶq
          d2<őĶq = ‚Č§<‚Ąö‚Üí<‚Ąö {d2} {dy} {őĶq} d2‚Č§dy dy<őĶq

          d1‚Č§őĶq : d1 ‚Č§‚Ąö őĶq
          d1‚Č§őĶq = <‚Ąö‚Üí‚Č§‚Ąö {d1} {őĶq} d1<őĶq

          d2‚Č§őĶq : d2 ‚Č§‚Ąö őĶq
          d2‚Č§őĶq = <‚Ąö‚Üí‚Č§‚Ąö {d2} {őĶq} d2<őĶq

          sum‚Č§ : (d1 +‚Ąö d2) ‚Č§‚Ąö (őĶq +‚Ąö őĶq)
          sum‚Č§ = ‚Č§‚Ąö-sum‚Č§double-nonneg d1 d2 őĶq d1Nonneg d2Nonneg őĶqNonneg d1‚Č§őĶq d2‚Č§őĶq

          sum<őĶ : (d1 +‚Ąö d2) <‚Ąö őĶ
          sum<őĶ = ‚Č§<‚Ąö‚Üí<‚Ąö {(d1 +‚Ąö d2)} {(őĶq +‚Ąö őĶq)} {őĶ} sum‚Č§ őĶq+őĶq<őĶ

          tri : dist‚Ąö (xn +‚Ąö yn) (x'n +‚Ąö y'n) ‚Č§‚Ąö (d1 +‚Ąö d2)
          tri = dist‚Ąö-triangle (xn +‚Ąö yn) (x'n +‚Ąö yn) (x'n +‚Ąö y'n)
        in
        ‚Č§<‚Ąö‚Üí<‚Ąö {dist‚Ąö (xn +‚Ąö yn) (x'n +‚Ąö y'n)} {(d1 +‚Ąö d2)} {őĶ} tri sum<őĶ)
  }
\end{code}

\paragraph{Step 3: Negation and Subtraction Congruence.}
Negation preserves distance by \texttt{dist‚Ąö-neg}, and subtraction
combines additive and negation congruence.

\begin{code}
-- ¬ß Negation respects ‚ČÉ‚ĄĚ
-‚ĄĚ-resp-‚ČÉ‚ĄĚ : {x x' : ‚ĄĚ} ‚Üí x ‚ČÉ‚ĄĚ x' ‚Üí (-‚ĄĚ x) ‚ČÉ‚ĄĚ (-‚ĄĚ x')
-‚ĄĚ-resp-‚ČÉ‚ĄĚ {x} {x'} x‚ČÉx' = record
  { conv0 = őĽ őĶ őĶpos ‚Üí
      let
        NxPack : ő£ ‚Ąē (őĽ N ‚Üí (n : ‚Ąē) ‚Üí N ‚Č§ n ‚Üí dist‚Ąö (seq x n) (seq x' n) <‚Ąö őĶ)
        NxPack = _‚ČÉ‚ĄĚ_.conv0 x‚ČÉx' őĶ őĶpos

        Nx : ‚Ąē
        Nx = fst NxPack

        NxConv : (n : ‚Ąē) ‚Üí Nx ‚Č§ n ‚Üí dist‚Ąö (seq x n) (seq x' n) <‚Ąö őĶ
        NxConv = snd NxPack
      in
      Nx , (őĽ n Nx‚Č§n ‚Üí
        let
          xn : ‚Ąö
          xn = seq x n

          x'n : ‚Ąö
          x'n = seq x' n

          d<őĶ : dist‚Ąö xn x'n <‚Ąö őĶ
          d<őĶ = NxConv n Nx‚Č§n

          negEq : dist‚Ąö (-‚Ąö xn) (-‚Ąö x'n) ‚ČÉ‚Ąö dist‚Ąö xn x'n
          negEq = dist‚Ąö-neg xn x'n

          d‚Č§ : dist‚Ąö (-‚Ąö xn) (-‚Ąö x'n) ‚Č§‚Ąö dist‚Ąö xn x'n
          d‚Č§ = ‚ČÉ‚Ąö‚Üí‚Č§‚Ąöň° {dist‚Ąö (-‚Ąö xn) (-‚Ąö x'n)} {dist‚Ąö xn x'n} negEq
        in
        ‚Č§<‚Ąö‚Üí<‚Ąö {dist‚Ąö (-‚Ąö xn) (-‚Ąö x'n)} {dist‚Ąö xn x'n} {őĶ} d‚Č§ d<őĶ)
  }

-- ¬ß Subtraction respects ‚ČÉ‚ĄĚ (derived from + and -)
-‚ĄĚ-resp-‚ČÉ‚ĄĚ‚āā : {x x' y y' : ‚ĄĚ} ‚Üí x ‚ČÉ‚ĄĚ x' ‚Üí y ‚ČÉ‚ĄĚ y' ‚Üí (x -‚ĄĚ y) ‚ČÉ‚ĄĚ (x' -‚ĄĚ y')
-‚ĄĚ-resp-‚ČÉ‚ĄĚ‚āā {x} {x'} {y} {y'} x‚ČÉx' y‚ČÉy' =
  +‚ĄĚ-resp-‚ČÉ‚ĄĚ x‚ČÉx' (-‚ĄĚ-resp-‚ČÉ‚ĄĚ y‚ČÉy')
\end{code}


%‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ
\section{Asymptotic Order on \texorpdfstring{$\mathbb{R}$}{R}}
\label{sec:reals:asymptotic-order}
%‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ

A field without order cannot host inequalities, and inequalities are the language of physics: every bound, every spectral gap, every convergence statement is an order statement.  But reals are equivalence classes of Cauchy sequences, and finite-prefix comparisons do not survive the passage to the quotient.  The only order that does survive is asymptotic: a comparison that holds on every tail, for every positive margin.

Order on $\mathbb{R}$ is forced to be asymptotic: finite prefixes cannot survive quotient formation, so only eventual rational comparison with arbitrary positive margins remains admissible. The unique strict and non-strict order notions compatible with representative elimination under $\simeq_{\mathbb{R}}$ are fixed below.

\textbf{Constraint:} Order must be compatible with the Cauchy quotient: if $x \simeq_{\mathbb{R}} x'$ and $x \leq_{\mathbb{R}} y$, then $x' \leq_{\mathbb{R}} y$.  Any order depending on a finite prefix is shattered by re-choosing the representative.

\textbf{Construction:} Define $x \leq_{\mathbb{R}} y$ iff for every $\varepsilon > 0$ there exists $N$ such that $x_n \leq y_n + \varepsilon$ for all $n \geq N$.  Define $x <_{\mathbb{R}} y$ iff there exists $\varepsilon > 0$ with the same tail comparison.

\textbf{Consequence:} Twelve order laws are forced---reflexivity, transitivity, antisymmetry, congruence under $\simeq_{\mathbb{R}}$, and additive monotonicity---closing the ordered-field scaffold required by the spectral constants.

\subsection*{Law 23.1: Non-strict asymptotic order}

Non-strict order is forced by eventual $\varepsilon$-comparison for every positive rational margin. Any weaker condition leaves room for prefix dependence, and that dependence is already excluded by the quotient from Cauchy representatives to reals.

\begin{code}
-- ¬ß Non-strict real order: eventual őĶ-approximation
infix 4 _‚Č§‚ĄĚ_ _<‚ĄĚ_

record _‚Č§‚ĄĚ_ (x y : ‚ĄĚ) : Set where
  field
    leReal : (őĶ : ‚Ąö) ‚Üí (0‚Ąö <‚Ąö őĶ) ‚Üí ő£ ‚Ąē (őĽ N ‚Üí (n : ‚Ąē) ‚Üí N ‚Č§ n ‚Üí (seq x n) ‚Č§‚Ąö ((seq y n) +‚Ąö őĶ))

-- ¬ß Type alias for non-strict real order
‚Č§‚ĄĚP : ‚ĄĚ ‚Üí ‚ĄĚ ‚Üí Set
‚Č§‚ĄĚP = _‚Č§‚ĄĚ_
\end{code}

\subsection*{Law 23.2: Strict asymptotic order}

Strict order is forced by a positive rational witness that survives on a tail. This eliminates vanishing separation and fixes strictness as stable asymptotic margin rather than finite-stage fluctuation.

\begin{code}
-- ¬ß Strict real order: witnessed separation
record _<‚ĄĚ_ (x y : ‚ĄĚ) : Set where
  field
    ltWitness : ő£ ‚Ąö (őĽ őĶ ‚Üí (0‚Ąö <‚Ąö őĶ) √ó ő£ ‚Ąē (őĽ N ‚Üí (n : ‚Ąē) ‚Üí N ‚Č§ n ‚Üí ((seq x n) +‚Ąö őĶ) ‚Č§‚Ąö (seq y n)))
\end{code}

\subsection*{Law 23.3: Strict order forces non-strict order}

Every strict witness forces non-strict order once the positive gap is forgotten after it has done its work. The margin survives only as an intermediate constraint and is then eliminated from the final asymptotic comparison.

\begin{code}
-- ¬ß Strict order implies non-strict by forgetting the margin
<‚ĄĚ‚Üí‚Č§‚ĄĚ : {x y : ‚ĄĚ} ‚Üí x <‚ĄĚ y ‚Üí x ‚Č§‚ĄĚ y
<‚ĄĚ‚Üí‚Č§‚ĄĚ {x} {y} x<y = record
  { leReal = őĽ őī őīpos ‚Üí
      let
        w : ő£ ‚Ąö (őĽ őĶ ‚Üí (0‚Ąö <‚Ąö őĶ) √ó ő£ ‚Ąē (őĽ N ‚Üí (n : ‚Ąē) ‚Üí N ‚Č§ n ‚Üí ((seq x n) +‚Ąö őĶ) ‚Č§‚Ąö (seq y n)))
        w = _<‚ĄĚ_.ltWitness x<y

        őĶ : ‚Ąö
        őĶ = fst w

        wRest : (0‚Ąö <‚Ąö őĶ) √ó ő£ ‚Ąē (őĽ N ‚Üí (n : ‚Ąē) ‚Üí N ‚Č§ n ‚Üí ((seq x n) +‚Ąö őĶ) ‚Č§‚Ąö (seq y n))
        wRest = snd w

        őĶpos : 0‚Ąö <‚Ąö őĶ
        őĶpos = fst wRest

        pack : ő£ ‚Ąē (őĽ N ‚Üí (n : ‚Ąē) ‚Üí N ‚Č§ n ‚Üí ((seq x n) +‚Ąö őĶ) ‚Č§‚Ąö (seq y n))
        pack = snd wRest

        N : ‚Ąē
        N = fst pack

        conv : (n : ‚Ąē) ‚Üí N ‚Č§ n ‚Üí ((seq x n) +‚Ąö őĶ) ‚Č§‚Ąö (seq y n)
        conv = snd pack
      in
      N , (őĽ n N‚Č§n ‚Üí
        let
          xn : ‚Ąö
          xn = seq x n

          yn : ‚Ąö
          yn = seq y n

          xn‚Č§xn+őĶ : xn ‚Č§‚Ąö (xn +‚Ąö őĶ)
          xn‚Č§xn+őĶ = ‚Č§‚Ąö-add-nonneg-right xn őĶ (<‚Ąö‚Üí‚Č§‚Ąö őĶpos)

          xn+őĶ‚Č§yn : (xn +‚Ąö őĶ) ‚Č§‚Ąö yn
          xn+őĶ‚Č§yn = conv n N‚Č§n

          xn‚Č§yn : xn ‚Č§‚Ąö yn
          xn‚Č§yn = ‚Č§‚Ąö-trans {xn} {(xn +‚Ąö őĶ)} {yn} xn‚Č§xn+őĶ xn+őĶ‚Č§yn

          yn‚Č§yn+őī : yn ‚Č§‚Ąö (yn +‚Ąö őī)
          yn‚Č§yn+őī = ‚Č§‚Ąö-add-nonneg-right yn őī (<‚Ąö‚Üí‚Č§‚Ąö őīpos)
        in
        ‚Č§‚Ąö-trans xn‚Č§yn yn‚Č§yn+őī)
  }
\end{code}

\subsection*{Law 23.4: Equivalence forces non-strict order}

distance bounds eliminate any persistent asymptotic gap. The resulting
Representative equivalence forces non-strict order because eventual distance bounds eliminate any persistent asymptotic gap. The resulting order law therefore remains invariant under replacement by an equivalent Cauchy representative.

\begin{code}
-- ¬ß Equivalence implies ‚Č§‚ĄĚ
‚ČÉ‚ĄĚ‚Üí‚Č§‚ĄĚ : {x y : ‚ĄĚ} ‚Üí x ‚ČÉ‚ĄĚ y ‚Üí x ‚Č§‚ĄĚ y
‚ČÉ‚ĄĚ‚Üí‚Č§‚ĄĚ {x} {y} x‚ČÉy = record
  { leReal = őĽ őĶ őĶpos ‚Üí
      let
        pack : ő£ ‚Ąē (őĽ N ‚Üí (n : ‚Ąē) ‚Üí N ‚Č§ n ‚Üí dist‚Ąö (seq x n) (seq y n) <‚Ąö őĶ)
        pack = _‚ČÉ‚ĄĚ_.conv0 x‚ČÉy őĶ őĶpos

        N : ‚Ąē
        N = fst pack

        conv : (n : ‚Ąē) ‚Üí N ‚Č§ n ‚Üí dist‚Ąö (seq x n) (seq y n) <‚Ąö őĶ
        conv = snd pack
      in
      N , (őĽ n N‚Č§n ‚Üí
        dist‚Ąö‚Č§őĶ‚Üíx‚Č§y+őĶ (seq x n) (seq y n) őĶ (<‚Ąö‚Üí‚Č§‚Ąö (conv n N‚Č§n)))
  }
\end{code}


%‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ
\section{Transitivity of Strict Order}
\label{sec:reals:strict-order-transitivity}
%‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ

Strict asymptotic separation must compose whenever two positive margins occur in sequence. The remaining freedom that an intermediate witness could block composition is eliminated, forcing strict order to survive transitive chaining.

\subsection*{Law 23.5: Strict order is transitive}

Witness margins compose after controlled shrinking. Quartering eliminates overflow in the combined error budget and forces one surviving positive margin for the composite comparison.

\begin{code}
-- ¬ß Transitivity of strict real order
<‚ĄĚ-trans : {x y z : ‚ĄĚ} ‚Üí x <‚ĄĚ y ‚Üí y <‚ĄĚ z ‚Üí x <‚ĄĚ z
<‚ĄĚ-trans {x} {y} {z} x<y y<z = record
  { ltWitness =
      let
        -- ¬ß Phase 1: Unpack both strict-order witnesses
        wxy : ő£ ‚Ąö (őĽ őĶ ‚Üí (0‚Ąö <‚Ąö őĶ) √ó ő£ ‚Ąē (őĽ N ‚Üí (n : ‚Ąē) ‚Üí N ‚Č§ n ‚Üí ((seq x n) +‚Ąö őĶ) ‚Č§‚Ąö (seq y n)))
        wxy = _<‚ĄĚ_.ltWitness x<y

        őĶ‚āĀ : ‚Ąö
        őĶ‚āĀ = fst wxy

        wxyRest : (0‚Ąö <‚Ąö őĶ‚āĀ) √ó ő£ ‚Ąē (őĽ N ‚Üí (n : ‚Ąē) ‚Üí N ‚Č§ n ‚Üí ((seq x n) +‚Ąö őĶ‚āĀ) ‚Č§‚Ąö (seq y n))
        wxyRest = snd wxy

        őĶ‚āĀpos : 0‚Ąö <‚Ąö őĶ‚āĀ
        őĶ‚āĀpos = fst wxyRest

        packXY : ő£ ‚Ąē (őĽ N ‚Üí (n : ‚Ąē) ‚Üí N ‚Č§ n ‚Üí ((seq x n) +‚Ąö őĶ‚āĀ) ‚Č§‚Ąö (seq y n))
        packXY = snd wxyRest

        Nxy : ‚Ąē
        Nxy = fst packXY

        convXY : (n : ‚Ąē) ‚Üí Nxy ‚Č§ n ‚Üí ((seq x n) +‚Ąö őĶ‚āĀ) ‚Č§‚Ąö (seq y n)
        convXY = snd packXY

        wyz : ő£ ‚Ąö (őĽ őĶ ‚Üí (0‚Ąö <‚Ąö őĶ) √ó ő£ ‚Ąē (őĽ N ‚Üí (n : ‚Ąē) ‚Üí N ‚Č§ n ‚Üí ((seq y n) +‚Ąö őĶ) ‚Č§‚Ąö (seq z n)))
        wyz = _<‚ĄĚ_.ltWitness y<z

        őĶ‚āā : ‚Ąö
        őĶ‚āā = fst wyz

        wyzRest : (0‚Ąö <‚Ąö őĶ‚āā) √ó ő£ ‚Ąē (őĽ N ‚Üí (n : ‚Ąē) ‚Üí N ‚Č§ n ‚Üí ((seq y n) +‚Ąö őĶ‚āā) ‚Č§‚Ąö (seq z n))
        wyzRest = snd wyz

        őĶ‚āāpos : 0‚Ąö <‚Ąö őĶ‚āā
        őĶ‚āāpos = fst wyzRest

        packYZ : ő£ ‚Ąē (őĽ N ‚Üí (n : ‚Ąē) ‚Üí N ‚Č§ n ‚Üí ((seq y n) +‚Ąö őĶ‚āā) ‚Č§‚Ąö (seq z n))
        packYZ = snd wyzRest

        Nyz : ‚Ąē
        Nyz = fst packYZ

        convYZ : (n : ‚Ąē) ‚Üí Nyz ‚Č§ n ‚Üí ((seq y n) +‚Ąö őĶ‚āā) ‚Č§‚Ąö (seq z n)
        convYZ = snd packYZ

        -- ¬ß Phase 2: Shrink the surviving margin
        őĶ : ‚Ąö
        őĶ = őĶQuarter őĶ‚āĀ

        őĶpos : 0‚Ąö <‚Ąö őĶ
        őĶpos = őĶQuarter-pos őĶ‚āĀ

        N : ‚Ąē
        N = Nxy +‚Ąē Nyz

        Nxy‚Č§N : Nxy ‚Č§ N
        Nxy‚Č§N =
          let
            mono : (Nxy +‚Ąē zero) ‚Č§ (Nxy +‚Ąē Nyz)
            mono = ‚Č§-+‚Ąē-monoň° {a = zero} {b = Nyz} z‚Č§n Nxy
          in
          subst (őĽ t ‚Üí t ‚Č§ (Nxy +‚Ąē Nyz)) (+‚Ąē-zero-right Nxy) mono

        Nyz‚Č§N : Nyz ‚Č§ N
        Nyz‚Č§N =
          let
            mono : (Nyz +‚Ąē zero) ‚Č§ (Nyz +‚Ąē Nxy)
            mono = ‚Č§-+‚Ąē-monoň° {a = zero} {b = Nxy} z‚Č§n Nyz

            base : Nyz ‚Č§ (Nyz +‚Ąē Nxy)
            base = subst (őĽ t ‚Üí t ‚Č§ (Nyz +‚Ąē Nxy)) (+‚Ąē-zero-right Nyz) mono
          in
          subst (őĽ t ‚Üí Nyz ‚Č§ t) (+‚Ąē-comm Nyz Nxy) base
      in
      őĶ , (őĶpos ,
        (N , (őĽ n N‚Č§n ‚Üí
          let
            -- ¬ß Phase 3: Chain the inequalities
            Nxy‚Č§n : Nxy ‚Č§ n
            Nxy‚Č§n = ‚Č§-trans Nxy‚Č§N N‚Č§n

            Nyz‚Č§n : Nyz ‚Č§ n
            Nyz‚Č§n = ‚Č§-trans Nyz‚Č§N N‚Č§n

            xn : ‚Ąö
            xn = seq x n

            yn : ‚Ąö
            yn = seq y n

            zn : ‚Ąö
            zn = seq z n

            xőĶ‚āĀ‚Č§y : (xn +‚Ąö őĶ‚āĀ) ‚Č§‚Ąö yn
            xőĶ‚āĀ‚Č§y = convXY n Nxy‚Č§n

            xőĶ‚Č§xőĶ‚āĀ : (xn +‚Ąö őĶ) ‚Č§‚Ąö (xn +‚Ąö őĶ‚āĀ)
            xőĶ‚Č§xőĶ‚āĀ =
              ‚Č§‚Ąö-+‚Ąö-mono-left xn őĶ őĶ‚āĀ (<‚Ąö‚Üí‚Č§‚Ąö (őĶQuarter<őĶ őĶ‚āĀ őĶ‚āĀpos))

            xőĶ‚Č§y : (xn +‚Ąö őĶ) ‚Č§‚Ąö yn
            xőĶ‚Č§y = ‚Č§‚Ąö-trans {(xn +‚Ąö őĶ)} {(xn +‚Ąö őĶ‚āĀ)} {yn} xőĶ‚Č§xőĶ‚āĀ xőĶ‚āĀ‚Č§y

            y‚Č§y+őĶ‚āā : yn ‚Č§‚Ąö (yn +‚Ąö őĶ‚āā)
            y‚Č§y+őĶ‚āā = ‚Č§‚Ąö-add-nonneg-right yn őĶ‚āā (<‚Ąö‚Üí‚Č§‚Ąö őĶ‚āāpos)

            xőĶ‚Č§y+őĶ‚āā : (xn +‚Ąö őĶ) ‚Č§‚Ąö (yn +‚Ąö őĶ‚āā)
            xőĶ‚Č§y+őĶ‚āā = ‚Č§‚Ąö-trans {(xn +‚Ąö őĶ)} {yn} {(yn +‚Ąö őĶ‚āā)} xőĶ‚Č§y y‚Č§y+őĶ‚āā
          in
            ‚Č§‚Ąö-trans xőĶ‚Č§y+őĶ‚āā (convYZ n Nyz‚Č§n))))
  }

\end{code}


%‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ
\section{Congruence of Strict Order under \texorpdfstring{$\simeq_{\mathbb{R}}$}{‚ČÉR}}
\label{sec:reals:strict-order-congruence}
%‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ

If strict order depends on which representative carries it, it is not an order on real numbers---it is merely an order on sequences.  The Cauchy quotient annihilates any comparison that cannot survive a representative swap.  The proof below shows that strict order does survive, by quartering the original witness gap and absorbing the equivalence corrections into the margin.

Strict order must survive elimination of representative choice, otherwise it would not descend from Cauchy data to the real quotient. That dependence is removed by transporting a strict witness across equivalence on both sides while preserving a positive asymptotic gap.

\textbf{Constraint:} The positive gap $\varepsilon_0$ that witnesses $x <_{\mathbb{R}} y$ must remain strictly positive after both sides are perturbed by representative change.

\textbf{Construction:} Quarter $\varepsilon_0$ into four budgets, spend two on transporting through $x \simeq x'$ and $y \simeq y'$, and retain a strictly positive remainder as the new witness.

\textbf{Consequence:} $<_{\mathbb{R}}$ is well-defined on the Cauchy quotient.  No comparison can be fabricated or destroyed by representative choice.

\subsection*{Law 23.6: Strict order is invariant under equivalence}

The witness margin survives both equivalence corrections by repeated quartering. This eliminates representative drift while preserving a strictly positive asymptotic gap.

\begin{code}

-- ¬ß Strict order respects ‚ČÉ‚ĄĚ on both sides
<‚ĄĚ-resp-‚ČÉ‚ĄĚ : {x x' y y' : ‚ĄĚ} ‚Üí x ‚ČÉ‚ĄĚ x' ‚Üí y ‚ČÉ‚ĄĚ y' ‚Üí x <‚ĄĚ y ‚Üí x' <‚ĄĚ y'
<‚ĄĚ-resp-‚ČÉ‚ĄĚ {x} {x'} {y} {y'} x‚ČÉx' y‚ČÉy' x<y =
  let
    -- ¬ß Phase 1: Unpack the strict-order witness
    wxy = _<‚ĄĚ_.ltWitness x<y

    őĶ‚āÄ : ‚Ąö
    őĶ‚āÄ = fst wxy

    wxyRest = snd wxy

    őĶ‚āÄpos : 0‚Ąö <‚Ąö őĶ‚āÄ
    őĶ‚āÄpos = fst wxyRest

    packXY = snd wxyRest

    Nxy : ‚Ąē
    Nxy = fst packXY

    convXY : (n : ‚Ąē) ‚Üí Nxy ‚Č§ n ‚Üí ((seq x n) +‚Ąö őĶ‚āÄ) ‚Č§‚Ąö (seq y n)
    convXY = snd packXY

    -- ¬ß Phase 2: Quarter the margin twice
    őĶ : ‚Ąö
    őĶ = őĶQuarter őĶ‚āÄ

    őĶpos : 0‚Ąö <‚Ąö őĶ
    őĶpos = őĶQuarter-pos őĶ‚āÄ

    őĪ : ‚Ąö
    őĪ = őĶQuarter őĶ

    ő≤ : ‚Ąö
    ő≤ = őĶQuarter őĶ

    őĪpos : 0‚Ąö <‚Ąö őĪ
    őĪpos = őĶQuarter-pos őĶ

    ő≤pos : 0‚Ąö <‚Ąö ő≤
    ő≤pos = őĶQuarter-pos őĶ

    -- ¬ß Phase 3: Derive closeness from equivalences
    x'‚Č§x : x' ‚Č§‚ĄĚ x
    x'‚Č§x = ‚ČÉ‚ĄĚ‚Üí‚Č§‚ĄĚ (‚ČÉ‚ĄĚ-sym x‚ČÉx')

    y‚Č§y' : y ‚Č§‚ĄĚ y'
    y‚Č§y' = ‚ČÉ‚ĄĚ‚Üí‚Č§‚ĄĚ y‚ČÉy'

    packX = _‚Č§‚ĄĚ_.leReal x'‚Č§x őĪ őĪpos
    packY = _‚Č§‚ĄĚ_.leReal y‚Č§y' ő≤ ő≤pos

    Nx : ‚Ąē
    Nx = fst packX

    Ny : ‚Ąē
    Ny = fst packY

    boundX : (n : ‚Ąē) ‚Üí Nx ‚Č§ n ‚Üí (seq x' n) ‚Č§‚Ąö ((seq x n) +‚Ąö őĪ)
    boundX = snd packX

    boundY : (n : ‚Ąē) ‚Üí Ny ‚Č§ n ‚Üí (seq y n) ‚Č§‚Ąö ((seq y' n) +‚Ąö ő≤)
    boundY = snd packY

    -- ¬ß Phase 4: Global modulus N
    N : ‚Ąē
    N = Nxy +‚Ąē (Nx +‚Ąē Ny)

    Nxy‚Č§N : Nxy ‚Č§ N
    Nxy‚Č§N =
      let
        step : (Nxy +‚Ąē zero) ‚Č§ (Nxy +‚Ąē (Nx +‚Ąē Ny))
        step = ‚Č§-+‚Ąē-monoň° {a = zero} {b = (Nx +‚Ąē Ny)} z‚Č§n Nxy
      in
      subst (őĽ t ‚Üí t ‚Č§ (Nxy +‚Ąē (Nx +‚Ąē Ny))) (+‚Ąē-zero-right Nxy) step

    Nx‚Č§N : Nx ‚Č§ N
    Nx‚Č§N =
      let
        step : (Nx +‚Ąē zero) ‚Č§ (Nx +‚Ąē (Nxy +‚Ąē Ny))
        step = ‚Č§-+‚Ąē-monoň° {a = zero} {b = (Nxy +‚Ąē Ny)} z‚Č§n Nx

        base : Nx ‚Č§ (Nx +‚Ąē (Nxy +‚Ąē Ny))
        base = subst (őĽ t ‚Üí t ‚Č§ (Nx +‚Ąē (Nxy +‚Ąē Ny))) (+‚Ąē-zero-right Nx) step

        eq : (Nx +‚Ąē (Nxy +‚Ąē Ny)) ‚Č° (Nxy +‚Ąē (Nx +‚Ąē Ny))
        eq =
          trans
            (sym (+‚Ąē-assoc Nx Nxy Ny))
            (trans
              (cong (őĽ t ‚Üí t +‚Ąē Ny) (+‚Ąē-comm Nx Nxy))
              (+‚Ąē-assoc Nxy Nx Ny))
      in
      subst (őĽ t ‚Üí Nx ‚Č§ t) eq base

    Ny‚Č§N : Ny ‚Č§ N
    Ny‚Č§N =
      let
        step : (Ny +‚Ąē zero) ‚Č§ (Ny +‚Ąē (Nxy +‚Ąē Nx))
        step = ‚Č§-+‚Ąē-monoň° {a = zero} {b = (Nxy +‚Ąē Nx)} z‚Č§n Ny

        base : Ny ‚Č§ (Ny +‚Ąē (Nxy +‚Ąē Nx))
        base = subst (őĽ t ‚Üí t ‚Č§ (Ny +‚Ąē (Nxy +‚Ąē Nx))) (+‚Ąē-zero-right Ny) step

        eq : (Ny +‚Ąē (Nxy +‚Ąē Nx)) ‚Č° (Nxy +‚Ąē (Nx +‚Ąē Ny))
        eq =
          trans
            (sym (+‚Ąē-assoc Ny Nxy Nx))
            (trans
              (cong (őĽ t ‚Üí t +‚Ąē Nx) (+‚Ąē-comm Ny Nxy))
              (trans
                (+‚Ąē-assoc Nxy Ny Nx)
                (cong (őĽ t ‚Üí Nxy +‚Ąē t) (+‚Ąē-comm Ny Nx))))
      in
      subst (őĽ t ‚Üí Ny ‚Č§ t) eq base
  in
  record
    { ltWitness =
        őĶ ,
        ( őĶpos
        , ( N
          , (őĽ n N‚Č§n ‚Üí
              let
                -- ¬ß Phase 5: The chain x'_n + őĶ ‚Č§ y'_n
                Nxy‚Č§n : Nxy ‚Č§ n
                Nxy‚Č§n = ‚Č§-trans Nxy‚Č§N N‚Č§n

                Nx‚Č§n : Nx ‚Č§ n
                Nx‚Č§n = ‚Č§-trans Nx‚Č§N N‚Č§n

                Ny‚Č§n : Ny ‚Č§ n
                Ny‚Č§n = ‚Č§-trans Ny‚Č§N N‚Č§n

                xn : ‚Ąö
                xn = seq x n

                x'n : ‚Ąö
                x'n = seq x' n

                yn : ‚Ąö
                yn = seq y n

                y'n : ‚Ąö
                y'n = seq y' n

                x'n‚Č§xn+őĪ : x'n ‚Č§‚Ąö (xn +‚Ąö őĪ)
                x'n‚Č§xn+őĪ = boundX n Nx‚Č§n

                őĪ+ő≤<őĶ : (őĪ +‚Ąö ő≤) <‚Ąö őĶ
                őĪ+ő≤<őĶ = őĶQuarter-double<őĶ őĶ őĶpos

                őĪ+ő≤‚Č§őĶ : (őĪ +‚Ąö ő≤) ‚Č§‚Ąö őĶ
                őĪ+ő≤‚Č§őĶ = <‚Ąö‚Üí‚Č§‚Ąö {(őĪ +‚Ąö ő≤)} {őĶ} őĪ+ő≤<őĶ

                őĶ+őĪ+ő≤‚Č§őĶ+őĶ : (őĶ +‚Ąö (őĪ +‚Ąö ő≤)) ‚Č§‚Ąö (őĶ +‚Ąö őĶ)
                őĶ+őĪ+ő≤‚Č§őĶ+őĶ = ‚Č§‚Ąö-+‚Ąö-mono-left őĶ (őĪ +‚Ąö ő≤) őĶ őĪ+ő≤‚Č§őĶ

                őĶ+őĶ<őĶ‚āÄ : (őĶ +‚Ąö őĶ) <‚Ąö őĶ‚āÄ
                őĶ+őĶ<őĶ‚āÄ = őĶQuarter-double<őĶ őĶ‚āÄ őĶ‚āÄpos

                őĶ+őĶ‚Č§őĶ‚āÄ : (őĶ +‚Ąö őĶ) ‚Č§‚Ąö őĶ‚āÄ
                őĶ+őĶ‚Č§őĶ‚āÄ = <‚Ąö‚Üí‚Č§‚Ąö {(őĶ +‚Ąö őĶ)} {őĶ‚āÄ} őĶ+őĶ<őĶ‚āÄ

                őĶ+őĪ+ő≤‚Č§őĶ‚āÄ : (őĶ +‚Ąö (őĪ +‚Ąö ő≤)) ‚Č§‚Ąö őĶ‚āÄ
                őĶ+őĪ+ő≤‚Č§őĶ‚āÄ = ‚Č§‚Ąö-trans {(őĶ +‚Ąö (őĪ +‚Ąö ő≤))} {(őĶ +‚Ąö őĶ)} {őĶ‚āÄ} őĶ+őĪ+ő≤‚Č§őĶ+őĶ őĶ+őĶ‚Č§őĶ‚āÄ

                t : ‚Ąö
                t = őĪ +‚Ąö (őĶ +‚Ąö ő≤)

                t‚ČÉőĶ+őĪ+ő≤ : t ‚ČÉ‚Ąö (őĶ +‚Ąö (őĪ +‚Ąö ő≤))
                t‚ČÉőĶ+őĪ+ő≤ =
                  ‚ČÉ‚Ąö-trans
                    (‚ČÉ‚Ąö-sym (+‚Ąö-assoc őĪ őĶ ő≤))
                    (‚ČÉ‚Ąö-trans
                      (+‚Ąö-resp-‚ČÉ (+‚Ąö-comm őĪ őĶ) (‚ČÉ‚Ąö-refl ő≤))
                      (+‚Ąö-assoc őĶ őĪ ő≤))

                t‚Č§őĶ‚āÄ : t ‚Č§‚Ąö őĶ‚āÄ
                t‚Č§őĶ‚āÄ = ‚Č§‚Ąö-trans (‚ČÉ‚Ąö‚Üí‚Č§‚Ąöň° t‚ČÉőĶ+őĪ+ő≤) őĶ+őĪ+ő≤‚Č§őĶ‚āÄ

                xnt‚Č§xnőĶ‚āÄ : (xn +‚Ąö t) ‚Č§‚Ąö (xn +‚Ąö őĶ‚āÄ)
                xnt‚Č§xnőĶ‚āÄ = ‚Č§‚Ąö-+‚Ąö-mono-left xn t őĶ‚āÄ t‚Č§őĶ‚āÄ

                x'n+őĶ+ő≤‚Č§xn+t : (x'n +‚Ąö (őĶ +‚Ąö ő≤)) ‚Č§‚Ąö (xn +‚Ąö t)
                x'n+őĶ+ő≤‚Č§xn+t =
                  let
                    step‚āĀ : (x'n +‚Ąö (őĶ +‚Ąö ő≤)) ‚Č§‚Ąö ((xn +‚Ąö őĪ) +‚Ąö (őĶ +‚Ąö ő≤))
                    step‚āĀ = ‚Č§‚Ąö-+‚Ąö-mono-right x'n (xn +‚Ąö őĪ) (őĶ +‚Ąö ő≤) x'n‚Č§xn+őĪ

                    lhsEq : ((xn +‚Ąö őĪ) +‚Ąö (őĶ +‚Ąö ő≤)) ‚ČÉ‚Ąö (xn +‚Ąö t)
                    lhsEq = +‚Ąö-assoc xn őĪ (őĶ +‚Ąö ő≤)
                  in
                  ‚Č§‚Ąö-trans step‚āĀ (‚ČÉ‚Ąö‚Üí‚Č§‚Ąöň° lhsEq)

                x'n+őĶ+ő≤‚Č§xn+őĶ‚āÄ : (x'n +‚Ąö (őĶ +‚Ąö ő≤)) ‚Č§‚Ąö (xn +‚Ąö őĶ‚āÄ)
                x'n+őĶ+ő≤‚Č§xn+őĶ‚āÄ = ‚Č§‚Ąö-trans {(x'n +‚Ąö (őĶ +‚Ąö ő≤))} {(xn +‚Ąö t)} {(xn +‚Ąö őĶ‚āÄ)} x'n+őĶ+ő≤‚Č§xn+t xnt‚Č§xnőĶ‚āÄ

                xn+őĶ‚āÄ‚Č§yn : (xn +‚Ąö őĶ‚āÄ) ‚Č§‚Ąö yn
                xn+őĶ‚āÄ‚Č§yn = convXY n Nxy‚Č§n

                x'n+őĶ+ő≤‚Č§yn : (x'n +‚Ąö (őĶ +‚Ąö ő≤)) ‚Č§‚Ąö yn
                x'n+őĶ+ő≤‚Č§yn = ‚Č§‚Ąö-trans {(x'n +‚Ąö (őĶ +‚Ąö ő≤))} {(xn +‚Ąö őĶ‚āÄ)} {yn} x'n+őĶ+ő≤‚Č§xn+őĶ‚āÄ xn+őĶ‚āÄ‚Č§yn

                x'n+őĶ‚Č§yn-ő≤ : (x'n +‚Ąö őĶ) ‚Č§‚Ąö (yn +‚Ąö (-‚Ąö ő≤))
                x'n+őĶ‚Č§yn-ő≤ =
                  let
                    step‚āĀ : ((x'n +‚Ąö (őĶ +‚Ąö ő≤)) +‚Ąö (-‚Ąö ő≤)) ‚Č§‚Ąö (yn +‚Ąö (-‚Ąö ő≤))
                    step‚āĀ = ‚Č§‚Ąö-+‚Ąö-mono-right (x'n +‚Ąö (őĶ +‚Ąö ő≤)) yn (-‚Ąö ő≤) x'n+őĶ+ő≤‚Č§yn

                    lhsEq‚āĀ : ((x'n +‚Ąö (őĶ +‚Ąö ő≤)) +‚Ąö (-‚Ąö ő≤)) ‚ČÉ‚Ąö (x'n +‚Ąö ((őĶ +‚Ąö ő≤) +‚Ąö (-‚Ąö ő≤)))
                    lhsEq‚āĀ = +‚Ąö-assoc x'n (őĶ +‚Ąö ő≤) (-‚Ąö ő≤)

                    lhsEq‚āā : ((őĶ +‚Ąö ő≤) +‚Ąö (-‚Ąö ő≤)) ‚ČÉ‚Ąö őĶ
                    lhsEq‚āā =
                      ‚ČÉ‚Ąö-trans
                        (+‚Ąö-assoc őĶ ő≤ (-‚Ąö ő≤))
                        (‚ČÉ‚Ąö-trans
                          (+‚Ąö-resp-‚ČÉ (‚ČÉ‚Ąö-refl őĶ) (+‚Ąö-inv-right ő≤))
                          (+‚Ąö-zero-right őĶ))

                    lhsEq : ((x'n +‚Ąö (őĶ +‚Ąö ő≤)) +‚Ąö (-‚Ąö ő≤)) ‚ČÉ‚Ąö (x'n +‚Ąö őĶ)
                    lhsEq = ‚ČÉ‚Ąö-trans lhsEq‚āĀ (+‚Ąö-resp-‚ČÉ (‚ČÉ‚Ąö-refl x'n) lhsEq‚āā)
                  in
                  ‚Č§‚Ąö-trans (‚ČÉ‚Ąö‚Üí‚Č§‚Ąö ≥ lhsEq) step‚āĀ

                yn‚Č§y'n+ő≤ : yn ‚Č§‚Ąö (y'n +‚Ąö ő≤)
                yn‚Č§y'n+ő≤ = boundY n Ny‚Č§n

                yn-ő≤‚Č§y'n : (yn +‚Ąö (-‚Ąö ő≤)) ‚Č§‚Ąö y'n
                yn-ő≤‚Č§y'n =
                  let
                    step‚āĀ : (yn +‚Ąö (-‚Ąö ő≤)) ‚Č§‚Ąö ((y'n +‚Ąö ő≤) +‚Ąö (-‚Ąö ő≤))
                    step‚āĀ = ‚Č§‚Ąö-+‚Ąö-mono-right yn (y'n +‚Ąö ő≤) (-‚Ąö ő≤) yn‚Č§y'n+ő≤

                    rhsEq‚āĀ : ((y'n +‚Ąö ő≤) +‚Ąö (-‚Ąö ő≤)) ‚ČÉ‚Ąö (y'n +‚Ąö (ő≤ +‚Ąö (-‚Ąö ő≤)))
                    rhsEq‚āĀ = +‚Ąö-assoc y'n ő≤ (-‚Ąö ő≤)

                    rhsEq‚āā : (ő≤ +‚Ąö (-‚Ąö ő≤)) ‚ČÉ‚Ąö 0‚Ąö
                    rhsEq‚āā = +‚Ąö-inv-right ő≤

                    rhsEq : ((y'n +‚Ąö ő≤) +‚Ąö (-‚Ąö ő≤)) ‚ČÉ‚Ąö y'n
                    rhsEq =
                      ‚ČÉ‚Ąö-trans
                        rhsEq‚āĀ
                        (‚ČÉ‚Ąö-trans
                          (+‚Ąö-resp-‚ČÉ (‚ČÉ‚Ąö-refl y'n) rhsEq‚āā)
                          (+‚Ąö-zero-right y'n))
                  in
                  ‚Č§‚Ąö-trans step‚āĀ (‚ČÉ‚Ąö‚Üí‚Č§‚Ąöň° rhsEq)
              in
              ‚Č§‚Ąö-trans x'n+őĶ‚Č§yn-ő≤ yn-ő≤‚Č§y'n
            )
          )
        )
    }
\end{code}


%‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ
\section{Reflexivity of \texorpdfstring{$\le_{\mathbb{R}}$}{<=R}}
\label{sec:reals:leq-reflexivity}
%‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ

Any admissible asymptotic order must retain self-comparison. The positive margin only relaxes the comparison further, so any failure of reflexivity would contradict the order structure already forced on representatives.

\subsection*{Law 23.7: Non-strict order is reflexive}

Reflexivity is forced because every positive margin preserves the same sequence pointwise. Self-comparison must therefore hold on every tail with no residual freedom.

\begin{code}
-- ¬ß Reflexivity of ‚Č§‚ĄĚ
‚Č§‚ĄĚ-refl : (x : ‚ĄĚ) ‚Üí x ‚Č§‚ĄĚ x
‚Č§‚ĄĚ-refl x = record
  { leReal = őĽ őĶ őĶpos ‚Üí
      zero , (őĽ n _ ‚Üí
        ‚Č§‚Ąö-add-nonneg-right (seq x n) őĶ (<‚Ąö‚Üí‚Č§‚Ąö őĶpos))
  }
\end{code}


%‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ
\section{Transitivity of \texorpdfstring{$\le_{\mathbb{R}}$}{<=R}}
\label{sec:reals:leq-transitivity}
%‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ

Eventual upper bounds are forced to compose across an intermediate real. The only remaining work is to control the accumulated margin so that the composite comparison still fits inside the requested tolerance.

\subsection*{Law 23.8: Non-strict order is transitive}

Two eventual $\varepsilon/4$ bounds are forced into one eventual $\varepsilon$ bound. Associativity eliminates ambiguity in how the margins accumulate along the composite comparison.

\begin{code}
-- ¬ß Transitivity of ‚Č§‚ĄĚ via őĶ-splitting
‚Č§‚ĄĚ-trans : {x y z : ‚ĄĚ} ‚Üí x ‚Č§‚ĄĚ y ‚Üí y ‚Č§‚ĄĚ z ‚Üí x ‚Č§‚ĄĚ z
‚Č§‚ĄĚ-trans {x} {y} {z} x‚Č§y y‚Č§z = record
  { leReal = őĽ őĶ őĶpos ‚Üí
      let
        -- ¬ß Phase 1: Budget allocation
        őĶq : ‚Ąö
        őĶq = őĶQuarter őĶ

        őĶqPos : 0‚Ąö <‚Ąö őĶq
        őĶqPos = őĶQuarter-pos őĶ

        őĶqNonneg : 0‚Ąö ‚Č§‚Ąö őĶq
        őĶqNonneg = <‚Ąö‚Üí‚Č§‚Ąö {0‚Ąö} {őĶq} őĶqPos

        őĶq+őĶq<őĶ : (őĶq +‚Ąö őĶq) <‚Ąö őĶ
        őĶq+őĶq<őĶ = őĶQuarter-double<őĶ őĶ őĶpos

        NxyPack : ő£ ‚Ąē (őĽ N ‚Üí (n : ‚Ąē) ‚Üí N ‚Č§ n ‚Üí (seq x n) ‚Č§‚Ąö ((seq y n) +‚Ąö őĶq))
        NxyPack = _‚Č§‚ĄĚ_.leReal x‚Č§y őĶq őĶqPos

        NyzPack : ő£ ‚Ąē (őĽ N ‚Üí (n : ‚Ąē) ‚Üí N ‚Č§ n ‚Üí (seq y n) ‚Č§‚Ąö ((seq z n) +‚Ąö őĶq))
        NyzPack = _‚Č§‚ĄĚ_.leReal y‚Č§z őĶq őĶqPos

        Nxy : ‚Ąē
        Nxy = fst NxyPack

        Nyz : ‚Ąē
        Nyz = fst NyzPack

        NxyConv : (n : ‚Ąē) ‚Üí Nxy ‚Č§ n ‚Üí (seq x n) ‚Č§‚Ąö ((seq y n) +‚Ąö őĶq)
        NxyConv = snd NxyPack

        NyzConv : (n : ‚Ąē) ‚Üí Nyz ‚Č§ n ‚Üí (seq y n) ‚Č§‚Ąö ((seq z n) +‚Ąö őĶq)
        NyzConv = snd NyzPack

        -- ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ Phase 2: Global modulus N ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ
        N : ‚Ąē
        N = Nxy +‚Ąē Nyz

        Nxy‚Č§N : Nxy ‚Č§ N
        Nxy‚Č§N =
          let
            mono : (Nxy +‚Ąē zero) ‚Č§ (Nxy +‚Ąē Nyz)
            mono = ‚Č§-+‚Ąē-monoň° {a = zero} {b = Nyz} z‚Č§n Nxy
          in
          subst (őĽ t ‚Üí t ‚Č§ (Nxy +‚Ąē Nyz)) (+‚Ąē-zero-right Nxy) mono

        Nyz‚Č§N : Nyz ‚Č§ N
        Nyz‚Č§N =
          let
            mono : (Nyz +‚Ąē zero) ‚Č§ (Nyz +‚Ąē Nxy)
            mono = ‚Č§-+‚Ąē-monoň° {a = zero} {b = Nxy} z‚Č§n Nyz

            base : Nyz ‚Č§ (Nyz +‚Ąē Nxy)
            base = subst (őĽ t ‚Üí t ‚Č§ (Nyz +‚Ąē Nxy)) (+‚Ąē-zero-right Nyz) mono
          in
          subst (őĽ t ‚Üí Nyz ‚Č§ t) (+‚Ąē-comm Nyz Nxy) base
      in
      N , (őĽ n N‚Č§n ‚Üí
        let
          -- ¬ß Phase 3: Chain and reassociate
          Nxy‚Č§n : Nxy ‚Č§ n
          Nxy‚Č§n = ‚Č§-trans Nxy‚Č§N N‚Č§n

          Nyz‚Č§n : Nyz ‚Č§ n
          Nyz‚Č§n = ‚Č§-trans Nyz‚Č§N N‚Č§n

          xn : ‚Ąö
          xn = seq x n

          yn : ‚Ąö
          yn = seq y n

          zn : ‚Ąö
          zn = seq z n

          xn‚Č§yn+őĶq : xn ‚Č§‚Ąö (yn +‚Ąö őĶq)
          xn‚Č§yn+őĶq = NxyConv n Nxy‚Č§n

          yn‚Č§zn+őĶq : yn ‚Č§‚Ąö (zn +‚Ąö őĶq)
          yn‚Č§zn+őĶq = NyzConv n Nyz‚Č§n

          step‚āĀ : (yn +‚Ąö őĶq) ‚Č§‚Ąö ((zn +‚Ąö őĶq) +‚Ąö őĶq)
          step‚āĀ = ‚Č§‚Ąö-+‚Ąö-mono-right yn (zn +‚Ąö őĶq) őĶq yn‚Č§zn+őĶq

          step‚āā : xn ‚Č§‚Ąö ((zn +‚Ąö őĶq) +‚Ąö őĶq)
          step‚āā = ‚Č§‚Ąö-trans {xn} {(yn +‚Ąö őĶq)} {((zn +‚Ąö őĶq) +‚Ąö őĶq)} xn‚Č§yn+őĶq step‚āĀ

          step‚āÉ : ((zn +‚Ąö őĶq) +‚Ąö őĶq) ‚Č§‚Ąö (zn +‚Ąö (őĶq +‚Ąö őĶq))
          step‚āÉ = ‚ČÉ‚Ąö‚Üí‚Č§‚Ąöň° {((zn +‚Ąö őĶq) +‚Ąö őĶq)} {(zn +‚Ąö (őĶq +‚Ąö őĶq))} (+‚Ąö-assoc zn őĶq őĶq)

          step‚āĄ : (zn +‚Ąö (őĶq +‚Ąö őĶq)) ‚Č§‚Ąö (zn +‚Ąö őĶ)
          step‚āĄ = ‚Č§‚Ąö-+‚Ąö-mono-left zn (őĶq +‚Ąö őĶq) őĶ (<‚Ąö‚Üí‚Č§‚Ąö őĶq+őĶq<őĶ)

          done : xn ‚Č§‚Ąö (zn +‚Ąö őĶ)
          done = ‚Č§‚Ąö-trans {xn} {((zn +‚Ąö őĶq) +‚Ąö őĶq)} {(zn +‚Ąö őĶ)} step‚āā (‚Č§‚Ąö-trans step‚āÉ step‚āĄ)
        in
        done)
  }
\end{code}


%‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ
\section{Antisymmetry of \texorpdfstring{$\le_{\mathbb{R}}$}{<=R}}
\label{sec:reals:leq-antisymmetry}
%‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ

Mutual asymptotic domination must collapse to equality of reals. If two representatives eventually bound each other at every positive margin, no residual distance can survive in the quotient.

\subsection*{Law 23.9: Mutual non-strict order forces equivalence}

Two-sided eventual bounds force asymptotic distance below every positive margin. What survives is exactly equivalence of the underlying Cauchy representatives.

\begin{code}
-- ¬ß Antisymmetry: mutual ‚Č§‚ĄĚ yields ‚ČÉ‚ĄĚ
‚Č§‚ĄĚ-antisym : {x y : ‚ĄĚ} ‚Üí x ‚Č§‚ĄĚ y ‚Üí y ‚Č§‚ĄĚ x ‚Üí x ‚ČÉ‚ĄĚ y
‚Č§‚ĄĚ-antisym {x} {y} x‚Č§y y‚Č§x = record
  { conv0 = őĽ őĶ őĶpos ‚Üí
      let
        őĶq : ‚Ąö
        őĶq = őĶQuarter őĶ

        őĶqPos : 0‚Ąö <‚Ąö őĶq
        őĶqPos = őĶQuarter-pos őĶ

        NxyPack : ő£ ‚Ąē (őĽ N ‚Üí (n : ‚Ąē) ‚Üí N ‚Č§ n ‚Üí (seq x n) ‚Č§‚Ąö ((seq y n) +‚Ąö őĶq))
        NxyPack = _‚Č§‚ĄĚ_.leReal x‚Č§y őĶq őĶqPos

        NyxPack : ő£ ‚Ąē (őĽ N ‚Üí (n : ‚Ąē) ‚Üí N ‚Č§ n ‚Üí (seq y n) ‚Č§‚Ąö ((seq x n) +‚Ąö őĶq))
        NyxPack = _‚Č§‚ĄĚ_.leReal y‚Č§x őĶq őĶqPos

        Nxy : ‚Ąē
        Nxy = fst NxyPack

        Nyx : ‚Ąē
        Nyx = fst NyxPack

        NxyConv : (n : ‚Ąē) ‚Üí Nxy ‚Č§ n ‚Üí (seq x n) ‚Č§‚Ąö ((seq y n) +‚Ąö őĶq)
        NxyConv = snd NxyPack

        NyxConv : (n : ‚Ąē) ‚Üí Nyx ‚Č§ n ‚Üí (seq y n) ‚Č§‚Ąö ((seq x n) +‚Ąö őĶq)
        NyxConv = snd NyxPack

        N : ‚Ąē
        N = Nxy +‚Ąē Nyx

        Nxy‚Č§N : Nxy ‚Č§ N
        Nxy‚Č§N =
          let
            mono : (Nxy +‚Ąē zero) ‚Č§ (Nxy +‚Ąē Nyx)
            mono = ‚Č§-+‚Ąē-monoň° {a = zero} {b = Nyx} z‚Č§n Nxy
          in
          subst (őĽ t ‚Üí t ‚Č§ (Nxy +‚Ąē Nyx)) (+‚Ąē-zero-right Nxy) mono

        Nyx‚Č§N : Nyx ‚Č§ N
        Nyx‚Č§N =
          let
            mono : (Nyx +‚Ąē zero) ‚Č§ (Nyx +‚Ąē Nxy)
            mono = ‚Č§-+‚Ąē-monoň° {a = zero} {b = Nxy} z‚Č§n Nyx

            base : Nyx ‚Č§ (Nyx +‚Ąē Nxy)
            base = subst (őĽ t ‚Üí t ‚Č§ (Nyx +‚Ąē Nxy)) (+‚Ąē-zero-right Nyx) mono
          in
          subst (őĽ t ‚Üí Nyx ‚Č§ t) (+‚Ąē-comm Nyx Nxy) base
      in
      N , (őĽ n N‚Č§n ‚Üí
        let
          Nxy‚Č§n : Nxy ‚Č§ n
          Nxy‚Č§n = ‚Č§-trans Nxy‚Č§N N‚Č§n

          Nyx‚Č§n : Nyx ‚Č§ n
          Nyx‚Č§n = ‚Č§-trans Nyx‚Č§N N‚Č§n

          xn : ‚Ąö
          xn = seq x n

          yn : ‚Ąö
          yn = seq y n

          xn‚Č§yn+őĶq : xn ‚Č§‚Ąö (yn +‚Ąö őĶq)
          xn‚Č§yn+őĶq = NxyConv n Nxy‚Č§n

          yn‚Č§xn+őĶq : yn ‚Č§‚Ąö (xn +‚Ąö őĶq)
          yn‚Č§xn+őĶq = NyxConv n Nyx‚Č§n

          d‚Č§őĶq : dist‚Ąö xn yn ‚Č§‚Ąö őĶq
          d‚Č§őĶq = dist‚Ąö-bounded-by-őĶ xn yn őĶq xn‚Č§yn+őĶq yn‚Č§xn+őĶq

          őĶq<őĶ : őĶq <‚Ąö őĶ
          őĶq<őĶ = őĶQuarter<őĶ őĶ őĶpos
        in
        ‚Č§<‚Ąö‚Üí<‚Ąö {dist‚Ąö xn yn} {őĶq} {őĶ} d‚Č§őĶq őĶq<őĶ)
  }
\end{code}


%‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ
\section{Congruence of \texorpdfstring{$\le_{\mathbb{R}}$}{<=R} under \texorpdfstring{$\simeq_{\mathbb{R}}$}{‚ČÉR}}
\label{sec:reals:leq-congruence}
%‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ

Strict order has been saved.  But if non-strict order cannot survive the same representative swap, the ordered-field structure is only half-built: reflexivity and transitivity would apply to sequences, not to reals.  The rescue is simpler than for strict order, because $\leq_{\mathbb{R}}$ can be transported by composing with the equivalence-induced bounds that already exist.

Non-strict order must also survive elimination of representative choice, otherwise order would not descend to the real quotient. The only admissible transport is composition with the equivalence-induced order bounds already forced above.

\textbf{Constraint:} $\leq_{\mathbb{R}}$ must be well-defined on the Cauchy quotient: if $x \simeq x'$ and $y \simeq y'$, then $x \leq y$ implies $x' \leq y'$.

\textbf{Construction:} Chain $x' \leq x$ (from $x \simeq x'$), $x \leq y$ (given), and $y \leq y'$ (from $y \simeq y'$) via transitivity.

\textbf{Consequence:} $\leq_{\mathbb{R}}$ descends to the quotient.  Together with $<_{\mathbb{R}}$, the full order structure is now representative-independent.

\subsection*{Law 23.10: Non-strict order is invariant under equivalence}

The congruence law is forced by composing equivalence-induced bounds on the left and right. No additional order data survives elimination.

\begin{code}
-- ¬ß Non-strict order respects ‚ČÉ‚ĄĚ
‚Č§‚ĄĚ-resp-‚ČÉ‚ĄĚ : {x x' y y' : ‚ĄĚ} ‚Üí x ‚ČÉ‚ĄĚ x' ‚Üí y ‚ČÉ‚ĄĚ y' ‚Üí x ‚Č§‚ĄĚ y ‚Üí x' ‚Č§‚ĄĚ y'
‚Č§‚ĄĚ-resp-‚ČÉ‚ĄĚ {x} {x'} {y} {y'} x‚ČÉx' y‚ČÉy' x‚Č§y =
  let
    x'‚Č§x : x' ‚Č§‚ĄĚ x
    x'‚Č§x = ‚ČÉ‚ĄĚ‚Üí‚Č§‚ĄĚ (‚ČÉ‚ĄĚ-sym x‚ČÉx')

    y‚Č§y' : y ‚Č§‚ĄĚ y'
    y‚Č§y' = ‚ČÉ‚ĄĚ‚Üí‚Č§‚ĄĚ y‚ČÉy'
  in
  ‚Č§‚ĄĚ-trans (‚Č§‚ĄĚ-trans x'‚Č§x x‚Č§y) y‚Č§y'
\end{code}


%‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ
\section{Right Additive Monotonicity of \texorpdfstring{$\le_{\mathbb{R}}$}{<=R}}
\label{sec:reals:right-additive-monotonicity}
%‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ

An ordered ring in which addition can reverse an inequality is useless for physics: every energy shift, every spectral translation depends on monotonicity under addition.  Adding a common term to both sides of an inequality must not destroy the comparison, or the later additive transport laws for the physical constants collapse.

Right addition must preserve asymptotic order. Once addition has been forced on reals, adjoining the same summand on the right cannot create a new comparison failure.

\textbf{Constraint:} $x \leq y$ must imply $x + z \leq y + z$ for every real~$z$.

\textbf{Construction:} Lift pointwise rational monotonicity through \texttt{+‚Ąö-mono-‚Č§-right}, then use $\varepsilon$-margin associativity to place the surviving margin correctly.

\textbf{Consequence:} Both right and left monotonicity are forced, completing the twelve-law ordered-field scaffold.

\subsection*{Law 23.11: Right addition preserves non-strict order}

Right monotonicity is forced by pointwise rational monotonicity. Associativity eliminates ambiguity in the placement of the surviving $\varepsilon$-margin.

\begin{code}
-- ¬ß Right monotonicity of +‚ĄĚ under ‚Č§‚ĄĚ
‚Č§‚ĄĚ-+‚ĄĚ-mono-right : {x y z : ‚ĄĚ} ‚Üí x ‚Č§‚ĄĚ y ‚Üí (x +‚ĄĚ z) ‚Č§‚ĄĚ (y +‚ĄĚ z)
‚Č§‚ĄĚ-+‚ĄĚ-mono-right {x} {y} {z} x‚Č§y = record
  { leReal = őĽ őĶ őĶpos ‚Üí
      let
        pack : ő£ ‚Ąē (őĽ N ‚Üí (n : ‚Ąē) ‚Üí N ‚Č§ n ‚Üí (seq x n) ‚Č§‚Ąö ((seq y n) +‚Ąö őĶ))
        pack = _‚Č§‚ĄĚ_.leReal x‚Č§y őĶ őĶpos

        N : ‚Ąē
        N = fst pack

        conv : (n : ‚Ąē) ‚Üí N ‚Č§ n ‚Üí (seq x n) ‚Č§‚Ąö ((seq y n) +‚Ąö őĶ)
        conv = snd pack
      in
      N , (őĽ n N‚Č§n ‚Üí
        let
          xn : ‚Ąö
          xn = seq x n

          yn : ‚Ąö
          yn = seq y n

          zn : ‚Ąö
          zn = seq z n

          step‚āĀ : (xn +‚Ąö zn) ‚Č§‚Ąö (((yn +‚Ąö őĶ) +‚Ąö zn))
          step‚āĀ = ‚Č§‚Ąö-+‚Ąö-mono-right xn (yn +‚Ąö őĶ) zn (conv n N‚Č§n)

          rhsEq : ((yn +‚Ąö őĶ) +‚Ąö zn) ‚ČÉ‚Ąö ((yn +‚Ąö zn) +‚Ąö őĶ)
          rhsEq =
            ‚ČÉ‚Ąö-trans
              (+‚Ąö-assoc yn őĶ zn)
              (‚ČÉ‚Ąö-trans
                (+‚Ąö-resp-‚ČÉ (‚ČÉ‚Ąö-refl yn) (+‚Ąö-comm őĶ zn))
                (‚ČÉ‚Ąö-sym (+‚Ąö-assoc yn zn őĶ)))

          step‚āā : (((yn +‚Ąö őĶ) +‚Ąö zn)) ‚Č§‚Ąö ((yn +‚Ąö zn) +‚Ąö őĶ)
          step‚āā = ‚ČÉ‚Ąö‚Üí‚Č§‚Ąöň° {(((yn +‚Ąö őĶ) +‚Ąö zn))} {((yn +‚Ąö zn) +‚Ąö őĶ)} rhsEq
        in
        ‚Č§‚Ąö-trans step‚āĀ step‚āā)
  }
\end{code}


%‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ
\section{Left Additive Monotonicity of \texorpdfstring{$\le_{\mathbb{R}}$}{<=R}}
\label{sec:reals:left-additive-monotonicity}
%‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ

Left addition must preserve the same asymptotic order. This removes any residual asymmetry between the two inputs of addition at the level of order transport.

\subsection*{Law 23.12: Left addition preserves non-strict order}

Left monotonicity is forced by the same rational order law, now applied through the left summand. Associativity again eliminates ambiguity in the placement of the surviving margin.

\begin{code}
-- ¬ß Left monotonicity of +‚ĄĚ under ‚Č§‚ĄĚ
‚Č§‚ĄĚ-+‚ĄĚ-mono-left : {x y z : ‚ĄĚ} ‚Üí x ‚Č§‚ĄĚ y ‚Üí (z +‚ĄĚ x) ‚Č§‚ĄĚ (z +‚ĄĚ y)
‚Č§‚ĄĚ-+‚ĄĚ-mono-left {x} {y} {z} x‚Č§y = record
  { leReal = őĽ őĶ őĶpos ‚Üí
      let
        pack : ő£ ‚Ąē (őĽ N ‚Üí (n : ‚Ąē) ‚Üí N ‚Č§ n ‚Üí (seq x n) ‚Č§‚Ąö ((seq y n) +‚Ąö őĶ))
        pack = _‚Č§‚ĄĚ_.leReal x‚Č§y őĶ őĶpos

        N : ‚Ąē
        N = fst pack

        conv : (n : ‚Ąē) ‚Üí N ‚Č§ n ‚Üí (seq x n) ‚Č§‚Ąö ((seq y n) +‚Ąö őĶ)
        conv = snd pack
      in
      N , (őĽ n N‚Č§n ‚Üí
        let
          xn : ‚Ąö
          xn = seq x n

          yn : ‚Ąö
          yn = seq y n

          zn : ‚Ąö
          zn = seq z n

          step‚āĀ : (zn +‚Ąö xn) ‚Č§‚Ąö (zn +‚Ąö (yn +‚Ąö őĶ))
          step‚āĀ = ‚Č§‚Ąö-+‚Ąö-mono-left zn xn (yn +‚Ąö őĶ) (conv n N‚Č§n)

          rhsEq : (zn +‚Ąö (yn +‚Ąö őĶ)) ‚ČÉ‚Ąö ((zn +‚Ąö yn) +‚Ąö őĶ)
          rhsEq = sym (+‚Ąö-assoc zn yn őĶ)

          step‚āā : (zn +‚Ąö (yn +‚Ąö őĶ)) ‚Č§‚Ąö ((zn +‚Ąö yn) +‚Ąö őĶ)
          step‚āā = ‚ČÉ‚Ąö‚Üí‚Č§‚Ąöň° {(zn +‚Ąö (yn +‚Ąö őĶ))} {((zn +‚Ąö yn) +‚Ąö őĶ)} rhsEq
        in
        ‚Č§‚Ąö-trans step‚āĀ step‚āā)
  }
\end{code}

\paragraph{Where we now stand.}
The numerical machinery is now in place. Natural numbers, integers, rationals, reals, order, distance, scaling, and spectral operators have all been forced from the surviving structure rather than imported from outside it. The document now possesses the full language required to evaluate $K_4$ quantitatively.

\paragraph{What must happen next.}
The final step is assembly. The invariants already derived must be gathered into a single verified record so that the physical correspondence can be inspected as computation rather than impression.


%‚ēź‚ēź‚ēź‚ēź‚ēź‚ēź‚ēź‚ēź‚ēź‚ēź‚ēź‚ēź‚ēź‚ēź‚ēź‚ēź‚ēź‚ēź‚ēź‚ēź‚ēź‚ēź‚ēź‚ēź‚ēź‚ēź‚ēź‚ēź‚ēź‚ēź‚ēź‚ēź‚ēź‚ēź‚ēź‚ēź‚ēź‚ēź‚ēź‚ēź‚ēź‚ēź‚ēź‚ēź‚ēź‚ēź‚ēź‚ēź‚ēź‚ēź‚ēź‚ēź‚ēź‚ēź‚ēź‚ēź‚ēź‚ēź‚ēź‚ēź‚ēź‚ēź‚ēź‚ēź‚ēź‚ēź‚ēź‚ēź‚ēź‚ēź‚ēź‚ēź‚ēź‚ēź‚ēź
\part{Physics from \texorpdfstring{$K_4$}{K4}}
%‚ēź‚ēź‚ēź‚ēź‚ēź‚ēź‚ēź‚ēź‚ēź‚ēź‚ēź‚ēź‚ēź‚ēź‚ēź‚ēź‚ēź‚ēź‚ēź‚ēź‚ēź‚ēź‚ēź‚ēź‚ēź‚ēź‚ēź‚ēź‚ēź‚ēź‚ēź‚ēź‚ēź‚ēź‚ēź‚ēź‚ēź‚ēź‚ēź‚ēź‚ēź‚ēź‚ēź‚ēź‚ēź‚ēź‚ēź‚ēź‚ēź‚ēź‚ēź‚ēź‚ēź‚ēź‚ēź‚ēź‚ēź‚ēź‚ēź‚ēź‚ēź‚ēź‚ēź‚ēź‚ēź‚ēź‚ēź‚ēź‚ēź‚ēź‚ēź‚ēź‚ēź‚ēź‚ēź

The arithmetic, spectral theory, and measurement formalism built in the preceding chapters constitute the apparatus needed to translate $K_4$'s combinatorial invariants into numbers. This part performs that translation.

Nothing new is assumed. The vertex count, edge count, Euler characteristic, and Laplacian eigenvalues of $K_4$ are assembled into a verified computation record. Each entry is a definitional equality closed by the Agda type system. The numerical values that result---including the inverse fine-structure constant $\alpha^{-1} = 137$---are consequences of the structure derived in Parts I through IV, not inputs chosen to match them.

This is the methodological boundary that matters for the numerology question. The numerical match is not being used here as evidence for the structure. The structure has already been forced by the elimination chain. What appears in this part is the evaluated consequence of that survivor. The burden is therefore not pattern recognition but invariance under the chain of eliminations that left no adjustable parameter standing.

The reader should therefore keep two claims sharply apart. The theorem-level claim is internal to the formal system: given the derivation already established, these equalities reduce and these numbers compute. The interpretation-level claim is external: some of these computed values may be read as physically meaningful because they align with measured observables. The first claim is proved here. The second is discussed here, but it is not smuggled into the proof.

Whether these numbers illuminate the structure of physics or merely reproduce it is a question the record itself cannot settle. That is an interpretive burden, not a formal one. But the record itself is exact, machine-verified, and fully open to inspection.

%‚ēź‚ēź‚ēź‚ēź‚ēź‚ēź‚ēź‚ēź‚ēź‚ēź‚ēź‚ēź‚ēź‚ēź‚ēź‚ēź‚ēź‚ēź‚ēź‚ēź‚ēź‚ēź‚ēź‚ēź‚ēź‚ēź‚ēź‚ēź‚ēź‚ēź‚ēź‚ēź‚ēź‚ēź‚ēź‚ēź‚ēź‚ēź‚ēź‚ēź‚ēź‚ēź‚ēź‚ēź‚ēź‚ēź‚ēź‚ēź‚ēź‚ēź‚ēź‚ēź‚ēź‚ēź‚ēź‚ēź‚ēź‚ēź‚ēź‚ēź‚ēź‚ēź‚ēź‚ēź‚ēź‚ēź‚ēź‚ēź‚ēź‚ēź‚ēź‚ēź‚ēź‚ēź‚ēź
\chapter{Natural-Number Division Infrastructure}
\label{ch:natural-number-division}
%‚ēź‚ēź‚ēź‚ēź‚ēź‚ēź‚ēź‚ēź‚ēź‚ēź‚ēź‚ēź‚ēź‚ēź‚ēź‚ēź‚ēź‚ēź‚ēź‚ēź‚ēź‚ēź‚ēź‚ēź‚ēź‚ēź‚ēź‚ēź‚ēź‚ēź‚ēź‚ēź‚ēź‚ēź‚ēź‚ēź‚ēź‚ēź‚ēź‚ēź‚ēź‚ēź‚ēź‚ēź‚ēź‚ēź‚ēź‚ēź‚ēź‚ēź‚ēź‚ēź‚ēź‚ēź‚ēź‚ēź‚ēź‚ēź‚ēź‚ēź‚ēź‚ēź‚ēź‚ēź‚ēź‚ēź‚ēź‚ēź‚ēź‚ēź‚ēź‚ēź‚ēź‚ēź‚ēź

The combinatorial laws of $K_4$ require exact natural-number division, yet the safe base environment does not provide the needed machinery. This chapter therefore fixes the minimal arithmetic infrastructure that survives the safety constraints and still forces every later combinatorial count to remain mechanically decidable.

Two primitives are needed: a greatest common divisor that terminates on all inputs, and an integer division that recovers exact quotients when they exist.  Both are fuel-bounded---the recursion carries an explicit natural-number counter that decreases at each step---because the safe base environment provides no other termination discipline.  This is not a design choice; it is forced by the absence of well-founded recursion in the minimal base.  The fuel bound is always the sum of the two inputs, which is enough because each Euclidean subtraction step reduces that sum by at least one.

\textbf{Constraint:} Termination of the Euclidean algorithm on arbitrary naturals requires an explicit fuel counter in the safe base.

\textbf{Construction:} A three-argument \texttt{gcd-fuel} recurrence that subtracts the smaller input from the larger at each step, consuming one unit of fuel.  A \texttt{div-fuel} recurrence that counts how many times the divisor fits.

\textbf{Consequence:} Both primitives are total and structurally decreasing in fuel.  Every downstream combinatorial identity reduces to \texttt{refl} once the fuel evaluates.

\begin{code}
-- ¬ß Boolean comparison on ‚Ąē
_‚Č§‚Ąē_ : ‚Ąē ‚Üí ‚Ąē ‚Üí Bool
zero  ‚Č§‚Ąē _     = true
suc _ ‚Č§‚Ąē zero  = false
suc m ‚Č§‚Ąē suc n = m ‚Č§‚Ąē n

-- ¬ß Fuel-bounded GCD
gcd-fuel : ‚Ąē ‚Üí ‚Ąē ‚Üí ‚Ąē ‚Üí ‚Ąē
gcd-fuel zero    m _       = m
gcd-fuel (suc _) zero n    = n
gcd-fuel (suc _) m zero    = m
gcd-fuel (suc f) (suc m) (suc n) with (suc m) ‚Č§‚Ąē (suc n)
... | true  = gcd-fuel f (suc m) (n ‚ął m)
... | false = gcd-fuel f (m ‚ął n) (suc n)

-- ¬ß One-step exposure of gcd-fuel on positive inputs.
gcd-fuel-step : ‚Ąē ‚Üí ‚Ąē ‚Üí ‚Ąē ‚Üí Bool ‚Üí ‚Ąē
gcd-fuel-step f m n true = gcd-fuel f m (n ‚ął m)
gcd-fuel-step f m n false = gcd-fuel f (m ‚ął n) n

gcd-fuel-suc-suc : (f m n : ‚Ąē) ‚Üí gcd-fuel (suc f) (suc m) (suc n) ‚Č° gcd-fuel-step f (suc m) (suc n) (m ‚Č§‚Ąē n)
gcd-fuel-suc-suc f m n with m ‚Č§‚Ąē n
... | true = refl
... | false = refl

-- ¬ß GCD with canonical fuel
gcd : ‚Ąē ‚Üí ‚Ąē ‚Üí ‚Ąē
gcd m n = gcd-fuel (m + n) m n
\end{code}

Once the GCD engine is in place, the only remaining primitive is exact division.  A rational quotient $n/d$ must be decomposable into a whole-number part whenever $d$ divides $n$; otherwise the correction fractions in the physics chapter would remain opaque to computation.  Division is again fuel-bounded, counting how many times the divisor can be subtracted before the remainder drops below it.

\begin{code}
-- ¬ß Successor coercion to ‚Ąē‚Āļ
sucTo‚Ąē‚Āļ : ‚Ąē ‚Üí ‚Ąē‚Āļ
sucTo‚Ąē‚Āļ zero = one‚Āļ
sucTo‚Ąē‚Āļ (suc n) = suc‚Āļ (sucTo‚Ąē‚Āļ n)

-- ¬ß General ‚Ąē to ‚Ąē‚Āļ coercion
‚Ąē-to-‚Ąē‚Āļ : ‚Ąē ‚Üí ‚Ąē‚Āļ
‚Ąē-to-‚Ąē‚Āļ = mk‚Ąē‚Āļ

-- ¬ß Fuel-bounded integer division
div-fuel : ‚Ąē ‚Üí ‚Ąē ‚Üí ‚Ąē‚Āļ ‚Üí ‚Ąē
div-fuel zero    _       _ = zero
div-fuel (suc f) n d with ‚Āļto‚Ąē d ‚Č§‚Ąē n
... | true  = suc (div-fuel f (n ‚ął ‚Āļto‚Ąē d) d)
... | false = zero

-- ¬ß One-step exposure of div-fuel on positive fuel.
div-fuel-step : ‚Ąē ‚Üí ‚Ąē ‚Üí ‚Ąē‚Āļ ‚Üí Bool ‚Üí ‚Ąē
div-fuel-step f n d true = suc (div-fuel f (n ‚ął ‚Āļto‚Ąē d) d)
div-fuel-step f n d false = zero

div-fuel-suc : (f n : ‚Ąē) ‚Üí (d : ‚Ąē‚Āļ) ‚Üí div-fuel (suc f) n d ‚Č° div-fuel-step f n d (‚Āļto‚Ąē d ‚Č§‚Ąē n)
div-fuel-suc f n d with ‚Āļto‚Ąē d ‚Č§‚Ąē n
... | true = refl
... | false = refl

-- ¬ß Division by ‚Ąē‚Āļ
_div_ : ‚Ąē ‚Üí ‚Ąē‚Āļ ‚Üí ‚Ąē
n div d = div-fuel n n d

-- ¬ß Division by ‚Ąē (zero-safe)
_div‚Ąē_ : ‚Ąē ‚Üí ‚Ąē ‚Üí ‚Ąē
_ div‚Ąē zero = zero
n div‚Ąē (suc d) = n div (sucTo‚Ąē‚Āļ d)
\end{code}

\section{Canonical Quotient Data and Reduced Witnesses}
\label{sec:rational-measurement:canonical-quotient-data}

The quotient layer cannot remain hidden behind decimal notation or behind the raw setoid. If a rational observable entered the theory with its numerator and denominator left implicit, the same presentation-noise that earlier chapters eliminated would quietly return at the arithmetic boundary. The rational layer must therefore expose its own surviving ledger.

Part~IV already forced admissible observables to collapse to canonical base content. That pressure now reaches the quotient level. Every admissible rational observable must carry pointwise quotient data visible at the base, and any reduced witness that survives must be unique. The gcd-and-division infrastructure built above now extracts a reduced witness for every quotient class---including negative and zero classes---so the canonical normal form is globally available.  What follows is therefore the full quotient-presentation layer: from the raw setoid lift through the divisibility engine to the final signed reduced form that every admissible rational observable is forced to carry.

\textbf{Constraint:} A rational observable may not hide its quotient structure behind the equality relation. If numerator and denominator remained observationally ambiguous, admissibility would collapse back into representation choice.

\textbf{Construction:} Lift rational values into $\mathit{Set}_\omega$, package their explicit quotient data, and then lift that presentation pointwise along admissible observables. Reduced witnesses are defined as positive numerator/denominator presentations together with coprimality, and uniqueness is forced by the quotient fields themselves. The same mechanism now also extracts a canonical reduction candidate from the raw fields by dividing numerator and denominator by their computed gcd.

\textbf{Consequence:} Every rational class now admits a signed reduced normal form---proved via the \texttt{canonical-reduction-form} theorem that splits on sign, reduces the magnitude through the gcd engine, and lifts the result through negation compatibility.  The irreducible nonnegative sector, the zero class, and the negative classes are all closed.  At the observable level, quotient fields are globally visible and any reduced witness is unique whenever it exists.

\subsection{Quotient presentation and field uniqueness}

Before reduction can begin, the rational type must expose its quotient fields as inspectable data.  If a rational $q = n/d$ entered the theory opaquely---with the constructor fields hidden behind the setoid relation---then later admissibility tests would have nothing concrete to classify.  The lift into $\mathit{Set}_\omega$ makes rational observables composable with the drift framework, and the presentation record simply reads off the constructor fields that already exist.  The uniqueness lemma then eliminates any residual doubt: these fields are not a choice of presentation but the only surviving data.

\begin{code}
-- ¬ß Lift rationals into SetŌČ so admissible observables can target them.
record ‚ĄöŌČ : SetŌČ where
  constructor wrap‚Ąö
  field
    lower‚Ąö : ‚Ąö

open ‚ĄöŌČ public

AdmissibleRationalObservable : SetŌČ
AdmissibleRationalObservable = AdmissibleObservable ‚ĄöŌČ

-- ¬ß Quotient presentation of a rational value.
record RationalPresentation (q : ‚Ąö) : Set where
  field
    pres-num : ‚Ą§
    pres-den : ‚Ąē‚Āļ
    presents-q : q ‚Č° pres-num / pres-den

-- ¬ß The constructor fields are already the canonical quotient data.
canonical-rational-presentation : (q : ‚Ąö) ‚Üí RationalPresentation q
canonical-rational-presentation (n / d) = record
  { pres-num = n
  ; pres-den = d
  ; presents-q = refl
  }

-- ¬ß Quotient fields are unique once a rational value is fixed.
rational-fields-unique :
  (q : ‚Ąö) ‚Üí
  (n‚āĀ n‚āā : ‚Ą§) ‚Üí
  (d‚āĀ d‚āā : ‚Ąē‚Āļ) ‚Üí
  q ‚Č° (n‚āĀ / d‚āĀ) ‚Üí
  q ‚Č° (n‚āā / d‚āā) ‚Üí
  (n‚āĀ ‚Č° n‚āā) √ó (d‚āĀ ‚Č° d‚āā)
rational-fields-unique (n / d) n‚āĀ n‚āā d‚āĀ d‚āā refl refl = refl , refl
\end{code}

\subsection{Reduced witnesses and irreducibility}

Once the quotient fields are visible and unique, the next question is sharper: when is a rational already in reduced form?  The answer is forced by coprimality.  A quotient $n/d$ is \emph{irreducible} when $\gcd(|n|, d) = 1$---when the numerator magnitude and the denominator share no factor beyond one.

A \emph{reduced rational witness} makes this concrete.  It records a natural numerator, a positive denominator, a proof that the rational equals this pair (up to definitional equality), and a proof that the pair is coprime.  The uniqueness of reduced witnesses follows immediately from the uniqueness of quotient fields: if two reduced witnesses both present the same rational, their numerators and denominators must agree, because the fields are read from the same constructor.

This subsection also establishes the injectivity of the natural embedding $\mathbb{N} \hookrightarrow \mathbb{Z}$, which is needed to transport the numerator equality from the integer level down to the natural level.

\begin{code}
-- ¬ß The ‚Ąē-embedding into ‚Ą§ is injective.
from‚Ąē‚Ą§-injective : {m n : ‚Ąē} ‚Üí from‚Ąē‚Ą§ m ‚Č° from‚Ąē‚Ą§ n ‚Üí m ‚Č° n
from‚Ąē‚Ą§-injective {zero} {zero} refl = refl
from‚Ąē‚Ą§-injective {zero} {suc n} ()
from‚Ąē‚Ą§-injective {suc m} {zero} ()
from‚Ąē‚Ą§-injective {suc m} {suc n} refl = refl

-- ¬ß Irreducibility for rational quotients: numerator magnitude and denominator are coprime.
IrreducibleQuotient : ‚Ąö ‚Üí Set
IrreducibleQuotient q = gcd (mag‚Ą§ (num q)) (‚Āļto‚Ąē (den q)) ‚Č° 1

-- ¬ß Reduced witness for a rational quotient with nonnegative numerator.
record ReducedRationalWitness (q : ‚Ąö) : Set where
  field
    red-num : ‚Ąē
    red-den : ‚Ąē‚Āļ
    red-value : q ‚Č° from‚Ąē‚Ą§ red-num / red-den
    red-coprime : gcd red-num (‚Āļto‚Ąē red-den) ‚Č° 1

-- ¬ß Reduced witnesses are unique whenever they exist.
reduced-rational-witness-unique :
  {q : ‚Ąö} ‚Üí
  (r‚āĀ r‚āā : ReducedRationalWitness q) ‚Üí
  ReducedRationalWitness.red-num r‚āĀ ‚Č° ReducedRationalWitness.red-num r‚āā
  √ó ReducedRationalWitness.red-den r‚āĀ ‚Č° ReducedRationalWitness.red-den r‚āā
reduced-rational-witness-unique {q} r‚āĀ r‚āā =
  let
    pair : from‚Ąē‚Ą§ (ReducedRationalWitness.red-num r‚āĀ)
         ‚Č° from‚Ąē‚Ą§ (ReducedRationalWitness.red-num r‚āā)
         √ó (ReducedRationalWitness.red-den r‚āĀ ‚Č° ReducedRationalWitness.red-den r‚āā)
    pair =
      rational-fields-unique q
        (from‚Ąē‚Ą§ (ReducedRationalWitness.red-num r‚āĀ))
        (from‚Ąē‚Ą§ (ReducedRationalWitness.red-num r‚āā))
        (ReducedRationalWitness.red-den r‚āĀ)
        (ReducedRationalWitness.red-den r‚āā)
        (ReducedRationalWitness.red-value r‚āĀ)
        (ReducedRationalWitness.red-value r‚āā)
  in
  from‚Ąē‚Ą§-injective (fst pair) , snd pair
\end{code}

\subsection{Arithmetic lemmas and exact division}

The reduction pipeline requires a corridor of small arithmetic facts---about subtraction, comparison reflection, packaging, and exact division---that would otherwise interrupt the flow of the main argument if proved inline.  None of these is surprising.  Each is a single-step verification that the safe base arithmetic behaves as expected under successor encoding.

The culmination of this corridor is the exact-division theorem: when $d$ divides $n$, the fuel-bounded division recovers the quotient exactly.  The proof proceeds by induction on the quotient $q$, showing that each subtraction step peels off one copy of $d$ and that the accumulated fuel suffices for the recursion.  This is the bridge between the raw division recurrence and the structured divisibility theory that follows.

\begin{code}
-- ¬ß Magnitude of the ‚Ąē-embedding is the original natural number.
mag‚Ą§-from‚Ąē‚Ą§ : (n : ‚Ąē) ‚Üí mag‚Ą§ (from‚Ąē‚Ą§ n) ‚Č° n
mag‚Ą§-from‚Ąē‚Ą§ zero = refl
mag‚Ą§-from‚Ąē‚Ą§ (suc n) = refl

-- ¬ß Subtracting zero on the right does nothing.
‚ął-zero-right : (n : ‚Ąē) ‚Üí n ‚ął zero ‚Č° n
‚ął-zero-right zero = refl
‚ął-zero-right (suc n) = refl

-- ¬ß Subtracting one from a successor recovers the predecessor.
‚ął-one-suc : (n : ‚Ąē) ‚Üí suc n ‚ął 1 ‚Č° n
‚ął-one-suc n = ‚ął-zero-right n

-- ¬ß A left summand cancels against subtraction from the whole sum.
+‚Ąē-‚ął-cancel-left : (m n : ‚Ąē) ‚Üí (m +‚Ąē n) ‚ął m ‚Č° n
+‚Ąē-‚ął-cancel-left zero n = ‚ął-zero-right n
+‚Ąē-‚ął-cancel-left (suc m) n = +‚Ąē-‚ął-cancel-left m n

-- ¬ß Every natural is ‚Č§‚Ąē its own right extension.
‚Č§‚Ąē-self-+‚Ąē-right : (m n : ‚Ąē) ‚Üí m ‚Č§‚Ąē (m +‚Ąē n) ‚Č° true
‚Č§‚Ąē-self-+‚Ąē-right zero n = refl
‚Č§‚Ąē-self-+‚Ąē-right (suc m) n = ‚Č§‚Ąē-self-+‚Ąē-right m n

-- ¬ß Boolean ‚Č§‚Ąē reflects propositional ‚Č§.
‚Č§‚Ąē-true‚Üí‚Č§ : {m n : ‚Ąē} ‚Üí m ‚Č§‚Ąē n ‚Č° true ‚Üí m ‚Č§ n
‚Č§‚Ąē-true‚Üí‚Č§ {zero} {n} _ = z‚Č§n
‚Č§‚Ąē-true‚Üí‚Č§ {suc m} {zero} ()
‚Č§‚Ąē-true‚Üí‚Č§ {suc m} {suc n} eq = s‚Č§s (‚Č§‚Ąē-true‚Üí‚Č§ eq)

-- ¬ß Boolean failure of ‚Č§‚Ąē forces the reverse propositional order.
‚Č§‚Ąē-false‚Üí‚Č§ : {m n : ‚Ąē} ‚Üí m ‚Č§‚Ąē n ‚Č° false ‚Üí n ‚Č§ m
‚Č§‚Ąē-false‚Üí‚Č§ {zero} {n} ()
‚Č§‚Ąē-false‚Üí‚Č§ {suc m} {zero} _ = z‚Č§n
‚Č§‚Ąē-false‚Üí‚Č§ {suc m} {suc n} eq = s‚Č§s (‚Č§‚Ąē-false‚Üí‚Č§ eq)

-- ¬ß Ordered subtraction splits the larger number into base plus remainder.
‚Č§‚Üí+‚Ąē-‚ął-split : {m n : ‚Ąē} ‚Üí m ‚Č§ n ‚Üí m +‚Ąē (n ‚ął m) ‚Č° n
‚Č§‚Üí+‚Ąē-‚ął-split {zero} {n} z‚Č§n = ‚ął-zero-right n
‚Č§‚Üí+‚Ąē-‚ął-split {suc m} {suc n} (s‚Č§s p) = cong suc (‚Č§‚Üí+‚Ąē-‚ął-split p)

-- ¬ß Packing the predecessor of a positive natural recovers the same ‚Ąē‚Āļ.
mk‚Ąē‚Āļ-‚Āļto‚Ąē-minus-one : (d : ‚Ąē‚Āļ) ‚Üí mk‚Ąē‚Āļ (‚Āļto‚Ąē d ‚ął 1) ‚Č° d
mk‚Ąē‚Āļ-‚Āļto‚Ąē-minus-one (mk‚Ąē‚Āļ p) = cong mk‚Ąē‚Āļ (‚ął-zero-right p)

-- ¬ß Division by one leaves naturals unchanged.
div-one‚Āļ : (n : ‚Ąē) ‚Üí n div one‚Āļ ‚Č° n
div-one‚Āļ zero = refl
div-one‚Āļ (suc n) =
  cong suc
    (trans
      (cong (őĽ t ‚Üí div-fuel n t one‚Āļ) (‚ął-zero-right n))
      (div-one‚Āļ n))

\end{code}

At this point the corridor is narrow enough that the exact-division theorem can fire cleanly.  The recurrence has no freedom left: each successful subtraction removes one copy of the divisor, and the surplus fuel merely witnesses that the recursion cannot stall too early.

\begin{code}

-- ¬ß Division recovers the quotient on exact multiples, even with extra fuel.
div-fuel-exact-multiple :
  (q slack : ‚Ąē) ‚Üí (d : ‚Ąē‚Āļ) ‚Üí
  div-fuel ((q *‚Ąē ‚Āļto‚Ąē d) +‚Ąē slack) (q *‚Ąē ‚Āļto‚Ąē d) d ‚Č° q
div-fuel-exact-multiple zero zero (mk‚Ąē‚Āļ p) = refl
div-fuel-exact-multiple zero (suc slack) (mk‚Ąē‚Āļ p) = refl
div-fuel-exact-multiple (suc q) slack (mk‚Ąē‚Āļ p) =
  let
    qk : ‚Ąē
    qk = q *‚Ąē suc p

    fuelShape : ((suc q *‚Ąē suc p) +‚Ąē slack) ‚Č° suc (p +‚Ąē (qk +‚Ąē slack))
    fuelShape = cong suc (+‚Ąē-assoc p qk slack)

    cmpEq : suc p ‚Č§‚Ąē (suc q *‚Ąē suc p) ‚Č° true
    cmpEq = ‚Č§‚Ąē-self-+‚Ąē-right p qk

    subEq : (suc q *‚Ąē suc p) ‚ął suc p ‚Č° qk
    subEq = +‚Ąē-‚ął-cancel-left (suc p) qk

    fuelEq : p +‚Ąē (qk +‚Ąē slack) ‚Č° qk +‚Ąē (p +‚Ąē slack)
    fuelEq =
      trans
        (sym (+‚Ąē-assoc p qk slack))
        (trans
          (cong (őĽ t ‚Üí t +‚Ąē slack) (+‚Ąē-comm p qk))
          (+‚Ąē-assoc qk p slack))
  in
  trans
    (cong (őĽ t ‚Üí div-fuel t (suc q *‚Ąē suc p) (mk‚Ąē‚Āļ p)) fuelShape)
    (trans
      (div-fuel-suc (p +‚Ąē (qk +‚Ąē slack)) (suc q *‚Ąē suc p) (mk‚Ąē‚Āļ p))
      (trans
        (cong (div-fuel-step (p +‚Ąē (qk +‚Ąē slack)) (suc q *‚Ąē suc p) (mk‚Ąē‚Āļ p)) cmpEq)
        (cong suc
          (trans
            (cong (őĽ t ‚Üí div-fuel (p +‚Ąē (qk +‚Ąē slack)) t (mk‚Ąē‚Āļ p)) subEq)
            (trans
              (cong (őĽ t ‚Üí div-fuel t qk (mk‚Ąē‚Āļ p)) fuelEq)
              (div-fuel-exact-multiple q (p +‚Ąē slack) (mk‚Ąē‚Āļ p)))))))

-- ¬ß Division by a positive factor recovers the quotient on exact multiples.
div-exact-multiple : (q : ‚Ąē) ‚Üí (d : ‚Ąē‚Āļ) ‚Üí (q *‚Ąē ‚Āļto‚Ąē d) div d ‚Č° q
div-exact-multiple q d =
  trans
    (cong (őĽ t ‚Üí div-fuel t (q *‚Ąē ‚Āļto‚Ąē d) d) (sym (+‚Ąē-zero-right (q *‚Ąē ‚Āļto‚Ąē d))))
    (div-fuel-exact-multiple q zero d)
\end{code}

\subsection{Positive divisibility theory}

Before the gcd can serve the reduction pipeline, a divisibility language is needed.  The raw statement ``$d$ divides $n$'' must be a constructive record carrying the explicit quotient and the factorization proof, because later proofs need to extract that quotient for exact division.  This is not a library convenience; it is forced by the absence of classical existentials in the safe base.

The \texttt{Divides‚Āļ} record captures exactly this: a positive divisor~$d$, a quotient, and a proof that $n = q \cdot d$.  From this single record, the section derives closure under addition, exact quotient extraction, quotient positivity, multiplication lift, right cancellation, order reflection, and subtraction---the complete divisibility algebra that the gcd engine needs.

\textbf{Constraint:} The gcd proofs require divisibility witnesses that carry computational content (the quotient), not merely propositional existence.

\textbf{Construction:} A record \texttt{Divides‚Āļ d n} with field \texttt{quotient‚Āļ} and factorization proof.  Closure under $+$, $-$, multiplication, and cancellation.

\textbf{Consequence:} Every later divisibility argument reduces to record operations on \texttt{Divides‚Āļ}.  No ad hoc arithmetic witness is needed downstream.

\begin{code}
-- ¬ß Positive divisibility packages an explicit quotient witness.
record Divides‚Āļ (d : ‚Ąē‚Āļ) (n : ‚Ąē) : Set where
  field
    quotient‚Āļ : ‚Ąē
    factor‚Āļ : n ‚Č° quotient‚Āļ *‚Ąē ‚Āļto‚Ąē d

open Divides‚Āļ public

-- ¬ß Every exact multiple yields a positive divisibility witness.
divides‚Āļ-multiple : (q : ‚Ąē) ‚Üí (d : ‚Ąē‚Āļ) ‚Üí Divides‚Āļ d (q *‚Ąē ‚Āļto‚Ąē d)
divides‚Āļ-multiple q d = record
  { quotient‚Āļ = q
  ; factor‚Āļ = refl
  }

-- ¬ß The divisor divides itself.
divides‚Āļ-self : (d : ‚Ąē‚Āļ) ‚Üí Divides‚Āļ d (‚Āļto‚Ąē d)
divides‚Āļ-self d = record
  { quotient‚Āļ = oneNat
  ; factor‚Āļ = sym (*‚Ąē-one-left (‚Āļto‚Ąē d))
  }

-- ¬ß Zero is divisible by every positive natural.
divides‚Āļ-zero : (d : ‚Ąē‚Āļ) ‚Üí Divides‚Āļ d zero
divides‚Āļ-zero d = record
  { quotient‚Āļ = zero
  ; factor‚Āļ = refl
  }

-- ¬ß Positive divisibility is closed under addition.
divides‚Āļ-+‚Ąē : {d : ‚Ąē‚Āļ} {m n : ‚Ąē} ‚Üí Divides‚Āļ d m ‚Üí Divides‚Āļ d n ‚Üí Divides‚Āļ d (m +‚Ąē n)
divides‚Āļ-+‚Ąē {d} {m} {n} dm dn = record
  { quotient‚Āļ = quotient‚Āļ dm +‚Ąē quotient‚Āļ dn
  ; factor‚Āļ =
      trans
        (cong‚āā _+‚Ąē_ (factor‚Āļ dm) (factor‚Āļ dn))
        (sym (*‚Ąē-distrib-left-+‚Ąē (quotient‚Āļ dm) (quotient‚Āļ dn) (‚Āļto‚Ąē d)))
  }

-- ¬ß Exact division extracts the stored quotient from a positive divisibility witness.
divides‚Āļ‚Üídiv : {d : ‚Ąē‚Āļ} {n : ‚Ąē} ‚Üí (dvd : Divides‚Āļ d n) ‚Üí n div d ‚Č° quotient‚Āļ dvd
divides‚Āļ‚Üídiv {d} {n} dvd =
  trans
    (cong (őĽ t ‚Üí t div d) (factor‚Āļ dvd))
    (div-exact-multiple (quotient‚Āļ dvd) d)

-- ¬ß A positive divisible natural has a positive stored quotient.
divides‚Āļ-positive-quotient : {d : ‚Ąē‚Āļ} {n : ‚Ąē} ‚Üí (dvd : Divides‚Āļ d (suc n)) ‚Üí ő£ ‚Ąē (őĽ k ‚Üí quotient‚Āļ dvd ‚Č° suc k)
divides‚Āļ-positive-quotient {d} {n} dvd with quotient‚Āļ dvd | factor‚Āļ dvd
... | zero | ()
... | suc k | _ = k , refl

-- ¬ß Built-in addition and manuscript addition coincide on naturals.
+-to-+‚Ąē : (m n : ‚Ąē) ‚Üí (m + n) ‚Č° (m +‚Ąē n)
+-to-+‚Ąē zero n = refl
+-to-+‚Ąē (suc m) n = cong suc (+-to-+‚Ąē m n)

-- ¬ß The ‚Ąē-view of ‚Ąē‚Āļ multiplication is ordinary natural multiplication.
‚Āļto‚Ąē-*‚Āļ : (x y : ‚Ąē‚Āļ) ‚Üí ‚Āļto‚Ąē (x *‚Āļ y) ‚Č° ‚Āļto‚Ąē x *‚Ąē ‚Āļto‚Ąē y
‚Āļto‚Ąē-*‚Āļ (mk‚Ąē‚Āļ a) (mk‚Ąē‚Āļ b) = cong suc (+‚Ąē-comm (a *‚Ąē suc b) b)

-- ¬ß Divisibility lifts through multiplication by a positive factor on the right.
divides‚Āļ-mul-right : {e : ‚Ąē‚Āļ} {n : ‚Ąē} ‚Üí Divides‚Āļ e n ‚Üí (d : ‚Ąē‚Āļ) ‚Üí Divides‚Āļ (e *‚Āļ d) (n *‚Ąē ‚Āļto‚Ąē d)
divides‚Āļ-mul-right {e} {n} dvd d = record
  { quotient‚Āļ = quotient‚Āļ dvd
  ; factor‚Āļ =
      trans
        (cong (őĽ t ‚Üí t *‚Ąē ‚Āļto‚Ąē d) (factor‚Āļ dvd))
        (trans
          (*‚Ąē-assoc (quotient‚Āļ dvd) (‚Āļto‚Ąē e) (‚Āļto‚Ąē d))
          (cong (őĽ t ‚Üí quotient‚Āļ dvd *‚Ąē t) (sym (‚Āļto‚Ąē-*‚Āļ e d))))
  }

-- ¬ß Equality after multiplying by the same positive factor cancels on the right.
*‚Ąē-cancel ≥-suc : {a b : ‚Ąē} ‚Üí (k : ‚Ąē) ‚Üí a *‚Ąē suc k ‚Č° b *‚Ąē suc k ‚Üí a ‚Č° b
*‚Ąē-cancel ≥-suc {a} {b} k eq =
  ‚Č§-antisym
    (‚Č§-*‚Ąē-cancel ≥-suc k (subst (őĽ t ‚Üí (a *‚Ąē suc k) ‚Č§ t) eq (‚Č§-refl (a *‚Ąē suc k))))
    (‚Č§-*‚Ąē-cancel ≥-suc k (subst (őĽ t ‚Üí (b *‚Ąē suc k) ‚Č§ t) (sym eq) (‚Č§-refl (b *‚Ąē suc k))))

-- ¬ß A product of two positive naturals equals one only in the trivial case.
positive-product-one : (a b : ‚Ąē) ‚Üí suc a *‚Ąē suc b ‚Č° oneNat ‚Üí (a ‚Č° zero) √ó (b ‚Č° zero)
positive-product-one a zero eq =
  let
    aEq : suc a ‚Č° oneNat
    aEq = trans (sym (*‚Ąē-one-right (suc a))) eq
  in
  suc-injective aEq , refl
positive-product-one a (suc b) ()

-- ¬ß If a product divisor of d also divides d, the extra factor must be one.
divides‚Āļ-product-factor-left-one : (e d : ‚Ąē‚Āļ) ‚Üí Divides‚Āļ (e *‚Āļ d) (‚Āļto‚Ąē d) ‚Üí e ‚Č° one‚Āļ
divides‚Āļ-product-factor-left-one e d dvd =
  let
    qPos : ő£ ‚Ąē (őĽ k ‚Üí quotient‚Āļ dvd ‚Č° suc k)
    qPos = divides‚Āļ-positive-quotient dvd

    k : ‚Ąē
    k = fst qPos

    qEq : quotient‚Āļ dvd ‚Č° suc k
    qEq = snd qPos

    factored : ‚Āļto‚Ąē d ‚Č° ((suc k *‚Ąē ‚Āļto‚Ąē e) *‚Ąē ‚Āļto‚Ąē d)
    factored =
      trans
        (factor‚Āļ dvd)
        (trans
          (cong (őĽ t ‚Üí t *‚Ąē ‚Āļto‚Ąē (e *‚Āļ d)) qEq)
          (trans
            (cong (őĽ t ‚Üí suc k *‚Ąē t) (‚Āļto‚Ąē-*‚Āļ e d))
            (sym (*‚Ąē-assoc (suc k) (‚Āļto‚Ąē e) (‚Āļto‚Ąē d)))))

    prodOne : suc k *‚Ąē ‚Āļto‚Ąē e ‚Č° oneNat
    prodOne =
      *‚Ąē-cancel ≥-suc
        (pred d)
        (trans
          (sym factored)
          (sym (*‚Ąē-one-left (‚Āļto‚Ąē d))))

    ePredZero : pred e ‚Č° zero
    ePredZero = snd (positive-product-one k (pred e) prodOne)
  in
  cong mk‚Ąē‚Āļ ePredZero
\end{code}

The factor constraints above show that divisibility by a product forces each factor to be trivial when the product divides a single copy of one factor. This is the positive-integer analogue of the familiar number-theoretic fact that if $ab \mid b$, then $a = 1$. With this in hand, the machinery can now compare quotients: if $d$ divides both $m$ and $n$ and $m \le n$, then the stored quotient for $m$ is at most the stored quotient for $n$.

\begin{code}
-- ¬ß Order on divisible multiples reflects to the stored quotients.
divides‚Āļ-quotient-‚Č§ :
  {d : ‚Ąē‚Āļ} {m n : ‚Ąē} ‚Üí
  m ‚Č§ n ‚Üí
  (dm : Divides‚Āļ d m) ‚Üí
  (dn : Divides‚Āļ d n) ‚Üí
  Divides‚Āļ.quotient‚Āļ dm ‚Č§ Divides‚Āļ.quotient‚Āļ dn
divides‚Āļ-quotient-‚Č§ {mk‚Ąē‚Āļ p} {m} {n} m‚Č§n dm dn =
  let
    step‚āĀ : m ‚Č§ (Divides‚Āļ.quotient‚Āļ dn *‚Ąē (suc p))
    step‚āĀ = subst (őĽ t ‚Üí m ‚Č§ t) (Divides‚Āļ.factor‚Āļ dn) m‚Č§n

    step‚āā : (Divides‚Āļ.quotient‚Āļ dm *‚Ąē (suc p)) ‚Č§ (Divides‚Āļ.quotient‚Āļ dn *‚Ąē (suc p))
    step‚āā = subst (őĽ t ‚Üí t ‚Č§ (Divides‚Āļ.quotient‚Āļ dn *‚Ąē (suc p))) (Divides‚Āļ.factor‚Āļ dm) step‚āĀ
  in
  ‚Č§-*‚Ąē-cancel ≥-suc p step‚āā

-- ¬ß Positive divisibility survives subtraction of a smaller divisible multiple.
divides‚Āļ-‚ął-‚Č§ :
  {d : ‚Ąē‚Āļ} {m n : ‚Ąē} ‚Üí
  m ‚Č§ n ‚Üí
  Divides‚Āļ d m ‚Üí
  Divides‚Āļ d n ‚Üí
  Divides‚Āļ d (n ‚ął m)
divides‚Āļ-‚ął-‚Č§ {d} {m} {n} m‚Č§n dm dn = record
  { quotient‚Āļ = Divides‚Āļ.quotient‚Āļ dn ‚ął Divides‚Āļ.quotient‚Āļ dm
  ; factor‚Āļ = factor
  }
  where
    q‚Č§ : Divides‚Āļ.quotient‚Āļ dm ‚Č§ Divides‚Āļ.quotient‚Āļ dn
    q‚Č§ = divides‚Āļ-quotient-‚Č§ m‚Č§n dm dn

    qSplit : Divides‚Āļ.quotient‚Āļ dm +‚Ąē (Divides‚Āļ.quotient‚Āļ dn ‚ął Divides‚Āļ.quotient‚Āļ dm) ‚Č° Divides‚Āļ.quotient‚Āļ dn
    qSplit = ‚Č§‚Üí+‚Ąē-‚ął-split q‚Č§

    nAsSum : n ‚Č° m +‚Ąē ((Divides‚Āļ.quotient‚Āļ dn ‚ął Divides‚Āļ.quotient‚Āļ dm) *‚Ąē ‚Āļto‚Ąē d)
    nAsSum =
      trans
        (Divides‚Āļ.factor‚Āļ dn)
        (trans
          (sym (cong (őĽ t ‚Üí t *‚Ąē ‚Āļto‚Ąē d) qSplit))
          (trans
            (*‚Ąē-distrib-left-+‚Ąē (Divides‚Āļ.quotient‚Āļ dm) (Divides‚Āļ.quotient‚Āļ dn ‚ął Divides‚Āļ.quotient‚Āļ dm) (‚Āļto‚Ąē d))
            (cong (őĽ t ‚Üí t +‚Ąē ((Divides‚Āļ.quotient‚Āļ dn ‚ął Divides‚Āļ.quotient‚Āļ dm) *‚Ąē ‚Āļto‚Ąē d)) (sym (Divides‚Āļ.factor‚Āļ dm)))))

    factor : n ‚ął m ‚Č° (Divides‚Āļ.quotient‚Āļ dn ‚ął Divides‚Āļ.quotient‚Āļ dm) *‚Ąē ‚Āļto‚Ąē d
    factor = subst (őĽ t ‚Üí t ‚ął m ‚Č° (Divides‚Āļ.quotient‚Āļ dn ‚ął Divides‚Āļ.quotient‚Āļ dm) *‚Ąē ‚Āļto‚Ąē d) (sym nAsSum)
      (+‚Ąē-‚ął-cancel-left m ((Divides‚Āļ.quotient‚Āļ dn ‚ął Divides‚Āļ.quotient‚Āļ dm) *‚Ąē ‚Āļto‚Ąē d))

-- ¬ß Adding back a divisible remainder recovers divisibility of the larger number.
divides‚Āļ-restore-‚ął :
  {d : ‚Ąē‚Āļ} {m n : ‚Ąē} ‚Üí
  m ‚Č§ n ‚Üí
  Divides‚Āļ d m ‚Üí
  Divides‚Āļ d (n ‚ął m) ‚Üí
  Divides‚Āļ d n
divides‚Āļ-restore-‚ął {d} {m} {n} m‚Č§n dm dnm =
  subst
    (Divides‚Āļ d)
    (‚Č§‚Üí+‚Ąē-‚ął-split m‚Č§n)
    (divides‚Āļ-+‚Ąē dm dnm)

-- ¬ß The ‚Ąē-view of sucTo‚Ąē‚Āļ is the expected successor.
‚Āļto‚Ąē-sucTo‚Ąē‚Āļ : (n : ‚Ąē) ‚Üí ‚Āļto‚Ąē (sucTo‚Ąē‚Āļ n) ‚Č° suc n
‚Āļto‚Ąē-sucTo‚Ąē‚Āļ zero = refl
‚Āļto‚Ąē-sucTo‚Ąē‚Āļ (suc n) = cong suc (‚Āļto‚Ąē-sucTo‚Ąē‚Āļ n)

-- ¬ß The successor-coded positive natural divides its own ‚Ąē-view.
divides‚Āļ-suc-self : (n : ‚Ąē) ‚Üí Divides‚Āļ (sucTo‚Ąē‚Āļ n) (suc n)
divides‚Āļ-suc-self n =
  subst
    (Divides‚Āļ (sucTo‚Ąē‚Āļ n))
    (‚Āļto‚Ąē-sucTo‚Ąē‚Āļ n)
    (divides‚Āļ-self (sucTo‚Ąē‚Āļ n))

-- ¬ß Zero/zero is a stable fixed point of gcd-fuel.
gcd-fuel-zero-zero : (f : ‚Ąē) ‚Üí gcd-fuel f zero zero ‚Č° zero
gcd-fuel-zero-zero zero = refl
gcd-fuel-zero-zero (suc f) = refl

-- ¬ß Positive fuel with a zero left input returns the right input.
gcd-fuel-zero-left-suc : (f n : ‚Ąē) ‚Üí gcd-fuel (suc f) zero (suc n) ‚Č° suc n
gcd-fuel-zero-left-suc f n = refl

-- ¬ß Positive fuel with a zero right input returns the left input.
gcd-fuel-zero-right-suc : (f m : ‚Ąē) ‚Üí gcd-fuel (suc f) (suc m) zero ‚Č° suc m
gcd-fuel-zero-right-suc f m = refl

-- ¬ß One Euclidean subtraction step preserves the total positive mass in the true branch.
euclid-sum-true : {m n : ‚Ąē} ‚Üí m ‚Č§ n ‚Üí (suc m) +‚Ąē (n ‚ął m) ‚Č° suc n
euclid-sum-true p = cong suc (‚Č§‚Üí+‚Ąē-‚ął-split p)

-- ¬ß One Euclidean subtraction step preserves the total positive mass in the false branch.
euclid-sum-false : {m n : ‚Ąē} ‚Üí n ‚Č§ m ‚Üí (m ‚ął n) +‚Ąē (suc n) ‚Č° suc m
euclid-sum-false {m} {n} p =
  trans
    (+‚Ąē-comm (m ‚ął n) (suc n))
    (cong suc (‚Č§‚Üí+‚Ąē-‚ął-split p))
\end{code}

\subsection{GCD engine: common-divisor preservation, output divisibility, and positivity}

The Euclidean gcd is now defined, but a bare recurrence carries no force.  Three properties must be forged before the gcd can serve the reduction pipeline:

\emph{Common-divisor preservation.}  Any positive integer that divides both inputs must still divide the output.  This is the ``upward'' direction: known divisors survive the algorithm.  The proof proceeds by mutual structural recursion, tracking divisibility through each subtraction step via the already established \texttt{divides‚Āļ-‚ął-‚Č§} lemma.

\emph{Output divisibility.}  Conversely, if the gcd output is positive, it divides both inputs.  This is the ``downward'' direction: the output itself is a common divisor.  The proof is again by structural recursion on fuel, restoring divisibility through the subtraction witness at each step.

\emph{Positivity.}  Whenever at least one input is positive, the gcd output is also positive.  This eliminates the degenerate case and ensures that the canonical reduction divisor is always well-formed as a positive natural.

Together, these three properties force the gcd to behave as the \emph{greatest} common divisor in the usual sense: it divides both inputs, and every other common divisor divides it.

\begin{code}
-- ¬ß Every natural is bounded by its own right extension.
‚Č§-self+‚Ąē : (m n : ‚Ąē) ‚Üí m ‚Č§ (m +‚Ąē n)
‚Č§-self+‚Ąē zero n = z‚Č§n
‚Č§-self+‚Ąē (suc m) n = s‚Č§s (‚Č§-self+‚Ąē m n)

mutual
  -- ¬ß One recursive Euclidean step preserves any common positive divisor.
  gcd-fuel-common-divisor-step :
    {d : ‚Ąē‚Āļ} ‚Üí
    (f m n : ‚Ąē) ‚Üí
    (b : Bool) ‚Üí
    m ‚Č§‚Ąē n ‚Č° b ‚Üí
    Divides‚Āļ d (suc m) ‚Üí
    Divides‚Āļ d (suc n) ‚Üí
    Divides‚Āļ d (gcd-fuel-step f (suc m) (suc n) b)
  gcd-fuel-common-divisor-step f m n true eq dm dn =
    gcd-fuel-common-divisor f (suc m) (n ‚ął m) dm
      (divides‚Āļ-‚ął-‚Č§ (s‚Č§s (‚Č§‚Ąē-true‚Üí‚Č§ eq)) dm dn)
  gcd-fuel-common-divisor-step f m n false eq dm dn =
    gcd-fuel-common-divisor f (m ‚ął n) (suc n)
