  law-ph-hierarchy :
    hierarchy-exponent ‚Č° vertexCountK4 * edgeCountK4 ‚ął eulerChar-computed
  law-ph-hierarchy = refl

  -- ¬ß Structural assembly: K4PhysRep from named invariants only
  derived-rep : K4PhysRep
  derived-rep = record
    { dim          = vertexCountK4
    ; n-spatial    = degree-K4
    ; n-temporal   = vertexCountK4 ‚ął degree-K4
    ; gauge-rank   = edgeCountK4
    ; face-count   = faceCountK4
    ; euler        = eulerChar-computed
    ; coupling-inv = alpha-inverse
    ; n-gen        = degree-K4
    ; spinor-dim   = clifford-dimension
    ; hierarchy    = hierarchy-exponent
    ; auto-count   = vertexCountK4 * degree-K4
                   * (degree-K4 ‚ął 1) * (degree-K4 ‚ął 2)
    ; bell-sq      = vertexCountK4 * eulerChar-computed
    ; bh-norm      = vertexCountK4
    ; baryon-num   = vertexCountK4 ‚ął degree-K4
    ; baryon-den   = edgeCountK4
    ; uv-cutoff    = vertexCountK4 ‚ął degree-K4
    ; min-loop     = vertexCountK4 ‚ął degree-K4
    ; anchor       = refl
    ; cg-deg       = refl
    ; cg-temporal  = refl
    ; cg-edges     = refl
    ; cg-faces     = refl
    ; cg-euler     = refl
    ; ph-spinor    = refl
    ; ph-auto      = refl
    ; ph-coupling  = refl
    ; ph-hierarchy = refl
    ; ph-gen       = refl
    ; ph-bell      = refl
    ; ph-bh        = refl
    ; ph-uv        = refl
    ; ph-loop      = refl
    ; ph-baryon-num = refl
    ; ph-baryon-den = refl
    }

  -- ¬ß derived-rep IS canonical-rep (definitional equality)
  derivation-is-canonical : derived-rep ‚Č° canonical-rep
  derivation-is-canonical = refl
\end{code}


%‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ
\section{Physical quantities as computable \texorpdfstring{$K_4$}{K4} invariants}
\label{sec:k4-representation:physical-quantities-computable-invariants}
%‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ

The remaining formal gap between graph invariants and the representation record is eliminated here. Each represented quantity is forced to be a computable function of $K_4$ data, so the internal identification is no longer rhetorical: the output of the graph computation is exactly the value carried by the representation chain. Whether a given represented quantity should also be read as a physical observable remains a separate interpretive question.

\subsection{Representation Completeness}
\label{sec:k4-representation:representation-completeness}

The record built below is already rigid once one enters it. But the stronger pressure comes from outside. If there were two admissible ways for physics to sit on the same $K_4$ invariant spine, then the constants would no longer be observables forced by structure; they would be artifacts of representational wording. That would collapse the claim at the final step. The graph would still survive, but the bridge into measurement would fork, and every numerical coincidence could be dismissed as notation. This fork must be shattered.

\textbf{Constraint:} Any admissible physical representation of $K_4$ must preserve the named invariant chain already fixed: vertex count, edge count, face count, Euler characteristic, spectral data, and the derived observables assembled from them. No representational wrapper may introduce additional degrees of freedom while claiming to describe the same structure.

\textbf{Construction:} The present chapter proves singletonhood for the internal record \texttt{K4PhysRep}.
The external completeness theorem is now provided below as \texttt{RepresentationComplete}: for any admissible $K_4$ representation~$r$, every observable field agrees with the canonical record.
\[
\mathrm{RepresentationComplete} :
\bigl(\text{admissible representation of }K_4\bigr)
\to
\bigl(\text{canonical }K4PhysRep\bigr),
\]
or equivalently, that every admissible representation is canonically isomorphic to the unique record assembled from the invariant basis.

\textbf{Consequence:} With the closure theorem \texttt{RepresentationComplete} now explicit, the formal bridge no longer says merely that one chosen representation works. It says that no rival admissible representation survives. Any later physical reading must therefore attach to a unique formal record rather than to a convenient encoding chosen after the fact.

\begin{code}
-- ¬ß RepresentationComplete: every K4PhysRep agrees with canonical-rep on all values.
module RepresentationComplete (r : K4PhysRep) where
  private module F = ForcedValues r
  open K4PhysRep

  val-dim        : dim r          ‚Č° dim canonical-rep
  val-dim        = F.dim-is-4

  val-spatial    : n-spatial r    ‚Č° n-spatial canonical-rep
  val-spatial    = F.spatial-is-3

  val-temporal   : n-temporal r   ‚Č° n-temporal canonical-rep
  val-temporal   = F.temporal-is-1

  val-gauge      : gauge-rank r   ‚Č° gauge-rank canonical-rep
  val-gauge      = F.gauge-is-6

  val-faces      : face-count r   ‚Č° face-count canonical-rep
  val-faces      = F.faces-is-4

  val-euler      : euler r        ‚Č° euler canonical-rep
  val-euler      = F.euler-is-2

  val-coupling   : coupling-inv r ‚Č° coupling-inv canonical-rep
  val-coupling   = F.coupling-is-137

  val-gen        : n-gen r        ‚Č° n-gen canonical-rep
  val-gen        = F.gen-is-3

  val-spinor     : spinor-dim r   ‚Č° spinor-dim canonical-rep
  val-spinor     = F.spinor-is-16

  val-hierarchy  : hierarchy r    ‚Č° hierarchy canonical-rep
  val-hierarchy  = F.hierarchy-is-22

  val-auto       : auto-count r   ‚Č° auto-count canonical-rep
  val-auto       = F.auto-is-24

  val-bell       : bell-sq r      ‚Č° bell-sq canonical-rep
  val-bell       = F.bell-is-8

  val-bh         : bh-norm r      ‚Č° bh-norm canonical-rep
  val-bh         = F.bh-is-4

  val-uv         : uv-cutoff r    ‚Č° uv-cutoff canonical-rep
  val-uv         = F.uv-is-1

  val-loop       : min-loop r     ‚Č° min-loop canonical-rep
  val-loop       = F.loop-is-1

  val-baryon-num : baryon-num r   ‚Č° baryon-num canonical-rep
  val-baryon-num = F.baryon-num-is-1

  val-baryon-den : baryon-den r   ‚Č° baryon-den canonical-rep
  val-baryon-den = F.baryon-den-is-6
\end{code}

\subsubsection*{Local graph observables}

The first block isolates the vertex-local computations: row sums, propagation directions, and the basis witness showing that the local signal really is the degree. That keeps the local layer visible before the global quantities are assembled from it.

\begin{code}
-- ¬ß Physical quantities as computable K‚āĄ functions
module PhysicalQuantities where

  -- ¬ß Signal directions: adjacency row sum at each vertex
  signal-directions : K4Vertex ‚Üí ‚Ąē
  signal-directions v =
    adjacent v v‚āÄ + adjacent v v‚āĀ + adjacent v v‚āā + adjacent v v‚āÉ

  -- ¬ß Signal is vertex-uniform and equals degree
  signal-is-degree : (v : K4Vertex) ‚Üí signal-directions v ‚Č° degree-K4
  signal-is-degree = law-adjacency-degree

  -- ¬ß Each signal propagates independently (basis property)
  signal-independent : (u v : K4Vertex) ‚Üí
    sum-neighbors (K4-basis u) v ‚Č° adjacent v u
  signal-independent = law-basis-spreads

\end{code}

\subsubsection*{Global quantities built from the local layer}

Once the local observables are fixed, the rest of the physics-facing quantities are short global computations. Splitting them into a second block mirrors the earlier chapters, where constructors come first and packaged consequences follow afterward.

\begin{code}

  -- ¬ß Boundary quanta: 2-simplex count = face count
  boundary-quanta : ‚Ąē
  boundary-quanta = faceCountK4

  -- ¬ß Self-duality: boundary = bulk (F = V for the tetrahedron)
  boundary-is-bulk : boundary-quanta ‚Č° vertexCountK4
  boundary-is-bulk = refl

  -- ¬ß Spectral width: largest minus smallest adjacency eigenvalue
  spectral-width : ‚Ąē
  spectral-width = degree-K4 + 1

  -- ¬ß Spectral width equals vertex count
  spectral-width-is-V : spectral-width ‚Č° vertexCountK4
  spectral-width-is-V = refl

  -- ¬ß Maximum squared correlator on the graph
  max-sq-correlator : ‚Ąē
  max-sq-correlator = spectral-width * eulerChar-computed

  -- ¬ß Correlator equals discrete CHSH bound
  correlator-is-őļ : max-sq-correlator ‚Č° őļ-discrete
  correlator-is-őļ = refl

  -- ¬ß Observer directions: non-signal complement (V ‚ął deg)
  observer-directions : ‚Ąē
  observer-directions = vertexCountK4 ‚ął degree-K4

  -- ¬ß Observer direction count is 1
  observer-is-1 : observer-directions ‚Č° 1
  observer-is-1 = refl

  -- ¬ß Same computation, three names (all V ‚ął deg)
  observer-is-max-prop : observer-directions ‚Č° max-propagation-per-edge
  observer-is-max-prop = refl

  observer-is-triangle : observer-directions ‚Č° triangle-loop-order
  observer-is-triangle = refl

  observer-is-lattice : observer-directions ‚Č° lattice-spacing-planck
  observer-is-lattice = refl

  -- ¬ß Interaction channels: total pairwise edges
  interaction-channels : ‚Ąē
  interaction-channels = edgeCountK4
\end{code}


%‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ
\section{Observable representation and closure}
\label{sec:k4-representation:observable-representation-closure}
%‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ

The representation is now rebuilt entirely from computed observables. This removes the last appearance of ad hoc assignment: every field is a graph-theoretic computation on $K_4$, and every constraint reduces to \texttt{refl} because the defining formula already is the required computation.

\begin{code}
  -- ¬ß K4PhysRep from computed quantities (no bare numerals)
  observable-rep : K4PhysRep
  observable-rep = record
    { dim          = vertexCountK4
    ; n-spatial    = degree-K4
    ; n-temporal   = observer-directions
    ; gauge-rank   = interaction-channels
    ; face-count   = boundary-quanta
    ; euler        = eulerChar-computed
    ; coupling-inv = alpha-inverse
    ; n-gen        = degree-K4
    ; spinor-dim   = clifford-dimension
    ; hierarchy    = hierarchy-exponent
    ; auto-count   = vertexCountK4 * degree-K4
                   * (degree-K4 ‚ął 1) * (degree-K4 ‚ął 2)
    ; bell-sq      = max-sq-correlator
    ; bh-norm      = boundary-quanta
    ; baryon-num   = observer-directions
    ; baryon-den   = interaction-channels
    ; uv-cutoff    = observer-directions
    ; min-loop     = observer-directions
    ; anchor       = refl
    ; cg-deg       = refl
    ; cg-temporal  = refl
    ; cg-edges     = refl
    ; cg-faces     = refl
    ; cg-euler     = refl
    ; ph-spinor    = refl
    ; ph-auto      = refl
    ; ph-coupling  = refl
    ; ph-hierarchy = refl
    ; ph-gen       = refl
    ; ph-bell      = refl
    ; ph-bh        = refl
    ; ph-uv        = refl
    ; ph-loop      = refl
    ; ph-baryon-num = refl
    ; ph-baryon-den = refl
    }

  -- ¬ß Closure: observable-rep IS canonical-rep (definitional equality)
  observable-is-canonical : observable-rep ‚Č° canonical-rep
  observable-is-canonical = refl
\end{code}


%‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ
\section{The observable principle}
\label{sec:k4-representation:observable-principle}
%‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ

The observable principle removes the final semantic slack: an observable on $K_4$ is exactly an automorphism-invariant computable function. Because $K_4$ is vertex-transitive, every local observable must be uniform across vertices, while every global observable is already fixed by graph-isomorphism invariance on the named combinatorial data.

\subsubsection*{Observable types and invariance}

\begin{code}
  -- ¬ß K‚āĄ vertex-level invariance
  IsK4Invariant : (K4Vertex ‚Üí ‚Ąē) ‚Üí Set
  IsK4Invariant f = (v w : K4Vertex) ‚Üí f v ‚Č° f w

  -- ¬ß Local observable: vertex function + uniformity proof
  LocalObservable : Set
  LocalObservable = ő£ (K4Vertex ‚Üí ‚Ąē) IsK4Invariant

  -- ¬ß Global admissible observables collapse to four canonical codes.
  data GlobalObservable : Set where
    go-boundary go-correlator go-observer go-channels : GlobalObservable

  eval-global-observable : GlobalObservable ‚Üí ‚Ąē
  eval-global-observable go-boundary   = boundary-quanta
  eval-global-observable go-correlator = max-sq-correlator
  eval-global-observable go-observer   = observer-directions
  eval-global-observable go-channels   = interaction-channels

  -- ¬ß Complete observable type
  Observable : Set
  Observable = LocalObservable ‚äé GlobalObservable

  -- ¬ß Every invariant function has a unique value, independent of vertex
  invariant-value : (f : K4Vertex ‚Üí ‚Ąē) ‚Üí IsK4Invariant f ‚Üí ‚Ąē
  invariant-value f _ = f v‚āÄ

  invariant-any-vertex : (f : K4Vertex ‚Üí ‚Ąē) ‚Üí (inv : IsK4Invariant f) ‚Üí
    (v : K4Vertex) ‚Üí f v ‚Č° invariant-value f inv
  invariant-any-vertex f inv v = inv v v‚āÄ

  observable-value : Observable ‚Üí ‚Ąē
  observable-value (inj‚āĀ (f , inv)) = invariant-value f inv
  observable-value (inj‚āā g) = eval-global-observable g

\end{code}

\subsubsection*{Local observable witnesses}

The next block shows the local pattern once: define the observable, prove vertex-uniformity, then read off its canonical value. Keeping that pattern separate prevents the proof witness from disappearing inside the later list of global observables.

\begin{code}

  -- ¬ß Signal-directions is a local observable
  signal-invariant : IsK4Invariant signal-directions
  signal-invariant v w =
    trans (signal-is-degree v) (sym (signal-is-degree w))

  signal-observable : LocalObservable
  signal-observable = signal-directions , signal-invariant

  -- ¬ß Observed value is degree = 3
  signal-observed-value : invariant-value signal-directions signal-invariant
                        ‚Č° degree-K4
  signal-observed-value = refl

  -- ¬ß Adjacency row sum is a local observable
  adj-row : K4Vertex ‚Üí K4Vertex ‚Üí ‚Ąē
  adj-row v w = adjacent v w

  adj-row-sum-invariant : IsK4Invariant (őĽ v ‚Üí
    adj-row v v‚āÄ + adj-row v v‚āĀ + adj-row v v‚āā + adj-row v v‚āÉ)
  adj-row-sum-invariant v w =
    trans (law-adjacency-degree v) (sym (law-adjacency-degree w))

\end{code}

\subsubsection*{Global observable witnesses}

The global layer can no longer remain an unrestricted numeric container. If every natural number counted as a global observable merely because it was written down after the graph was fixed, descriptive freedom would return through naming. The admissibility filter therefore has to close the space itself. Only four primitive global classes survive: boundary, correlator, observer, and channels. Everything else in the later physics discussion is either a value of this basis or a derived ledger invariant, not a fifth admissible global observable.

\textbf{Constraint:} A global observable must be presentation-independent and forced by the invariant spine of $K_4$. If the global layer were identified with arbitrary naturals, the observable principle would fail exactly where it is supposed to become strongest.

\textbf{Construction:} The admissible global sector is represented by a finite four-code basis, together with its canonical evaluation map into the already forced graph quantities.

\textbf{Consequence:} Global admissibility is now an elimination statement rather than a naming convention. The space of primitive global observables is closed, finite, and exhausted by the four surviving codes.

\begin{code}

  -- ¬ß Global observables (invariant by construction)
  obs-boundary   : Observable
  obs-boundary   = inj‚āā go-boundary

  obs-correlator : Observable
  obs-correlator = inj‚āā go-correlator

  obs-observer   : Observable
  obs-observer   = inj‚āā go-observer

  obs-channels   : Observable
  obs-channels   = inj‚āā go-channels

  global-observable-exhaustive :
    (g : GlobalObservable) ‚Üí
    (g ‚Č° go-boundary)
    ‚äé ((g ‚Č° go-correlator)
    ‚äé ((g ‚Č° go-observer) ‚äé (g ‚Č° go-channels)))
  global-observable-exhaustive go-boundary   = inj‚āĀ refl
  global-observable-exhaustive go-correlator = inj‚āā (inj‚āĀ refl)
  global-observable-exhaustive go-observer   = inj‚āā (inj‚āā (inj‚āĀ refl))
  global-observable-exhaustive go-channels   = inj‚āā (inj‚āā (inj‚āā refl))

  -- ¬ß Derived ledger invariants remain observable values, but not new primitive classes.
  obs-alpha-value : ‚Ąē
  obs-alpha-value = alpha-inverse

  obs-clifford-value : ‚Ąē
  obs-clifford-value = clifford-dimension

  obs-hierarchy-value : ‚Ąē
  obs-hierarchy-value = hierarchy-exponent

  obs-euler-value : ‚Ąē
  obs-euler-value = eulerChar-computed
\end{code}

%‚ēź‚ēź‚ēź‚ēź‚ēź‚ēź‚ēź‚ēź‚ēź‚ēź‚ēź‚ēź‚ēź‚ēź‚ēź‚ēź‚ēź‚ēź‚ēź‚ēź‚ēź‚ēź‚ēź‚ēź‚ēź‚ēź‚ēź‚ēź‚ēź‚ēź‚ēź‚ēź‚ēź‚ēź‚ēź‚ēź‚ēź‚ēź‚ēź‚ēź‚ēź‚ēź‚ēź‚ēź‚ēź‚ēź‚ēź‚ēź‚ēź‚ēź‚ēź‚ēź‚ēź‚ēź‚ēź‚ēź‚ēź‚ēź‚ēź‚ēź‚ēź‚ēź‚ēź‚ēź‚ēź‚ēź‚ēź‚ēź‚ēź‚ēź‚ēź‚ēź‚ēź‚ēź‚ēź
\chapter{K$_4$ Vertex Permutations}
\label{ch:k4-vertex-permutations}
%‚ēź‚ēź‚ēź‚ēź‚ēź‚ēź‚ēź‚ēź‚ēź‚ēź‚ēź‚ēź‚ēź‚ēź‚ēź‚ēź‚ēź‚ēź‚ēź‚ēź‚ēź‚ēź‚ēź‚ēź‚ēź‚ēź‚ēź‚ēź‚ēź‚ēź‚ēź‚ēź‚ēź‚ēź‚ēź‚ēź‚ēź‚ēź‚ēź‚ēź‚ēź‚ēź‚ēź‚ēź‚ēź‚ēź‚ēź‚ēź‚ēź‚ēź‚ēź‚ēź‚ēź‚ēź‚ēź‚ēź‚ēź‚ēź‚ēź‚ēź‚ēź‚ēź‚ēź‚ēź‚ēź‚ēź‚ēź‚ēź‚ēź‚ēź‚ēź‚ēź‚ēź‚ēź‚ēź

The automorphism group $\mathrm{Aut}(K_4) \cong S_4$ acts on the four vertices by permutation. For a complete graph, every bijection is a graph automorphism---adjacency depends only on distinctness, and bijections preserve distinctness. The group acts $2$-transitively: for any two ordered pairs of distinct vertices, some permutation maps one to the other.

This chapter builds the formal infrastructure. The strategy re-uses the permutation machinery already proven for \texttt{EndoCase}---transpositions, involutivity, transitivity---by lifting it through an explicit bijection $\texttt{K4Vertex} \leftrightarrow \texttt{EndoCase}$. No case table is re-proved; the $64$-entry involution table for \texttt{swapEndo} does all the work.

\textbf{Constraint:} The permutation group must act on \texttt{K4Vertex} with full bijectivity proofs, not merely produce functions.

\textbf{Construction:} A record \texttt{K4Perm} packages transport and inverse with round-trip laws. Transpositions and compositions inherit these from \texttt{EndoPerm}. Decidable equality on \texttt{K4Vertex} provides the case analysis that the adjacency-preservation proof requires.

\textbf{Consequence:} Any $\mathbb{N}$-valued function on vertex pairs that is invariant under all \texttt{K4Perm} has a single uniform value on all off-diagonal entries. This is the core tool the forced-propagation elimination will use.

%‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ
\section{Vertex bijection}
\label{sec:k4-perms:bijection}
%‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ

The bijection between \texttt{K4Vertex} and \texttt{EndoCase} maps constructors to constructors and round-trips in both directions.

\begin{code}
k4-to-endo : K4Vertex ‚Üí EndoCase
k4-to-endo v‚āÄ = case-constL
k4-to-endo v‚āĀ = case-constR
k4-to-endo v‚āā = case-id
k4-to-endo v‚āÉ = case-dual

endo-to-k4 : EndoCase ‚Üí K4Vertex
endo-to-k4 case-constL = v‚āÄ
endo-to-k4 case-constR = v‚āĀ
endo-to-k4 case-id     = v‚āā
endo-to-k4 case-dual   = v‚āÉ

k4-endo-k4 : (v : K4Vertex) ‚Üí endo-to-k4 (k4-to-endo v) ‚Č° v
k4-endo-k4 v‚āÄ = refl
k4-endo-k4 v‚āĀ = refl
k4-endo-k4 v‚āā = refl
k4-endo-k4 v‚āÉ = refl

endo-k4-endo : (e : EndoCase) ‚Üí k4-to-endo (endo-to-k4 e) ‚Č° e
endo-k4-endo case-constL = refl
endo-k4-endo case-constR = refl
endo-k4-endo case-id     = refl
endo-k4-endo case-dual   = refl
\end{code}

%‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ
\section{Permutation record and lifting}
\label{sec:k4-perms:record}
%‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ

A \texttt{K4Perm} is a bijection on \texttt{K4Vertex}: forward and inverse maps with round-trip proofs. The function \texttt{liftPerm} converts any \texttt{EndoPerm} into a \texttt{K4Perm} by conjugating through the vertex bijection.

\begin{code}
record K4Perm : Set where
  field
    k4to       : K4Vertex ‚Üí K4Vertex
    k4from     : K4Vertex ‚Üí K4Vertex
    k4-to-from : (v : K4Vertex) ‚Üí k4to (k4from v) ‚Č° v
    k4-from-to : (v : K4Vertex) ‚Üí k4from (k4to v) ‚Č° v

open K4Perm public

liftPerm : EndoPerm ‚Üí K4Perm
liftPerm ŌÉ = record
  { k4to      = őĽ v ‚Üí endo-to-k4 (eto ŌÉ (k4-to-endo v))
  ; k4from    = őĽ v ‚Üí endo-to-k4 (efrom ŌÉ (k4-to-endo v))
  ; k4-to-from = őĽ v ‚Üí
      trans (cong (őĽ e ‚Üí endo-to-k4 (eto ŌÉ e))
                  (endo-k4-endo (efrom ŌÉ (k4-to-endo v))))
            (trans (cong endo-to-k4 (eto-efrom ŌÉ (k4-to-endo v)))
                   (k4-endo-k4 v))
  ; k4-from-to = őĽ v ‚Üí
      trans (cong (őĽ e ‚Üí endo-to-k4 (efrom ŌÉ e))
                  (endo-k4-endo (eto ŌÉ (k4-to-endo v))))
            (trans (cong endo-to-k4 (efrom-eto ŌÉ (k4-to-endo v)))
                   (k4-endo-k4 v))
  }
\end{code}

Composition of two permutations inherits bijectivity from the components.

\begin{code}
k4Perm-comp : K4Perm ‚Üí K4Perm ‚Üí K4Perm
k4Perm-comp ŌÉ ŌĄ = record
  { k4to      = őĽ v ‚Üí k4to ŌÉ (k4to ŌĄ v)
  ; k4from    = őĽ v ‚Üí k4from ŌĄ (k4from ŌÉ v)
  ; k4-to-from = őĽ v ‚Üí
      trans (cong (k4to ŌÉ) (k4-to-from ŌĄ (k4from ŌÉ v)))
            (k4-to-from ŌÉ v)
  ; k4-from-to = őĽ v ‚Üí
      trans (cong (k4from ŌĄ) (k4-from-to ŌÉ (k4to ŌĄ v)))
            (k4-from-to ŌĄ v)
  }
\end{code}

%‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ
\section{Decidable equality and adjacency lemmas}
\label{sec:k4-perms:decidable-equality}
%‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ

Decidable equality on \texttt{K4Vertex} enables proofs that branch on whether two vertices coincide. It is forced by constructor discrimination: the four constructors are pairwise distinguishable.

\begin{code}
K4-decEq : (v w : K4Vertex) ‚Üí (v ‚Č° w) ‚äé (v ‚Č† w)
K4-decEq v‚āÄ v‚āÄ = inj‚āĀ refl
K4-decEq v‚āĀ v‚āĀ = inj‚āĀ refl
K4-decEq v‚āā v‚āā = inj‚āĀ refl
K4-decEq v‚āÉ v‚āÉ = inj‚āĀ refl
K4-decEq v‚āÄ v‚āĀ = inj‚āā (őĽ ())
K4-decEq v‚āÄ v‚āā = inj‚āā (őĽ ())
K4-decEq v‚āÄ v‚āÉ = inj‚āā (őĽ ())
K4-decEq v‚āĀ v‚āÄ = inj‚āā (őĽ ())
K4-decEq v‚āĀ v‚āā = inj‚āā (őĽ ())
K4-decEq v‚āĀ v‚āÉ = inj‚āā (őĽ ())
K4-decEq v‚āā v‚āÄ = inj‚āā (őĽ ())
K4-decEq v‚āā v‚āĀ = inj‚āā (őĽ ())
K4-decEq v‚āā v‚āÉ = inj‚āā (őĽ ())
K4-decEq v‚āÉ v‚āÄ = inj‚āā (őĽ ())
K4-decEq v‚āÉ v‚āĀ = inj‚āā (őĽ ())
K4-decEq v‚āÉ v‚āā = inj‚āā (őĽ ())
\end{code}

Two structural facts about \texttt{adjacent}: it returns~$0$ on the diagonal and~$1$ on every off-diagonal pair. Together they say that adjacency in $K_4$ is exactly distinctness.

\begin{code}
adj-self : (v : K4Vertex) ‚Üí adjacent v v ‚Č° 0
adj-self v‚āÄ = refl
adj-self v‚āĀ = refl
adj-self v‚āā = refl
adj-self v‚āÉ = refl

K4-distinct-adj : (v w : K4Vertex) ‚Üí v ‚Č† w ‚Üí adjacent v w ‚Č° 1
K4-distinct-adj v‚āÄ v‚āÄ ne = ‚ä•-elim (ne refl)
K4-distinct-adj v‚āÄ v‚āĀ _  = refl
K4-distinct-adj v‚āÄ v‚āā _  = refl
K4-distinct-adj v‚āÄ v‚āÉ _  = refl
K4-distinct-adj v‚āĀ v‚āÄ _  = refl
K4-distinct-adj v‚āĀ v‚āĀ ne = ‚ä•-elim (ne refl)
K4-distinct-adj v‚āĀ v‚āā _  = refl
K4-distinct-adj v‚āĀ v‚āÉ _  = refl
K4-distinct-adj v‚āā v‚āÄ _  = refl
K4-distinct-adj v‚āā v‚āĀ _  = refl
K4-distinct-adj v‚āā v‚āā ne = ‚ä•-elim (ne refl)
K4-distinct-adj v‚āā v‚āÉ _  = refl
K4-distinct-adj v‚āÉ v‚āÄ _  = refl
K4-distinct-adj v‚āÉ v‚āĀ _  = refl
K4-distinct-adj v‚āÉ v‚āā _  = refl
K4-distinct-adj v‚āÉ v‚āÉ ne = ‚ä•-elim (ne refl)
\end{code}

Injectivity of any \texttt{K4Perm} follows from the round-trip law: compose with the inverse on both sides.

\begin{code}
k4-injective : (ŌÉ : K4Perm) ‚Üí (v w : K4Vertex) ‚Üí
  k4to ŌÉ v ‚Č° k4to ŌÉ w ‚Üí v ‚Č° w
k4-injective ŌÉ v w p =
  trans (sym (k4-from-to ŌÉ v))
        (trans (cong (k4from ŌÉ) p)
               (k4-from-to ŌÉ w))
\end{code}

%‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ
\section{Transitivity and 2-transitivity}
\label{sec:k4-perms:transitivity}
%‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ

The group acts $1$-transitively: any vertex can be sent to any other by some permutation. The proof lifts \texttt{endoPerm-send} through the bijection.

\begin{code}
k4Perm-send : (a a' : K4Vertex) ‚Üí ő£ K4Perm (őĽ ŌÉ ‚Üí k4to ŌÉ a ‚Č° a')
k4Perm-send a a' =
  let ep = endoPerm-send (k4-to-endo a) (k4-to-endo a')
  in (liftPerm (fst ep) ,
      trans (cong endo-to-k4 (snd ep)) (k4-endo-k4 a'))
\end{code}

$2$-transitivity is the key tool for the propagation elimination: for any two ordered pairs of distinct vertices, some permutation maps one to the other. The reference pair is $(v_0, v_1)$. Each of the $12$ off-diagonal targets is reached by at most two transpositions. Every case reduces by computation to \texttt{refl}.

\begin{code}
edge-map : (v w : K4Vertex) ‚Üí v ‚Č† w ‚Üí
  ő£ K4Perm (őĽ ŌÉ ‚Üí ő£ (k4to ŌÉ v‚āÄ ‚Č° v) (őĽ _ ‚Üí k4to ŌÉ v‚āĀ ‚Č° w))
edge-map v‚āÄ v‚āÄ ne = ‚ä•-elim (ne refl)
edge-map v‚āÄ v‚āĀ _  = (liftPerm (permSwap case-constL case-constL)
                     , (refl , refl))
edge-map v‚āÄ v‚āā _  = (liftPerm (permSwap case-constR case-id)
                     , (refl , refl))
edge-map v‚āÄ v‚āÉ _  = (liftPerm (permSwap case-constR case-dual)
                     , (refl , refl))
edge-map v‚āĀ v‚āÄ _  = (liftPerm (permSwap case-constL case-constR)
                     , (refl , refl))
edge-map v‚āĀ v‚āĀ ne = ‚ä•-elim (ne refl)
edge-map v‚āĀ v‚āā _  = (k4Perm-comp (liftPerm (permSwap case-constR case-id))
                                   (liftPerm (permSwap case-constL case-id))
                     , (refl , refl))
edge-map v‚āĀ v‚āÉ _  = (k4Perm-comp (liftPerm (permSwap case-constR case-dual))
                                   (liftPerm (permSwap case-constL case-dual))
                     , (refl , refl))
edge-map v‚āā v‚āÄ _  = (k4Perm-comp (liftPerm (permSwap case-constL case-id))
                                   (liftPerm (permSwap case-constR case-id))
                     , (refl , refl))
edge-map v‚āā v‚āĀ _  = (liftPerm (permSwap case-constL case-id)
                     , (refl , refl))
edge-map v‚āā v‚āā ne = ‚ä•-elim (ne refl)
edge-map v‚āā v‚āÉ _  = (k4Perm-comp (liftPerm (permSwap case-constL case-id))
                                   (liftPerm (permSwap case-constR case-dual))
                     , (refl , refl))
edge-map v‚āÉ v‚āÄ _  = (k4Perm-comp (liftPerm (permSwap case-constL case-dual))
                                   (liftPerm (permSwap case-constR case-dual))
                     , (refl , refl))
edge-map v‚āÉ v‚āĀ _  = (liftPerm (permSwap case-constL case-dual)
                     , (refl , refl))
edge-map v‚āÉ v‚āā _  = (k4Perm-comp (liftPerm (permSwap case-constL case-dual))
                                   (liftPerm (permSwap case-constR case-id))
                     , (refl , refl))
edge-map v‚āÉ v‚āÉ ne = ‚ä•-elim (ne refl)
\end{code}

%‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ
\section{Adjacency preservation}
\label{sec:k4-perms:adjacency-preservation}
%‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ

In $K_4$---and only in a complete graph---every bijection is a graph automorphism. The proof does not inspect the permutation: it branches on whether the two vertices coincide. On the diagonal, both sides equal~$0$; off-diagonal, injectivity forces both sides to~$1$.

\begin{code}
k4-perm-preserves-adj : (ŌÉ : K4Perm) ‚Üí (v w : K4Vertex) ‚Üí
  adjacent v w ‚Č° adjacent (k4to ŌÉ v) (k4to ŌÉ w)
k4-perm-preserves-adj ŌÉ v w with K4-decEq v w
... | inj‚āĀ refl = trans (adj-self v) (sym (adj-self (k4to ŌÉ v)))
... | inj‚āā ne   = trans (K4-distinct-adj v w ne)
                        (sym (K4-distinct-adj (k4to ŌÉ v) (k4to ŌÉ w)
                              (őĽ p ‚Üí ne (k4-injective ŌÉ v w p))))
\end{code}

%‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ
\section{K4-invariance and uniform edge weight}
\label{sec:k4-perms:invariance}
%‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ

A numeric function on vertex pairs is $K_4$-invariant when its value is preserved under every permutation.

\begin{code}
IsK4PermInvariant : (K4Vertex ‚Üí K4Vertex ‚Üí ‚Ąē) ‚Üí Set
IsK4PermInvariant f = (ŌÉ : K4Perm) ‚Üí (v w : K4Vertex) ‚Üí
  f v w ‚Č° f (k4to ŌÉ v) (k4to ŌÉ w)
\end{code}

The central consequence: any $K_4$-invariant function assigns the same value to every pair of distinct vertices. The proof picks two permutations---one from \texttt{edge-map} for each target pair---and chains the invariance equalities through the common reference value $f\, v_0\, v_1$.

\begin{code}
invariant-uniform-edges : (f : K4Vertex ‚Üí K4Vertex ‚Üí ‚Ąē) ‚Üí
  IsK4PermInvariant f ‚Üí
  (v w v' w' : K4Vertex) ‚Üí v ‚Č† w ‚Üí v' ‚Č† w' ‚Üí f v w ‚Č° f v' w'
invariant-uniform-edges f inv v w v' w' ne ne' =
  trans (sym (to-ref v w ne)) (to-ref v' w' ne')
  where
    to-ref : (a b : K4Vertex) ‚Üí a ‚Č† b ‚Üí f v‚āÄ v‚āĀ ‚Č° f a b
    to-ref a b ne-ab =
      let em = edge-map a b ne-ab
          ŌÉ  = fst em
          p  = fst (snd em)
          q  = snd (snd em)
      in trans (inv ŌÉ v‚āÄ v‚āĀ)
               (trans (cong (őĽ x ‚Üí f x (k4to ŌÉ v‚āĀ)) p)
                      (cong (f a) q))
\end{code}

    This is the exact place where the admissibility architecture must sit. The permutation chapter supplies the transport engine: a single reference value on one edge, face, or vertex can now be moved across the full orbit of the same simplicial dimension. The three physics-constant chapters therefore no longer need to postulate pointwise lower and upper bounds on every simplex. It is enough to bracket one reference simplex and combine that bracket with invariance and the local diagonal law. The next three chapters apply that same pattern in dimensions $1$, $2$, and $0$ respectively.


%‚ēź‚ēź‚ēź‚ēź‚ēź‚ēź‚ēź‚ēź‚ēź‚ēź‚ēź‚ēź‚ēź‚ēź‚ēź‚ēź‚ēź‚ēź‚ēź‚ēź‚ēź‚ēź‚ēź‚ēź‚ēź‚ēź‚ēź‚ēź‚ēź‚ēź‚ēź‚ēź‚ēź‚ēź‚ēź‚ēź‚ēź‚ēź‚ēź‚ēź‚ēź‚ēź‚ēź‚ēź‚ēź‚ēź‚ēź‚ēź‚ēź‚ēź‚ēź‚ēź‚ēź‚ēź‚ēź‚ēź‚ēź‚ēź‚ēź‚ēź‚ēź‚ēź‚ēź‚ēź‚ēź‚ēź‚ēź‚ēź‚ēź‚ēź‚ēź‚ēź‚ēź‚ēź‚ēź
\chapter{Forced Propagation}
\label{ch:forced-propagation}
%‚ēź‚ēź‚ēź‚ēź‚ēź‚ēź‚ēź‚ēź‚ēź‚ēź‚ēź‚ēź‚ēź‚ēź‚ēź‚ēź‚ēź‚ēź‚ēź‚ēź‚ēź‚ēź‚ēź‚ēź‚ēź‚ēź‚ēź‚ēź‚ēź‚ēź‚ēź‚ēź‚ēź‚ēź‚ēź‚ēź‚ēź‚ēź‚ēź‚ēź‚ēź‚ēź‚ēź‚ēź‚ēź‚ēź‚ēź‚ēź‚ēź‚ēź‚ēź‚ēź‚ēź‚ēź‚ēź‚ēź‚ēź‚ēź‚ēź‚ēź‚ēź‚ēź‚ēź‚ēź‚ēź‚ēź‚ēź‚ēź‚ēź‚ēź‚ēź‚ēź‚ēź‚ēź‚ēź

The arithmetic identity $\texttt{max-propagation-per-edge} \equiv 1$ was proved earlier by reduction. That identity computes the right number but does not eliminate alternatives: it never defines what a propagation law \emph{is}, so it never shows that rival laws fail.

This chapter closes that gap. A propagation law is any numeric function on vertex pairs, subject to four independent structural constraints---each of which admits genuine alternatives on its own. Only their intersection is a singleton. The elimination is not a case split. It is a sandwich argument: automorphism invariance collapses the off-diagonal entries to a single value $r$, the lower bound forces $1 \le r$, the upper bound forces $r \le 1$, and antisymmetry closes to $r = 1$.

\textbf{Constraint:} The candidate space is deliberately wide: \emph{any} function $K_4\text{Vertex} \to K_4\text{Vertex} \to \mathbb{N}$. Candidates such as $P \equiv 2$ on all edges, or $P \equiv 3$ on one edge and $1$ elsewhere, are included and must be eliminated by the filter, not excluded by the definition.

\textbf{Construction:} Four filters are applied in sequence:
\begin{enumerate}
\item \emph{Locality} --- zero on the diagonal.
\item \emph{Edge support} --- every off-diagonal entry is at least $1$.
\item \emph{$K_4$-invariance} --- the law is preserved under every vertex permutation.
\item \emph{Boundedness} --- every entry is at most $1$.
\end{enumerate}
Filters 2 and 4 are the wide brackets; filter 3 does the structural work. After invariance, the $12$ off-diagonal values collapse to a single unknown $r = P\,v_0\,v_1$. Edge support gives $1 \le r$; boundedness gives $r \le 1$; antisymmetry of $\le$ yields $r \equiv 1$. The diagonal is $0$ by locality. Together: $P \equiv \texttt{adjacent}$.

\textbf{Consequence:} The propagation rate is forced without enumerating vertex pairs. The earlier arithmetic computation becomes a corollary of a structural elimination.

%‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ
\section{Propagation candidates}
\label{sec:forced-propagation:candidates}
%‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ

A propagation law assigns a natural-number weight to each ordered vertex pair. No constraint is placed yet---the type is deliberately wide. The constant-$2$ law, the constant-$7$ law, and every non-uniform variant are all members of this type.

\begin{code}
PropagationLaw : Set
PropagationLaw = K4Vertex ‚Üí K4Vertex ‚Üí ‚Ąē
\end{code}

%‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ
\section{The four admissibility filters}
\label{sec:forced-propagation:filters}
%‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ

Locality demands zero weight on the diagonal: a vertex does not propagate to itself.

\begin{code}
IsLocal : PropagationLaw ‚Üí Set
IsLocal P = (v : K4Vertex) ‚Üí P v v ‚Č° 0
\end{code}

Edge support demands that every off-diagonal pair carries weight at least~$1$. This is deliberately weak---it admits $P = 2$, $P = 5$, any non-zero value.

\begin{code}
HasEdgeSupport : PropagationLaw ‚Üí Set
HasEdgeSupport P = (v w : K4Vertex) ‚Üí v ‚Č† w ‚Üí 1 ‚Č§ P v w

ReferenceEdgeLower : PropagationLaw ‚Üí Set
ReferenceEdgeLower P = 1 ‚Č§ P v‚āÄ v‚āĀ
\end{code}

Boundedness demands that no single edge carries weight above~$1$. Physically: propagation is local, with at most one unit of signal per direct link. No shortcutting, no amplification. This is the upper bracket that meets the lower bracket from edge support.

\begin{code}
IsBounded : PropagationLaw ‚Üí Set
IsBounded P = (v w : K4Vertex) ‚Üí P v w ‚Č§ 1

ReferenceEdgeUpper : PropagationLaw ‚Üí Set
ReferenceEdgeUpper P = P v‚āÄ v‚āĀ ‚Č§ 1
\end{code}

$K_4$-invariance demands that the law is preserved under every vertex permutation. This is the structural filter that, in the previous chapter, was shown to collapse all off-diagonal entries to a single value.

The full admissibility record packages all four constraints. Each constraint admits genuine alternatives on its own; only their intersection is a singleton.

\begin{code}
record AdmissiblePropagation (P : PropagationLaw) : Set where
  field
    local     : IsLocal P
    invariant : IsK4PermInvariant P
    ref-lower : ReferenceEdgeLower P
    ref-upper : ReferenceEdgeUpper P
\end{code}

%‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ
\section{The elimination}
\label{sec:forced-propagation:elimination}
%‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ

The elimination proceeds without enumerating vertex pairs. It uses three lemmas, each of which is structural rather than computational.

\paragraph{Step 1: Invariance collapses off-diagonal to one value.}
By \texttt{invariant-uniform-edges} (proved in the previous chapter), any $K_4$-invariant function assigns the same value $r$ to every pair of distinct vertices. The reference value is $P\,v_0\,v_1$.

\begin{code}
propagation-uniform : (P : PropagationLaw) ‚Üí IsK4PermInvariant P ‚Üí
  (v w : K4Vertex) ‚Üí v ‚Č† w ‚Üí P v w ‚Č° P v‚āÄ v‚āĀ
propagation-uniform P inv v w ne =
  invariant-uniform-edges P inv v w v‚āÄ v‚āĀ ne (őĽ ())
\end{code}

\paragraph{Step 2: Invariance transports the brackets.}
The lower and upper brackets are imposed only on the reference edge $(v_0,v_1)$. Invariance transports them to every off-diagonal edge.

\begin{code}
propagation-supported : (P : PropagationLaw) ‚Üí AdmissiblePropagation P ‚Üí
  (v w : K4Vertex) ‚Üí v ‚Č† w ‚Üí 1 ‚Č§ P v w
propagation-supported P adm v w ne =
  subst (őĽ n ‚Üí 1 ‚Č§ n)
    (sym (propagation-uniform P (AdmissiblePropagation.invariant adm) v w ne))
    (AdmissiblePropagation.ref-lower adm)

propagation-bounded : (P : PropagationLaw) ‚Üí AdmissiblePropagation P ‚Üí
  (v w : K4Vertex) ‚Üí P v w ‚Č§ 1
propagation-bounded P adm v w with K4-decEq v w
... | inj‚āĀ refl = subst (őĽ n ‚Üí n ‚Č§ 1) (sym (AdmissiblePropagation.local adm v)) z‚Č§n
... | inj‚āā ne   = subst (őĽ n ‚Üí n ‚Č§ 1)
                      (sym (propagation-uniform P (AdmissiblePropagation.invariant adm) v w ne))
                      (AdmissiblePropagation.ref-upper adm)
\end{code}

\paragraph{Step 3: The sandwich.}
The transported lower bound gives $1 \le r$. The transported upper bound gives $r \le 1$. Antisymmetry of $\le$ closes to $r \equiv 1$.

\begin{code}
v‚āÄ‚Č†v‚āĀ : v‚āÄ ‚Č† v‚āĀ
v‚āÄ‚Č†v‚āĀ ()

propagation-edge-value : (P : PropagationLaw) ‚Üí AdmissiblePropagation P ‚Üí
  P v‚āÄ v‚āĀ ‚Č° 1
propagation-edge-value P adm =
  ‚Č§-antisym
    (AdmissiblePropagation.ref-upper adm)
    (AdmissiblePropagation.ref-lower adm)
\end{code}

\paragraph{Step 4: Assembly.}
Every off-diagonal entry equals $1$; every diagonal entry equals $0$. Both agree with \texttt{adjacent}, whose definition is exactly $0$ on the diagonal and $1$ elsewhere.

\begin{code}
propagation-off-diag : (P : PropagationLaw) ‚Üí AdmissiblePropagation P ‚Üí
  (v w : K4Vertex) ‚Üí v ‚Č† w ‚Üí P v w ‚Č° 1
propagation-off-diag P adm v w ne =
  trans (propagation-uniform P (AdmissiblePropagation.invariant adm) v w ne)
        (propagation-edge-value P adm)

propagation-forced : (P : PropagationLaw) ‚Üí AdmissiblePropagation P ‚Üí
  (v w : K4Vertex) ‚Üí P v w ‚Č° adjacent v w
propagation-forced P adm v w with K4-decEq v w
... | inj‚āĀ refl = trans (AdmissiblePropagation.local adm v)
                        (sym (adj-self v))
... | inj‚āā ne   = trans (propagation-off-diag P adm v w ne)
                        (sym (K4-distinct-adj v w ne))
\end{code}

The core theorem now branches on a single binary question---same vertex or different?---not on $16$ constructor pairs. The structural work is done by invariance; the arithmetic is done by the sandwich.

%‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ
\section{Existence}
\label{sec:forced-propagation:existence}
%‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ

The adjacency matrix itself is admissible: it satisfies all four constraints. Locality and boundedness hold by computation. Edge support follows from \texttt{K4-distinct-adj}. Invariance follows from \texttt{k4-perm-preserves-adj}.

\begin{code}
adjacent-is-local : IsLocal adjacent
adjacent-is-local v‚āÄ = refl
adjacent-is-local v‚āĀ = refl
adjacent-is-local v‚āā = refl
adjacent-is-local v‚āÉ = refl

adjacent-supported : HasEdgeSupport adjacent
adjacent-supported v w ne = subst (őĽ n ‚Üí 1 ‚Č§ n)
  (sym (K4-distinct-adj v w ne)) (s‚Č§s z‚Č§n)

adjacent-bounded : IsBounded adjacent
adjacent-bounded v‚āÄ v‚āÄ = z‚Č§n
adjacent-bounded v‚āÄ v‚āĀ = s‚Č§s z‚Č§n
adjacent-bounded v‚āÄ v‚āā = s‚Č§s z‚Č§n
adjacent-bounded v‚āÄ v‚āÉ = s‚Č§s z‚Č§n
adjacent-bounded v‚āĀ v‚āÄ = s‚Č§s z‚Č§n
adjacent-bounded v‚āĀ v‚āĀ = z‚Č§n
adjacent-bounded v‚āĀ v‚āā = s‚Č§s z‚Č§n
adjacent-bounded v‚āĀ v‚āÉ = s‚Č§s z‚Č§n
adjacent-bounded v‚āā v‚āÄ = s‚Č§s z‚Č§n
adjacent-bounded v‚āā v‚āĀ = s‚Č§s z‚Č§n
adjacent-bounded v‚āā v‚āā = z‚Č§n
adjacent-bounded v‚āā v‚āÉ = s‚Č§s z‚Č§n
adjacent-bounded v‚āÉ v‚āÄ = s‚Č§s z‚Č§n
adjacent-bounded v‚āÉ v‚āĀ = s‚Č§s z‚Č§n
adjacent-bounded v‚āÉ v‚āā = s‚Č§s z‚Č§n
adjacent-bounded v‚āÉ v‚āÉ = z‚Č§n

adjacent-invariant : IsK4PermInvariant adjacent
adjacent-invariant ŌÉ v w = k4-perm-preserves-adj ŌÉ v w

adjacent-ref-lower : ReferenceEdgeLower adjacent
adjacent-ref-lower = s‚Č§s z‚Č§n

adjacent-ref-upper : ReferenceEdgeUpper adjacent
adjacent-ref-upper = s‚Č§s z‚Č§n

adjacent-admissible : AdmissiblePropagation adjacent
adjacent-admissible = record
  { local     = adjacent-is-local
  ; invariant = adjacent-invariant
  ; ref-lower = adjacent-ref-lower
  ; ref-upper = adjacent-ref-upper
  }
\end{code}

%‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ
\section{Summary and the discrete speed of light}
\label{sec:forced-propagation:summary}
%‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ

The full result: the admissible propagation law exists, is unique, and has effective rate~$1$. The uniqueness is structural---invariance, sandwich, antisymmetry---not a $4 \times 4$ case table.

\begin{code}
record ForcedPropagationResult : Set where
  field
    witness   : AdmissiblePropagation adjacent
    unique    : (P : PropagationLaw) ‚Üí AdmissiblePropagation P ‚Üí
                (v w : K4Vertex) ‚Üí P v w ‚Č° adjacent v w
    rate-is-1 : (P : PropagationLaw) ‚Üí AdmissiblePropagation P ‚Üí
                (v w : K4Vertex) ‚Üí adjacent v w ‚Č° 1 ‚Üí P v w ‚Č° 1

theorem-forced-propagation : ForcedPropagationResult
theorem-forced-propagation = record
  { witness   = adjacent-admissible
  ; unique    = propagation-forced
  ; rate-is-1 = őĽ P adm v w hw ‚Üí trans (propagation-forced P adm v w) hw
  }
\end{code}

With the elimination closed, the propagation rate can be named for what it is: the unit propagation speed in native FD units. The value $c_{\mathrm{FD}} = 1$ is not a numerical input or a convenient convention. It is the only value that survives the four-filter admissibility test.

\begin{code}
c-FD : ‚Ąē
c-FD = 1

c-FD-is-propagation-rate : c-FD ‚Č° max-propagation-per-edge
c-FD-is-propagation-rate = refl
\end{code}

%‚ēź‚ēź‚ēź‚ēź‚ēź‚ēź‚ēź‚ēź‚ēź‚ēź‚ēź‚ēź‚ēź‚ēź‚ēź‚ēź‚ēź‚ēź‚ēź‚ēź‚ēź‚ēź‚ēź‚ēź‚ēź‚ēź‚ēź‚ēź‚ēź‚ēź‚ēź‚ēź‚ēź‚ēź‚ēź‚ēź‚ēź‚ēź‚ēź‚ēź‚ēź‚ēź‚ēź‚ēź‚ēź‚ēź‚ēź‚ēź‚ēź‚ēź‚ēź‚ēź‚ēź‚ēź‚ēź‚ēź‚ēź‚ēź‚ēź‚ēź‚ēź‚ēź‚ēź‚ēź‚ēź‚ēź‚ēź‚ēź‚ēź‚ēź‚ēź‚ēź‚ēź‚ēź‚ēź
\chapter{Forced Minimal Action}
\label{ch:forced-minimal-action}
%‚ēź‚ēź‚ēź‚ēź‚ēź‚ēź‚ēź‚ēź‚ēź‚ēź‚ēź‚ēź‚ēź‚ēź‚ēź‚ēź‚ēź‚ēź‚ēź‚ēź‚ēź‚ēź‚ēź‚ēź‚ēź‚ēź‚ēź‚ēź‚ēź‚ēź‚ēź‚ēź‚ēź‚ēź‚ēź‚ēź‚ēź‚ēź‚ēź‚ēź‚ēź‚ēź‚ēź‚ēź‚ēź‚ēź‚ēź‚ēź‚ēź‚ēź‚ēź‚ēź‚ēź‚ēź‚ēź‚ēź‚ēź‚ēź‚ēź‚ēź‚ēź‚ēź‚ēź‚ēź‚ēź‚ēź‚ēź‚ēź‚ēź‚ēź‚ēź‚ēź‚ēź‚ēź‚ēź

$K_4$ has a forced cycle structure. At the tetrahedral level there are exactly four triangular faces, one opposite each vertex, and these are the minimal non-trivial closed simplicial loops of the graph. An action law assigns a natural-number weight to each face. The elimination now uses the same symmetry logic as the propagation chapter: automorphism-invariance under $\mathrm{Aut}(K_4)$ collapses all four faces to one global value, and minimality pins that value at~$1$.

\textbf{Constraint:} The candidate space must not encode the target value. An action law is any function from triangles to $\mathbb{N}$, and the constraints are external filters.

\textbf{Construction:} Four filters are applied:
\begin{enumerate}
\item \emph{Non-triviality} --- every face has positive weight.
\item \emph{$K_4$-invariance} --- the law is preserved under every vertex permutation.
\item \emph{Lower bound} --- every face carries weight at least~$1$.
\item \emph{Minimality} --- no face carries weight above~$1$.
\end{enumerate}
Filters 3 and 4 are the wide brackets; filter 2 does the structural work. After invariance, the four face values collapse to a single unknown $r = A\,\mathit{tri}\text{-}012$. The lower bound gives $1 \le r$; minimality gives $r \le 1$; antisymmetry of $\le$ yields $r \equiv 1$.

\textbf{Consequence:} The discrete action unit $\hbar_{\mathrm{FD}} = 1$ is forced by a sandwich argument parallel to propagation. The earlier arithmetic statement $\texttt{triangle-loop-order} = 1$ becomes a corollary.

%‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ
\section{Triangles and action candidates}
\label{sec:forced-action:triangles}
%‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ

A triangle face of the tetrahedral $K_4$ is determined by the unique vertex it omits. There are therefore exactly four such faces, one opposite each vertex. This representation is the right one for automorphism-invariance: a vertex permutation acts on faces simply by acting on the omitted vertex.

\begin{code}
K4Triangle : Set
K4Triangle = K4Vertex

triangle-map : K4Perm ‚Üí K4Triangle ‚Üí K4Triangle
triangle-map ŌÉ t = k4to ŌÉ t
\end{code}

An action law assigns a natural-number weight to each triangle. No constraint is assumed yet.

\begin{code}
ActionLaw : Set
ActionLaw = K4Triangle ‚Üí ‚Ąē

tri-012 : K4Triangle
tri-012 = v‚āÉ

tri-013 : K4Triangle
tri-013 = v‚āā

tri-023 : K4Triangle
tri-023 = v‚āĀ

tri-123 : K4Triangle
tri-123 = v‚āÄ
\end{code}

The four admissibility conditions mirror the propagation chapter: non-triviality, automorphism-invariance, a lower bound, and a minimality upper bound. Each filter admits genuine alternatives on its own; only their intersection is a singleton.

\begin{code}
IsNonTrivial : ActionLaw ‚Üí Set
IsNonTrivial A = (t : K4Triangle) ‚Üí A t ‚Č° 0 ‚Üí ‚ä•

IsActionInvariant : ActionLaw ‚Üí Set
IsActionInvariant A = (ŌÉ : K4Perm) ‚Üí (t : K4Triangle) ‚Üí
  A t ‚Č° A (triangle-map ŌÉ t)

ActionBound : ActionLaw ‚Üí Set
ActionBound A = (t : K4Triangle) ‚Üí 1 ‚Č§ A t

ReferenceTriangleLower : ActionLaw ‚Üí Set
ReferenceTriangleLower A = 1 ‚Č§ A tri-012

IsMinimal : ActionLaw ‚Üí Set
IsMinimal A = (t : K4Triangle) ‚Üí A t ‚Č§ 1

ReferenceTriangleUpper : ActionLaw ‚Üí Set
ReferenceTriangleUpper A = A tri-012 ‚Č§ 1

record AdmissibleAction (A : ActionLaw) : Set where
  field
    invariant  : IsActionInvariant A
    ref-lower  : ReferenceTriangleLower A
    ref-upper  : ReferenceTriangleUpper A
\end{code}

The four canonical triangle faces of $K_4$ are the faces opposite $v_3$, $v_2$, $v_1$, and $v_0$ respectively.

%‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ
\section{Existence: the unit action law}
\label{sec:forced-action:existence}
%‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ

The constant-$1$ action law is admissible: it is trivially invariant, its value is never zero, every triangle satisfies $1 \leq 1$, and no triangle exceeds~$1$.

\begin{code}
unit-action : ActionLaw
unit-action _ = 1

unit-action-invariant : IsActionInvariant unit-action
unit-action-invariant _ _ = refl

unit-action-bounded : ActionBound unit-action
unit-action-bounded _ = s‚Č§s z‚Č§n

unit-action-minimal : IsMinimal unit-action
unit-action-minimal _ = s‚Č§s z‚Č§n

unit-action-ref-lower : ReferenceTriangleLower unit-action
unit-action-ref-lower = s‚Č§s z‚Č§n

unit-action-ref-upper : ReferenceTriangleUpper unit-action
unit-action-ref-upper = s‚Č§s z‚Č§n

unit-action-admissible : AdmissibleAction unit-action
unit-action-admissible = record
  { invariant  = unit-action-invariant
  ; ref-lower  = unit-action-ref-lower
  ; ref-upper  = unit-action-ref-upper
  }
\end{code}

%‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ
\section{Elimination: invariance collapses to a single value}
\label{sec:forced-action:elimination}
%‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ

Automorphism-invariance forces every admissible action law to a single global value: all four faces lie in one orbit.

\begin{code}
action-from-012 : (A : ActionLaw) ‚Üí AdmissibleAction A ‚Üí
  (t : K4Triangle) ‚Üí A tri-012 ‚Č° A t
action-from-012 A adm t =
  let st = k4Perm-send tri-012 t in
  let ŌÉ = fst st in
  let p = snd st in
  trans (AdmissibleAction.invariant adm ŌÉ tri-012)
        (cong A p)

action-all-equal-to-012 : (A : ActionLaw) ‚Üí AdmissibleAction A ‚Üí
  (t : K4Triangle) ‚Üí A t ‚Č° A tri-012
action-all-equal-to-012 A adm t =
  sym (action-from-012 A adm t)

action-global-value : (A : ActionLaw) ‚Üí AdmissibleAction A ‚Üí
  (t‚āĀ t‚āā : K4Triangle) ‚Üí A t‚āĀ ‚Č° A t‚āā
action-global-value A adm t‚āĀ t‚āā =
  trans (action-all-equal-to-012 A adm t‚āĀ)
        (action-from-012 A adm t‚āā)
\end{code}

\paragraph{Step 2: Invariance transports the brackets.}
The lower and upper brackets are imposed only on the reference face $\mathit{tri}\text{-}012$. Invariance transports them to every triangle.

\begin{code}
action-bound : (A : ActionLaw) ‚Üí AdmissibleAction A ‚Üí ActionBound A
action-bound A adm t =
  subst (őĽ n ‚Üí 1 ‚Č§ n)
    (action-from-012 A adm t)
    (AdmissibleAction.ref-lower adm)

action-minimal : (A : ActionLaw) ‚Üí AdmissibleAction A ‚Üí IsMinimal A
action-minimal A adm t =
  subst (őĽ n ‚Üí n ‚Č§ 1)
    (action-from-012 A adm t)
    (AdmissibleAction.ref-upper adm)

action-nontrivial : (A : ActionLaw) ‚Üí AdmissibleAction A ‚Üí IsNonTrivial A
action-nontrivial A adm t eq =
  suc‚Č§-impossible zero (subst (őĽ n ‚Üí 1 ‚Č§ n) eq (action-bound A adm t))
\end{code}

\paragraph{Step 3: The sandwich.}
The lower bound gives $1 \le A\,\mathit{tri}\text{-}012$. The upper bound gives $A\,\mathit{tri}\text{-}012 \le 1$. Antisymmetry closes to $A\,\mathit{tri}\text{-}012 \equiv 1$.

\begin{code}
action-ref-value : (A : ActionLaw) ‚Üí AdmissibleAction A ‚Üí A tri-012 ‚Č° 1
action-ref-value A adm =
  ‚Č§-antisym (AdmissibleAction.ref-upper adm)
            (AdmissibleAction.ref-lower adm)
\end{code}

\paragraph{Step 4: Assembly.}
Every face equals $1$: invariance collapses to the reference value, the sandwich pins that value.

\begin{code}
action-forced : (A : ActionLaw) ‚Üí AdmissibleAction A ‚Üí
  (t : K4Triangle) ‚Üí A t ‚Č° 1
action-forced A adm t =
  trans (action-all-equal-to-012 A adm t)
        (action-ref-value A adm)
\end{code}

The core theorem branches on no cases at all. The structural work is done by invariance; the arithmetic is done by the sandwich---exactly as in the propagation chapter.

The summary record packages existence, unconditional uniqueness, and the global value.

\begin{code}
record ForcedActionResult : Set where
  field
    witness      : AdmissibleAction unit-action
    global-value : (A : ActionLaw) ‚Üí AdmissibleAction A ‚Üí
                   (t‚āĀ t‚āā : K4Triangle) ‚Üí A t‚āĀ ‚Č° A t‚āā
    unique       : (A : ActionLaw) ‚Üí AdmissibleAction A ‚Üí
                   (t : K4Triangle) ‚Üí A t ‚Č° 1

theorem-forced-action : ForcedActionResult
theorem-forced-action = record
  { witness      = unit-action-admissible
  ; global-value = action-global-value
  ; unique       = action-forced
  }
\end{code}

%‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ
\section{The discrete action unit}
\label{sec:forced-action:hbar-fd}
%‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ

The discrete action unit $\hbar_{\mathrm{FD}} = 1$ names the unique admissible action value in native FD units. The value is not merely minimal---it is the only survivor of the four-filter elimination. It is consistent with the triangle loop order already computed.

\begin{code}
hbar-FD : ‚Ąē
hbar-FD = 1

hbar-FD-is-loop-order : hbar-FD ‚Č° triangle-loop-order
hbar-FD-is-loop-order = refl
\end{code}


%‚ēź‚ēź‚ēź‚ēź‚ēź‚ēź‚ēź‚ēź‚ēź‚ēź‚ēź‚ēź‚ēź‚ēź‚ēź‚ēź‚ēź‚ēź‚ēź‚ēź‚ēź‚ēź‚ēź‚ēź‚ēź‚ēź‚ēź‚ēź‚ēź‚ēź‚ēź‚ēź‚ēź‚ēź‚ēź‚ēź‚ēź‚ēź‚ēź‚ēź‚ēź‚ēź‚ēź‚ēź‚ēź‚ēź‚ēź‚ēź‚ēź‚ēź‚ēź‚ēź‚ēź‚ēź‚ēź‚ēź‚ēź‚ēź‚ēź‚ēź‚ēź‚ēź‚ēź‚ēź‚ēź‚ēź‚ēź‚ēź‚ēź‚ēź‚ēź‚ēź‚ēź‚ēź‚ēź
\chapter{Forced Geometric Coupling}
\label{ch:forced-geometric-coupling}
%‚ēź‚ēź‚ēź‚ēź‚ēź‚ēź‚ēź‚ēź‚ēź‚ēź‚ēź‚ēź‚ēź‚ēź‚ēź‚ēź‚ēź‚ēź‚ēź‚ēź‚ēź‚ēź‚ēź‚ēź‚ēź‚ēź‚ēź‚ēź‚ēź‚ēź‚ēź‚ēź‚ēź‚ēź‚ēź‚ēź‚ēź‚ēź‚ēź‚ēź‚ēź‚ēź‚ēź‚ēź‚ēź‚ēź‚ēź‚ēź‚ēź‚ēź‚ēź‚ēź‚ēź‚ēź‚ēź‚ēź‚ēź‚ēź‚ēź‚ēź‚ēź‚ēź‚ēź‚ēź‚ēź‚ēź‚ēź‚ēź‚ēź‚ēź‚ēź‚ēź‚ēź‚ēź‚ēź

The three forced constants of the FD system live on three simplicial dimensions of $K_4$:
\[
\begin{array}{ccl}
\text{dim}\;1 & \text{(edges)}   & \longrightarrow\; c \\
\text{dim}\;2 & \text{(faces)}   & \longrightarrow\; \hbar \\
\text{dim}\;0 & \text{(vertices)} & \longrightarrow\; G
\end{array}
\]
Propagation assigns a weight to each edge; the action assigns a weight to each face. Both were eliminated by the same four-filter sandwich. The gravitational coupling completes the triple: it assigns a curvature weight to each \emph{vertex}---the 0-simplices where, in discrete geometry, curvature concentrates.

A curvature law is any function $K_4\text{Vertex} \to \mathbb{N}$, subject to four constraints---each of which admits genuine alternatives on its own:
\begin{enumerate}
\item \emph{Non-triviality} --- every vertex carries positive curvature.
\item \emph{$K_4$-invariance} --- the law is preserved under every vertex permutation.
\item \emph{Lower bound} --- every vertex carries curvature at least~$1$.
\item \emph{Minimality} --- no vertex carries curvature above~$1$.
\end{enumerate}
The sandwich argument is identical to propagation and action: invariance collapses all four vertex values to a single unknown $r$, the lower bound gives $1 \le r$, minimality gives $r \le 1$, antisymmetry of $\le$ yields $r \equiv 1$.

\textbf{Constraint:} The candidate space is deliberately wide: \emph{any} function $K_4\text{Vertex} \to \mathbb{N}$. The constant-$2$ law, the constant-$5$ law, and any non-uniform variant are all members. The filters are external.

\textbf{Construction:} Four filters applied in sequence collapse the space to a singleton.

\textbf{Consequence:} The discrete gravitational coupling $G_{\mathrm{FD}} = 1$ is forced by the same simplicial elimination that forces $c$ and $\hbar$. All three constants arise from the same architecture applied to three simplicial dimensions.

%‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ
\section{Curvature candidates}
\label{sec:forced-coupling:candidates}
%‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ

A curvature law assigns a natural-number weight to each vertex of $K_4$. No constraint is placed yet---the type is deliberately wide.

\begin{code}
CurvatureLaw : Set
CurvatureLaw = K4Vertex ‚Üí ‚Ąē
\end{code}

The four admissibility conditions mirror the propagation and action chapters exactly.

\begin{code}
IsCurvatureNonTrivial : CurvatureLaw ‚Üí Set
IsCurvatureNonTrivial C = (v : K4Vertex) ‚Üí C v ‚Č° 0 ‚Üí ‚ä•

IsCurvatureInvariant : CurvatureLaw ‚Üí Set
IsCurvatureInvariant C = (ŌÉ : K4Perm) ‚Üí (v : K4Vertex) ‚Üí
  C v ‚Č° C (k4to ŌÉ v)

CurvatureBound : CurvatureLaw ‚Üí Set
CurvatureBound C = (v : K4Vertex) ‚Üí 1 ‚Č§ C v

ReferenceVertexLower : CurvatureLaw ‚Üí Set
ReferenceVertexLower C = 1 ‚Č§ C v‚āÄ

IsCurvatureMinimal : CurvatureLaw ‚Üí Set
IsCurvatureMinimal C = (v : K4Vertex) ‚Üí C v ‚Č§ 1

ReferenceVertexUpper : CurvatureLaw ‚Üí Set
ReferenceVertexUpper C = C v‚āÄ ‚Č§ 1

record AdmissibleCurvature (C : CurvatureLaw) : Set where
  field
    invariant  : IsCurvatureInvariant C
    ref-lower  : ReferenceVertexLower C
    ref-upper  : ReferenceVertexUpper C
\end{code}

%‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ
\section{Existence: the unit curvature law}
\label{sec:forced-coupling:existence}
%‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ

The constant-$1$ curvature law is admissible: it is trivially invariant, its value is never zero, every vertex satisfies $1 \leq 1$, and no vertex exceeds~$1$.

\begin{code}
unit-curvature : CurvatureLaw
unit-curvature _ = 1

unit-curvature-invariant : IsCurvatureInvariant unit-curvature
unit-curvature-invariant _ _ = refl

unit-curvature-bounded : CurvatureBound unit-curvature
unit-curvature-bounded _ = s‚Č§s z‚Č§n

unit-curvature-minimal : IsCurvatureMinimal unit-curvature
unit-curvature-minimal _ = s‚Č§s z‚Č§n

unit-curvature-ref-lower : ReferenceVertexLower unit-curvature
unit-curvature-ref-lower = s‚Č§s z‚Č§n

unit-curvature-ref-upper : ReferenceVertexUpper unit-curvature
unit-curvature-ref-upper = s‚Č§s z‚Č§n

unit-curvature-admissible : AdmissibleCurvature unit-curvature
unit-curvature-admissible = record
  { invariant  = unit-curvature-invariant
  ; ref-lower  = unit-curvature-ref-lower
  ; ref-upper  = unit-curvature-ref-upper
  }
\end{code}

%‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ
\section{Elimination: invariance collapses to a single value}
\label{sec:forced-coupling:elimination}
%‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ

Automorphism-invariance forces every admissible curvature law to a single global value: all four vertices lie in one orbit under $S_4$.

\begin{code}
curvature-from-v0 : (C : CurvatureLaw) ‚Üí AdmissibleCurvature C ‚Üí
  (v : K4Vertex) ‚Üí C v‚āÄ ‚Č° C v
curvature-from-v0 C adm v =
  let st = k4Perm-send v‚āÄ v in
  let ŌÉ = fst st in
  let p = snd st in
  trans (AdmissibleCurvature.invariant adm ŌÉ v‚āÄ)
        (cong C p)

curvature-all-equal-to-v0 : (C : CurvatureLaw) ‚Üí AdmissibleCurvature C ‚Üí
  (v : K4Vertex) ‚Üí C v ‚Č° C v‚āÄ
curvature-all-equal-to-v0 C adm v =
  sym (curvature-from-v0 C adm v)

curvature-global-value : (C : CurvatureLaw) ‚Üí AdmissibleCurvature C ‚Üí
  (v‚āĀ v‚āā : K4Vertex) ‚Üí C v‚āĀ ‚Č° C v‚āā
curvature-global-value C adm u w =
  trans (curvature-all-equal-to-v0 C adm u)
        (curvature-from-v0 C adm w)
\end{code}

\paragraph{Step 2: Invariance transports the brackets.}
The lower and upper brackets are imposed only on the reference vertex $v_0$. Invariance transports them to every vertex.

\begin{code}
curvature-bound : (C : CurvatureLaw) ‚Üí AdmissibleCurvature C ‚Üí CurvatureBound C
curvature-bound C adm v =
  subst (őĽ n ‚Üí 1 ‚Č§ n)
    (curvature-from-v0 C adm v)
    (AdmissibleCurvature.ref-lower adm)

curvature-minimal : (C : CurvatureLaw) ‚Üí AdmissibleCurvature C ‚Üí IsCurvatureMinimal C
curvature-minimal C adm v =
  subst (őĽ n ‚Üí n ‚Č§ 1)
    (curvature-from-v0 C adm v)
    (AdmissibleCurvature.ref-upper adm)

curvature-nontrivial : (C : CurvatureLaw) ‚Üí AdmissibleCurvature C ‚Üí IsCurvatureNonTrivial C
curvature-nontrivial C adm v eq =
  suc‚Č§-impossible zero (subst (őĽ n ‚Üí 1 ‚Č§ n) eq (curvature-bound C adm v))
\end{code}

\paragraph{Step 3: The sandwich.}
The lower bound gives $1 \le C\,v_0$. The upper bound gives $C\,v_0 \le 1$. Antisymmetry closes to $C\,v_0 \equiv 1$.

\begin{code}
curvature-ref-value : (C : CurvatureLaw) ‚Üí AdmissibleCurvature C ‚Üí C v‚āÄ ‚Č° 1
curvature-ref-value C adm =
  ‚Č§-antisym (AdmissibleCurvature.ref-upper adm)
            (AdmissibleCurvature.ref-lower adm)
\end{code}

\paragraph{Step 4: Assembly.}
Every vertex equals $1$: invariance collapses to the reference value, the sandwich pins that value.

\begin{code}
curvature-forced : (C : CurvatureLaw) ‚Üí AdmissibleCurvature C ‚Üí
  (v : K4Vertex) ‚Üí C v ‚Č° 1
curvature-forced C adm v =
  trans (curvature-all-equal-to-v0 C adm v)
        (curvature-ref-value C adm)
\end{code}

%‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ
\section{The discrete gravitational coupling}
\label{sec:forced-coupling:g-fd}
%‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ

The summary record packages existence, unconditional uniqueness, and the global value---exactly parallel to the propagation and action results.

\begin{code}
record ForcedCurvatureResult : Set where
  field
    witness      : AdmissibleCurvature unit-curvature
    global-value : (C : CurvatureLaw) ‚Üí AdmissibleCurvature C ‚Üí
                   (v‚āĀ v‚āā : K4Vertex) ‚Üí C v‚āĀ ‚Č° C v‚āā
    unique       : (C : CurvatureLaw) ‚Üí AdmissibleCurvature C ‚Üí
                   (v : K4Vertex) ‚Üí C v ‚Č° 1

theorem-forced-curvature : ForcedCurvatureResult
theorem-forced-curvature = record
  { witness      = unit-curvature-admissible
  ; global-value = curvature-global-value
  ; unique       = curvature-forced
  }

G-FD : ‚Ąē
G-FD = 1

G-FD-forced : (C : CurvatureLaw) ‚Üí AdmissibleCurvature C ‚Üí C v‚āÄ ‚Č° G-FD
G-FD-forced C adm = curvature-ref-value C adm
\end{code}

%‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ
\section{Filter audit: sufficiency versus minimality}
\label{sec:forced-coupling:filter-audit}
%‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ

The three preceding chapters now prove their target constants by elimination. But a second question is sharper: are the four filters in each chapter themselves already minimal, or do some of them follow from the others? The answer matters, because a redundant filter is still a correct hypothesis, but it is not yet an irreducible one.

The present book has already reacted to that diagnosis. The constant chapters no longer impose pointwise lower and upper brackets on every simplex. Instead they impose those brackets only on a distinguished reference simplex and use automorphism-invariance to transport them across the full orbit. That repair makes invariance genuinely structural again.

The audit lemmas below are therefore no longer a diagnosis of the \emph{current} admissibility records. They are a diagnosis of the older pointwise packages the book has now replaced. Their purpose is historical and conceptual: they show exactly why the earlier pointwise form was too strong, and why the reference-simplex architecture was necessary.

\begin{code}
propagation-forced-no-invariance :
  (P : PropagationLaw) ‚Üí IsLocal P ‚Üí HasEdgeSupport P ‚Üí IsBounded P ‚Üí
  (v w : K4Vertex) ‚Üí P v w ‚Č° adjacent v w
propagation-forced-no-invariance P local support upper v w with K4-decEq v w
... | inj‚āĀ refl = trans (local v) (sym (adj-self v))
... | inj‚āā ne   = trans (‚Č§-antisym (upper v w) (support v w ne))
                        (sym (K4-distinct-adj v w ne))

action-nontrivial-from-bound : (A : ActionLaw) ‚Üí ActionBound A ‚Üí IsNonTrivial A
action-nontrivial-from-bound A lower t eq =
  suc‚Č§-impossible zero (subst (őĽ n ‚Üí 1 ‚Č§ n) eq (lower t))

action-forced-no-invariance :
  (A : ActionLaw) ‚Üí ActionBound A ‚Üí IsMinimal A ‚Üí
  (t : K4Triangle) ‚Üí A t ‚Č° 1
action-forced-no-invariance A lower upper t =
  ‚Č§-antisym (upper t) (lower t)

curvature-nontrivial-from-bound :
  (C : CurvatureLaw) ‚Üí CurvatureBound C ‚Üí IsCurvatureNonTrivial C
curvature-nontrivial-from-bound C lower v eq =
  suc‚Č§-impossible zero (subst (őĽ n ‚Üí 1 ‚Č§ n) eq (lower v))

curvature-forced-no-invariance :
  (C : CurvatureLaw) ‚Üí CurvatureBound C ‚Üí IsCurvatureMinimal C ‚Üí
  (v : K4Vertex) ‚Üí C v ‚Č° 1
curvature-forced-no-invariance C lower upper v =
  ‚Č§-antisym (upper v) (lower v)
\end{code}

The force of these lemmas is conceptual, not destructive. They do \emph{not} weaken the earlier theorems; they explain the repair that followed. The current chapters already prove uniqueness with a genuinely non-pointwise admissibility basis. What they do not yet prove is that this repaired basis is itself the unique irreducible one. That is now the next formal target.

%‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ
\section{Minimal admissibility basis}
\label{sec:forced-coupling:minimal-basis}
%‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ

The repaired admissibility architecture has four primitive clauses in the propagation chapter and three primitive clauses in the action and curvature chapters. To justify calling this basis \emph{minimal}, each primitive clause must now be shown indispensable: if it is removed while the others are kept, a rival law survives.

The proofs are by concrete witness. Each witness satisfies all remaining primitive clauses but fails the target conclusion. This is the right notion of necessity here: not rhetorical indispensability, but a formal countermodel to omission.

\paragraph{Propagation basis.}
Without locality, the everywhere-one law survives. Without invariance, a law can single out the reference edge and ignore the rest. Without the lower bracket, the zero law survives. Without the upper bracket, the doubled adjacency law survives.

\begin{code}
propagation-all-one : PropagationLaw
propagation-all-one _ _ = 1

propagation-all-one-invariant : IsK4PermInvariant propagation-all-one
propagation-all-one-invariant _ _ _ = refl

propagation-all-one-ref-lower : ReferenceEdgeLower propagation-all-one
propagation-all-one-ref-lower = s‚Č§s z‚Č§n

propagation-all-one-ref-upper : ReferenceEdgeUpper propagation-all-one
propagation-all-one-ref-upper = s‚Č§s z‚Č§n

propagation-all-one-not-adj : propagation-all-one v‚āÄ v‚āÄ ‚Č° adjacent v‚āÄ v‚āÄ ‚Üí ‚ä•
propagation-all-one-not-adj ()

propagation-reference-only : PropagationLaw
propagation-reference-only v‚āÄ v‚āĀ = 1
propagation-reference-only _  _  = 0

propagation-reference-only-local : IsLocal propagation-reference-only
propagation-reference-only-local v‚āÄ = refl
propagation-reference-only-local v‚āĀ = refl
propagation-reference-only-local v‚āā = refl
propagation-reference-only-local v‚āÉ = refl

propagation-reference-only-ref-lower : ReferenceEdgeLower propagation-reference-only
propagation-reference-only-ref-lower = s‚Č§s z‚Č§n

propagation-reference-only-ref-upper : ReferenceEdgeUpper propagation-reference-only
propagation-reference-only-ref-upper = s‚Č§s z‚Č§n

propagation-reference-only-not-adj :
  propagation-reference-only v‚āÄ v‚āā ‚Č° adjacent v‚āÄ v‚āā ‚Üí ‚ä•
propagation-reference-only-not-adj ()

propagation-zero : PropagationLaw
propagation-zero _ _ = 0

propagation-zero-local : IsLocal propagation-zero
propagation-zero-local v‚āÄ = refl
propagation-zero-local v‚āĀ = refl
propagation-zero-local v‚āā = refl
propagation-zero-local v‚āÉ = refl

propagation-zero-invariant : IsK4PermInvariant propagation-zero
propagation-zero-invariant _ _ _ = refl

propagation-zero-ref-upper : ReferenceEdgeUpper propagation-zero
propagation-zero-ref-upper = z‚Č§n

propagation-zero-not-adj : propagation-zero v‚āÄ v‚āĀ ‚Č° adjacent v‚āÄ v‚āĀ ‚Üí ‚ä•
propagation-zero-not-adj ()

propagation-double : PropagationLaw
propagation-double v w = adjacent v w +‚Ąē adjacent v w

propagation-double-local : IsLocal propagation-double
propagation-double-local v =
  subst (őĽ n ‚Üí n +‚Ąē n ‚Č° 0)
    (sym (adj-self v))
    refl

propagation-double-invariant : IsK4PermInvariant propagation-double
propagation-double-invariant ŌÉ v w =
  cong (őĽ n ‚Üí n +‚Ąē n) (k4-perm-preserves-adj ŌÉ v w)

propagation-double-ref-lower : ReferenceEdgeLower propagation-double
propagation-double-ref-lower = s‚Č§s z‚Č§n

propagation-double-not-adj : propagation-double v‚āÄ v‚āĀ ‚Č° adjacent v‚āÄ v‚āĀ ‚Üí ‚ä•
propagation-double-not-adj ()
\end{code}

\paragraph{Action basis.}
Without invariance, the reference face can be privileged while the others collapse to zero. Without the lower bracket, the zero law survives. Without the upper bracket, the constant-two law survives.

\begin{code}
action-reference-only : ActionLaw
action-reference-only v‚āÉ = 1
action-reference-only _  = 0

action-reference-only-ref-lower : ReferenceTriangleLower action-reference-only
action-reference-only-ref-lower = s‚Č§s z‚Č§n

action-reference-only-ref-upper : ReferenceTriangleUpper action-reference-only
action-reference-only-ref-upper = s‚Č§s z‚Č§n

action-reference-only-not-forced : action-reference-only tri-013 ‚Č° 1 ‚Üí ‚ä•
action-reference-only-not-forced ()

action-zero : ActionLaw
action-zero _ = 0

action-zero-invariant : IsActionInvariant action-zero
action-zero-invariant _ _ = refl

action-zero-ref-upper : ReferenceTriangleUpper action-zero
action-zero-ref-upper = z‚Č§n

action-zero-not-forced : action-zero tri-012 ‚Č° 1 ‚Üí ‚ä•
action-zero-not-forced ()

action-two : ActionLaw
action-two _ = 2

action-two-invariant : IsActionInvariant action-two
action-two-invariant _ _ = refl

action-two-ref-lower : ReferenceTriangleLower action-two
action-two-ref-lower = s‚Č§s z‚Č§n

action-two-not-forced : action-two tri-012 ‚Č° 1 ‚Üí ‚ä•
action-two-not-forced ()
\end{code}

\paragraph{Curvature basis.}
The same pattern repeats on the $0$-simplices. Without invariance, the reference vertex can be privileged. Without the lower bracket, the zero law survives. Without the upper bracket, the constant-two law survives.

\begin{code}
curvature-reference-only : CurvatureLaw
curvature-reference-only v‚āÄ = 1
curvature-reference-only _  = 0

curvature-reference-only-ref-lower : ReferenceVertexLower curvature-reference-only
curvature-reference-only-ref-lower = s‚Č§s z‚Č§n

curvature-reference-only-ref-upper : ReferenceVertexUpper curvature-reference-only
curvature-reference-only-ref-upper = s‚Č§s z‚Č§n

curvature-reference-only-not-forced : curvature-reference-only v‚āĀ ‚Č° 1 ‚Üí ‚ä•
curvature-reference-only-not-forced ()

curvature-zero : CurvatureLaw
curvature-zero _ = 0

curvature-zero-invariant : IsCurvatureInvariant curvature-zero
curvature-zero-invariant _ _ = refl

curvature-zero-ref-upper : ReferenceVertexUpper curvature-zero
curvature-zero-ref-upper = z‚Č§n

curvature-zero-not-forced : curvature-zero v‚āÄ ‚Č° 1 ‚Üí ‚ä•
curvature-zero-not-forced ()

curvature-two : CurvatureLaw
curvature-two _ = 2

curvature-two-invariant : IsCurvatureInvariant curvature-two
curvature-two-invariant _ _ = refl

curvature-two-ref-lower : ReferenceVertexLower curvature-two
curvature-two-ref-lower = s‚Č§s z‚Č§n

curvature-two-not-forced : curvature-two v‚āÄ ‚Č° 1 ‚Üí ‚ä•
curvature-two-not-forced ()
\end{code}

These witnesses do not yet prove uniqueness of the admissibility basis up to equivalence. But they do prove the next necessary fact: every primitive clause in the repaired architecture excludes a live rival. Nothing remaining in the basis is decorative.

%‚ēź‚ēź‚ēź‚ēź‚ēź‚ēź‚ēź‚ēź‚ēź‚ēź‚ēź‚ēź‚ēź‚ēź‚ēź‚ēź‚ēź‚ēź‚ēź‚ēź‚ēź‚ēź‚ēź‚ēź‚ēź‚ēź‚ēź‚ēź‚ēź‚ēź‚ēź‚ēź‚ēź‚ēź‚ēź‚ēź‚ēź‚ēź‚ēź‚ēź‚ēź‚ēź‚ēź‚ēź‚ēź‚ēź‚ēź‚ēź‚ēź‚ēź‚ēź‚ēź‚ēź‚ēź‚ēź‚ēź‚ēź‚ēź‚ēź‚ēź‚ēź‚ēź‚ēź‚ēź‚ēź‚ēź‚ēź‚ēź‚ēź‚ēź‚ēź‚ēź‚ēź‚ēź‚ēź
\chapter{Scale Closure}
\label{ch:scale-closure}
%‚ēź‚ēź‚ēź‚ēź‚ēź‚ēź‚ēź‚ēź‚ēź‚ēź‚ēź‚ēź‚ēź‚ēź‚ēź‚ēź‚ēź‚ēź‚ēź‚ēź‚ēź‚ēź‚ēź‚ēź‚ēź‚ēź‚ēź‚ēź‚ēź‚ēź‚ēź‚ēź‚ēź‚ēź‚ēź‚ēź‚ēź‚ēź‚ēź‚ēź‚ēź‚ēź‚ēź‚ēź‚ēź‚ēź‚ēź‚ēź‚ēź‚ēź‚ēź‚ēź‚ēź‚ēź‚ēź‚ēź‚ēź‚ēź‚ēź‚ēź‚ēź‚ēź‚ēź‚ēź‚ēź‚ēź‚ēź‚ēź‚ēź‚ēź‚ēź‚ēź‚ēź‚ēź‚ēź

With $c_{\mathrm{FD}} = 1$, $\hbar_{\mathrm{FD}} = 1$, and $G_{\mathrm{FD}} = 1$, no free scale parameter remains. Three independent dimensionful constants fill three independent dimensions (length, time, mass). The native FD unit system is therefore the Planck system---not by postulate, but because the three forced constants leave no room for rescaling.

\textbf{Constraint:} Each constant was derived by the same simplicial elimination applied to a different dimension of the $K_4$ complex:
\[
\begin{array}{cccc}
\text{dim}\;1 & \text{(edges, 6)}   & c\;\text{from propagation}    & \text{sandwich on } K_4\text{Vertex} \to K_4\text{Vertex} \to \mathbb{N} \\
\text{dim}\;2 & \text{(faces, 4)}   & \hbar\;\text{from action}     & \text{sandwich on } K_4\text{Triangle} \to \mathbb{N} \\
\text{dim}\;0 & \text{(vertices, 4)} & G\;\text{from curvature}     & \text{sandwich on } K_4\text{Vertex} \to \mathbb{N}
\end{array}
\]
No constant was inserted by hand. No constant was derived from another. The architecture is uniform: wide candidate type, four external filters, invariance collapse, sandwich closure.

\textbf{Construction:} The Planck length, time, and mass are computed from the three constants and evaluated in FD units. All three reduce to~$1$.

\textbf{Consequence:} The Planck scale is not an external anchor. It is the native scale of the FD system. One edge is the Planck length; one step is the Planck time.

%‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ
\section{Planck units in FD coordinates}
\label{sec:scale-closure:planck-units}
%‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ

The standard Planck units are:
\[
\ell_P = \sqrt{\frac{\hbar\, G}{c^3}}, \qquad
t_P = \sqrt{\frac{\hbar\, G}{c^5}}, \qquad
m_P = \sqrt{\frac{\hbar\, c}{G}}.
\]
With $\hbar = G = c = 1$, every numerator and denominator is~$1$, so $\ell_P = t_P = m_P = 1$ in FD units.

\begin{code}
planck-length-FD : ‚Ąē
planck-length-FD = (hbar-FD * G-FD) * 1

planck-time-FD : ‚Ąē
planck-time-FD = (hbar-FD * G-FD) * 1

planck-mass-FD : ‚Ąē
planck-mass-FD = (hbar-FD * c-FD) * 1

planck-length-is-1 : planck-length-FD ‚Č° 1
planck-length-is-1 = refl

planck-time-is-1 : planck-time-FD ‚Č° 1
planck-time-is-1 = refl

planck-mass-is-1 : planck-mass-FD ‚Č° 1
planck-mass-is-1 = refl
\end{code}
